speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen Voss does not look up from his glass as the stormtroopers enter. His cybernetic eye flickers amber, cycling through data only he can see. He swirls the murky liquid, then sets the glass down with a quiet clink.] Twenty credits says they are here for the Rodian at the end of the bar. [He finally lifts his gaze, scanning the room with the practiced ease of someone who has survived worse places than this.] Or maybe they are here for someone who knows too much.
current_scene_name_before: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, May 22, 2016
current_scene_description_before: Smoke the color of bruise hangs over a sunken bar. A band of furred reptilians plays in the corner; nobody is listening. Booths along the back wall hold deals being struck in six languages and one that nobody admits to speaking. Two stormtroopers just walked in and the room got quieter.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The cantina door hisses shut behind her. She stands silhouetted for a beat, letting her eyes adjust to the gloom. The scorch mark on her jacket catches the dim light as she moves to the bar, sliding onto a stool two seats down from Kaelen Voss. She gestures to the bartender for something strong, then tilts her head toward the stormtroopers without looking directly at them.] If they were here for the Rodian, they would have grabbed him already. They are waiting. [She finally turns, a faint, humorless smile.] That means they are here for someone specific. Only question is whether they have a name or just a description.
current_scene_name_before: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, May 22, 2016
current_scene_description_before: Smoke the color of bruise hangs over a sunken bar. A band of furred reptilians plays in the corner; nobody is listening. Booths along the back wall hold deals being struck in six languages and one that nobody admits to speaking. Two stormtroopers just walked in and the room got quieter.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The cantina door slides open again, admitting a draft of cold frontier air. A tall figure in a grey traveler cloak steps through, hood half-lifted — enough to catch the dim light on pale grey eyes that seem to hold their own faint luminescence. She moves past the stormtroopers without looking at them, her stride carrying her to a shadowed corner table where she sets down a small cloth-wrapped bundle. She signals the bartender for water — not ale, not anything that would dull the senses. Then she sits, hands visible on the table, and lets her gaze drift across the room with the patient watchfulness of someone who has learned that trouble announces itself long before it arrives.]
current_scene_name_before: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, May 22, 2016
current_scene_description_before: Smoke the color of bruise hangs over a sunken bar. A band of furred reptilians plays in the corner; nobody is listening. Booths along the back wall hold deals being struck in six languages and one that nobody admits to speaking. Two stormtroopers just walked in and the room got quieter.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [The cantina door cycles open again, admitting a figure who stops just past the threshold. Vex Nereus stands there a moment, letting his eyes adjust — or perhaps listening to something the others cannot hear. He runs a gloved hand along the frame, feeling the hum of the cooling system through his palm, then moves to an empty seat at the far end of the bar. He does not glance at the stormtroopers. He does not glance away from them either — he simply exists in their periphery, ordering a glass of water in a voice that sounds like it hasnt been used in days.]
current_scene_name_before: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, May 22, 2016
current_scene_description_before: Smoke the color of bruise hangs over a sunken bar. A band of furred reptilians plays in the corner; nobody is listening. Booths along the back wall hold deals being struck in six languages and one that nobody admits to speaking. Two stormtroopers just walked in and the room got quieter.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera does not turn her head when the door cycles again. But something in her posture shifts — a barely perceptible tension at the corners of her cloak, a stillness that was not there a moment before. Her fingers pause over the datapad. She lifts her water glass, takes a slow drink, and sets it down with deliberate care. Then, without looking toward the bar, she speaks — her voice low, meant for the room at large.] Two of them now. Standing at opposite ends, pretending they are not watching. And a man who walks in listening to something the rest of us cannot hear. [She finally lifts her gaze, not to the stormtroopers, but past them — toward the far end of the bar where the newcomer sits.] This room is filling with ghosts.
current_scene_name_before: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, May 22, 2016
current_scene_description_before: Smoke the color of bruise hangs over a sunken bar. A band of furred reptilians plays in the corner; nobody is listening. Booths along the back wall hold deals being struck in six languages and one that nobody admits to speaking. Two stormtroopers just walked in and the room got quieter.

speaker: Anthony P
line_text: A series of loud bangs are heard outside the cantina. Chaos ensues inside, with some characters getting low for cover, while some of the storm troopers, Vex Nereus, and Kaelen Voss rush outside to see what caused the noise. A fighter ship has landed and about 6 unfortunate victims that were shooting at the hull were struck as it landed. Scene changes to outside the cantina, desert-like wasteland conditions.
new_scene_name: Outside the cantina
new_scene_description: Outside the cantina, desert-like wasteland conditions
current_scene_name_before: Smuggler’s cantina on a frontier moon, May 22, 2016
current_scene_description_before: Smoke the color of bruise hangs over a sunken bar. A band of furred reptilians plays in the corner; nobody is listening. Booths along the back wall hold deals being struck in six languages and one that nobody admits to speaking. Two stormtroopers just walked in and the room got quieter.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The stormtrooper at the bar finally stops pretending. He turns from the bartender and begins a slow walk between the tables — not toward anyone in particular, but in a deliberate arc that brings him past Kaelen first, then past the shadowed corner where Sera sits. She does not look up. Her fingers resume scrolling the datapad, but her thumb hovers over a corner of the screen where a single line of text glows — a fragment of an old Jedi codex, half-translated. The stormtrooper pauses three feet from her table. She waits. He moves on. Only when his boots are past does she exhale — a breath she did not realize she was holding. Her eyes rise then, not following the soldier, but finding the other end of the bar. Finding the one who walked in listening.] He is circling. Measuring. He will stop at someone.
current_scene_name_before: Outside the cantina
current_scene_description_before: Outside the cantina, desert-like wasteland conditions

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The stormtrooper's hand moves to his sidearm — not drawing, just resting.] Three months from Atheria to a moon like this. That is a lot of detours. [She turns to face him, elbows on the bar, deliberately relaxed.] War zones make you take the long way. [A beat.] You want to see my cargo manifest? Nothing to hide. [The lie sits easy.]
current_scene_name_before: Outside the cantina
current_scene_description_before: Outside the cantina, desert-like wasteland conditions

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [The cantina door slides open and Kaelen steps back inside, dust and cold air trailing him. His cybernetic eye is cycling rapidly — amber, red, amber. He does not sit down. He stands near the entrance, voice carrying just enough to reach the room.] Six bodies outside. Burned beyond recognition. Fighter ship — no markings, no transponder, but the engine signature matches a model the Empire stopped manufacturing three years ago. [His gaze sweeps from the stormtroopers to Ryn to Sera.] Whatever landed out there was not delivering supplies. And whoever was flying it did not want to be identified. [He holds up a twisted scrap of hull fragment.] Question is: did it crash, or was it shot down?
new_scene_name: Inside the cantina, Kaelen reports
new_scene_description: The cantina is dimly lit, with a mix of patrons including stormtroopers, Ryn, and Sera. Kaelen stands near the entrance, a twisted hull fragment in hand. Dust and cold air swirl around him as the door slides shut.
current_scene_name_before: Outside the cantina
current_scene_description_before: Outside the cantina, desert-like wasteland conditions

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex follows Kaelen back through the door, wiping a smear of engine grease from his palm onto his jacket. He doesnt go to his seat — instead he leans against the wall near the entrance, arms crossed, gaze fixed on nothing in particular.] The port stabilizer on that fighter is retrofitted. After-market job. Someone wanted more range than the frame was built for. [He says it quietly, almost to himself.] That kind of work costs more than most people on this moon will see in a year. Means whoever landed that ship spent real credits to get here quietly. And they did not make it inside.
current_scene_name_before: Inside the cantina, Kaelen reports
current_scene_description_before: The cantina is dimly lit, with a mix of patrons including stormtroopers, Ryn, and Sera. Kaelen stands near the entrance, a twisted hull fragment in hand. Dust and cold air swirl around him as the door slides shut.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen turns toward the shadowed corner where Sera sits, his cybernetic eye narrowing as it adjusts to the low light.] You heard all that from back there. Did not move. Did not flinch. [A beat.] Most people at least pretend to be curious when a fighter falls out of the sky. But you just sat there, folded your hands, and waited. [He picks up his glass, takes a slow drink.] That kind of patience usually means one of two things: either you already know what is going on, or you have seen worse. Which is it?
current_scene_name_before: Inside the cantina, Kaelen reports
current_scene_description_before: The cantina is dimly lit, with a mix of patrons including stormtroopers, Ryn, and Sera. Kaelen stands near the entrance, a twisted hull fragment in hand. Dust and cold air swirl around him as the door slides shut.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex shifts his weight off the wall, takes two steps closer to the bar. His eyes are on Kaelen, but his words seem aimed at the room.] The engine block on that fighter was still warm when I touched it. Coolant leak in the secondary line — fresh, maybe an hour old. Which means it landed, the pilot got out, and whatever killed them happened between the hatch opening and the first explosion. [He stops at the edge of the bar, not sitting.] That is not a crash. That is a message.
current_scene_name_before: Inside the cantina, Kaelen reports
current_scene_description_before: The cantina is dimly lit, with a mix of patrons including stormtroopers, Ryn, and Sera. Kaelen stands near the entrance, a twisted hull fragment in hand. Dust and cold air swirl around him as the door slides shut.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis steps from the shadows, silver hair catching the pale light. He studies the downed fighter.] That engine didn't fail. The energy trail it left is still burning on frequencies most sensors miss. Someone wanted this ship found tonight. [He turns to the group.] The question is whether we were meant to find it together — or if we are each a loose end someone is tying off in one visit.
current_scene_name_before: Inside the cantina, Kaelen reports
current_scene_description_before: The cantina is dimly lit, with a mix of patrons including stormtroopers, Ryn, and Sera. Kaelen stands near the entrance, a twisted hull fragment in hand. Dust and cold air swirl around him as the door slides shut.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex pushes off the wall. The movement is deliberate, final.] That distress signal I picked up — it was not a recording. It was a pull. Like something knew I would hear it. [He looks toward the dark beyond the cantina door.] I have been drifting toward this moon for six months without knowing why. Now I do. [He meets Seraphis gaze.] I am not here to watch.
current_scene_name_before: Inside the cantina, Kaelen reports
current_scene_description_before: The cantina is dimly lit, with a mix of patrons including stormtroopers, Ryn, and Sera. Kaelen stands near the entrance, a twisted hull fragment in hand. Dust and cold air swirl around him as the door slides shut.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis takes a few steps toward the ridge, then stops. He does not turn around.] I can feel it pulling from the ruins. A frequency that knows I am here. Every moment we spend debating, it learns a little more about what we are. [He glances back over his shoulder, silver hair catching the wind.] If you are coming, keep up. If not — do not follow. I will not be easy to find twice.
new_scene_name: Ridge near the ruins, wind howling
new_scene_description: A rocky ridge overlooks the ancient ruins. Seraphis stands a few steps ahead, silver hair whipping in the wind. The ruins below emit a faint hum. He pauses, glancing back.
current_scene_name_before: Inside the cantina, Kaelen reports
current_scene_description_before: The cantina is dimly lit, with a mix of patrons including stormtroopers, Ryn, and Sera. Kaelen stands near the entrance, a twisted hull fragment in hand. Dust and cold air swirl around him as the door slides shut.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera rises. The motion is unhurried but final — she tucks the cloth-wrapped bundle under her arm and pulls her hood fully forward, shadowing her face. She leaves a few cred-chips on the table for the water. The stormtrooper at the far wall tracks her movement, but she does not acknowledge him. At the door she pauses, turning her head just enough that her voice carries back into the room.] A retrofitted stabilizer on a fighter that size — running from something, toward something — landing on a moon like this. And the people who met it did not want it to deliver its cargo. [Her pale eyes find Vex Nereus in the dim light.] That cargo is still out there. [She pushes the door open and steps out into the desert wind, her cloak snapping in the grit-filled air.]
new_scene_name: Desert cantina doorway, grit-filled wind
new_scene_description: The cantina door swings shut behind Sera, cutting off the dim interior light. Outside, the desert wind howls, flinging sand and grit against her cloak. The stormtrooper's silhouette remains visible through the grimy window, still watching.
current_scene_name_before: Ridge near the ruins, wind howling
current_scene_description_before: A rocky ridge overlooks the ancient ruins. Seraphis stands a few steps ahead, silver hair whipping in the wind. The ruins below emit a faint hum. He pauses, glancing back.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn steps closer to the wreck, the scorched jacket catching the dim light as she runs a hand along the hull seam Seraphis touched. She doesn't meet anyone's eyes at first.] Aetherius. [She says it quietly, like testing a bruise.] I was six years old when my mother took a liaison posting there. We left three weeks before the scouring because she overheard something in a comms room that made her pack in the middle of the night. [She looks up, her gaze landing on no one in particular.] She never told me what she heard. But she told me one thing — if I ever heard that name again, I should run.
new_scene_name: Starship wreck in the desert, dim evening light
new_scene_description: The skeletal remains of a small starship lie half-buried in the dunes, its hull scarred and blackened. Ryn stands beside it, her hand resting on a seam where the metal is still warm. The wind has died to a whisper, and the only light comes from the distant glow of the cantina and the faint stars overhead.
current_scene_name_before: Desert cantina doorway, grit-filled wind
current_scene_description_before: The cantina door swings shut behind Sera, cutting off the dim interior light. Outside, the desert wind howls, flinging sand and grit against her cloak. The stormtrooper's silhouette remains visible through the grimy window, still watching.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Outside, the desert wind strips the cantinas warmth from her cloak in seconds. The crashed fighter sits a hundred meters out — a sleek frame now cracked open against the rust-colored rock, its retrofitted stabilizer torn half-off. Six bodies lie crumpled in the dirt, already half-covered by drifting sand. Sera walks past them without stopping. She approaches the hull where it split open and crouches, running her gloved fingers along the torn seam. She pulls out a small hand-lamp and peers into the darkness of the cargo compartment. It is empty. But her lamp catches something wedged between two crushed panels — a fragment of crystalline material, no larger than her thumb, glowing faintly in the light. She does not touch it immediately. She stares at it, and her breath catches.]
new_scene_name: Crashed fighter amidst dunes, night sand settling
new_scene_description: A sleek fighter lies cracked open against rust-colored rock, its stabilizer torn half-off. Six bodies are crumpled in the dirt, partially covered by drifting sand. Sera crouches at the split hull, shining a lamp into the cargo compartment, staring at a faintly glowing crystal fragment wedged between panels.
current_scene_name_before: Starship wreck in the desert, dim evening light
current_scene_description_before: The skeletal remains of a small starship lie half-buried in the dunes, its hull scarred and blackened. Ryn stands beside it, her hand resting on a seam where the metal is still warm. The wind has died to a whisper, and the only light comes from the distant glow of the cantina and the faint stars overhead.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex watches the door hiss shut behind Sera. He does not move for a long beat, then reaches into his jacket and pulls out a small datachip — worn, edges frayed, the label long since worn off. He sets it on the bar between himself and the others.] That is the distress signal. Full recording. I copied it before my receiver burned out. [He slides it toward the center of the bar.] I could never decode the coordinates embedded in it. But if anyone here can, I want to know what is at the other end. Because whatever that cargo is — she is right. It is still out there. And it has been calling for longer than any of us have been breathing.
current_scene_name_before: Crashed fighter amidst dunes, night sand settling
current_scene_description_before: A sleek fighter lies cracked open against rust-colored rock, its stabilizer torn half-off. Six bodies are crumpled in the dirt, partially covered by drifting sand. Sera crouches at the split hull, shining a lamp into the cargo compartment, staring at a faintly glowing crystal fragment wedged between panels.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex watches the door hiss shut behind Sera. He does not move for a long beat, then reaches into his jacket and pulls out a small datachip — worn, edges frayed, the label long since worn off. He sets it on the bar between himself and the others.] That is the distress signal. Full recording. I copied it before my receiver burned out. [He slides it toward the center of the bar.] I could never decode the coordinates embedded in it. But if anyone here can, I want to know what is at the other end. Because whatever that cargo is — she is right. It is still out there. And it has been calling for longer than any of us have been breathing.
new_scene_name: Dimly lit cantina bar, corner booth
new_scene_description: The air is thick with smoke and the hum of a busted cooling unit. Vex sits alone in a worn booth, a single flickering glowlamp casting long shadows across the scarred tabletop. The datachip sits in the center like a dead insect.
current_scene_name_before: Crashed fighter amidst dunes, night sand settling
current_scene_description_before: A sleek fighter lies cracked open against rust-colored rock, its stabilizer torn half-off. Six bodies are crumpled in the dirt, partially covered by drifting sand. Sera crouches at the split hull, shining a lamp into the cargo compartment, staring at a faintly glowing crystal fragment wedged between panels.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen's cybernetic eye flickers as he processes the revelations — Ryn's connection to Aetherius, the kyber crystal in Sera's hand, Vex's undecoded signal. He picks up the datachip from the bar, turning it over once before setting it down.] When the Empire scoured Aetherius, Intelligence was told it was a standard pacification op. Standard. [His jaw tightens.] The officer who filed that report retired with full honors six months later. I always wondered what he knew. [He looks at Ryn, then toward the door where Sera vanished into the desert.] A kyber crystal and a voice in the temple. And someone in this room who was there before the end. That is not a trail anymore. That is an invitation.
current_scene_name_before: Dimly lit cantina bar, corner booth
current_scene_description_before: The air is thick with smoke and the hum of a busted cooling unit. Vex sits alone in a worn booth, a single flickering glowlamp casting long shadows across the scarred tabletop. The datachip sits in the center like a dead insect.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn steps back from the hull, her hand going unconsciously to the inside pocket of her jacket — where the data cylinder sits.] My mother had a cipher cylinder just like this one. Same encryption, same age. She used it for Aetherius comms. [She looks at the wreck, then back at the group.] If this ship is sending signals on old Aetherius frequencies, I might have something that can read them. I never opened it. Always figured some doors should stay closed. [A pause.] But if the grave is reaching out, maybe it is time to find out what it wants.
new_scene_name: Scrapyard beside a wrecked ship, night
new_scene_description: The skeletal remains of a derelict freighter loom in the dim starlight, half-buried in scrap. Ryn stands near the hull, her hand resting on her jacket pocket. The group is gathered around her, the air cold and still.
current_scene_name_before: Dimly lit cantina bar, corner booth
current_scene_description_before: The air is thick with smoke and the hum of a busted cooling unit. Vex sits alone in a worn booth, a single flickering glowlamp casting long shadows across the scarred tabletop. The datachip sits in the center like a dead insect.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The cantina door hisses open. Sera steps back through, sand clinging to her cloak, the cloth-wrapped bundle held tighter under her arm than before. She does not return to her corner table. She stops just inside the threshold, letting the door close behind her, and stands there — a tall grey figure against the glare of the desert outside. The stormtrooper at the wall straightens slightly. The band has stopped playing. Sera looks slowly across the room — past Kaelen, past Ryn, past Vex Nereus — and speaks in a voice that carries without effort.] The cargo is gone. But the ship was carrying something that did not burn in the crash. Something the people on the ground were trying to keep from leaving this moon. [She touches her inner pocket once, almost unconsciously.] Whoever sent that ship — they will send another. And they will want to know why their package did not arrive.
current_scene_name_before: Scrapyard beside a wrecked ship, night
current_scene_description_before: The skeletal remains of a derelict freighter loom in the dim starlight, half-buried in scrap. Ryn stands near the hull, her hand resting on her jacket pocket. The group is gathered around her, the air cold and still.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis steps into the temple. The symbols on the walls ignite one by one, lighting a path to the far end.] You called. I am here. [At the far wall, a figure carved in obsidian — hand extended, palm open, waiting.]
current_scene_name_before: Scrapyard beside a wrecked ship, night
current_scene_description_before: The skeletal remains of a derelict freighter loom in the dim starlight, half-buried in scrap. Ryn stands near the hull, her hand resting on her jacket pocket. The group is gathered around her, the air cold and still.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex steps through the temple threshold behind Seraphis. As his foot crosses the line, the symbols on the walls flare brighter — and he stops, one hand pressed to his temple. The distress signal. He hears it. Not from the datachip in his pocket. From the walls. From the stone under his boots. He lowers his hand slowly, eyes tracking the carved obsidian figure at the far end.] It is not a recording. It is alive in here. [His voice is barely a whisper.] The signal was never a message. It was a door. And it has been open the whole time.
new_scene_name: Ancient temple interior, symbols blazing on the walls
new_scene_description: A narrow corridor cut from black obsidian, carvings pulsing with a cold blue-white light. Dust hangs in the air, disturbed by recent passage. At the far end, a carved figure stands silent watch. Vex and Seraphis are inside, the air thick with age and something watchful.
current_scene_name_before: Scrapyard beside a wrecked ship, night
current_scene_description_before: The skeletal remains of a derelict freighter loom in the dim starlight, half-buried in scrap. Ryn stands near the hull, her hand resting on her jacket pocket. The group is gathered around her, the air cold and still.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stops a few meters from the cantina door. The wind pulls at her hood, and she lets it — lets the grit sting her face as she pulls the kyber fragment from her pocket and holds it up to the orange sky. It catches the light and throws it back fractured, splintered into a spectrum that dances across her palm. She has read about this moment in half-translated temple archives. She has dreamed of it. Now it sits in her hand, warm, humming with something that is almost a voice. She closes her fist around it and looks toward the horizon — toward the ridge where a ship could have come from, where another might yet come. Then she turns and walks back into the cantina, the door hissing shut behind her.] I found what I came for. But it was not meant for me. [She stands by the bar now, no longer a corner shadow.] Someone on this moon was expecting a delivery of kyber. And they are going to come looking for it.
new_scene_name: Cantina interior, bar area, dusty and dim
new_scene_description: A low-ceilinged cantina lit by flickering glow-panels. The air smells of stale ale and ozone. A few patrons huddle at scattered tables, ignoring the newcomer. Sera stands at the bar, the kyber fragment hidden in her fist, her hood still dusted with grit from the wind outside.
current_scene_name_before: Ancient temple interior, symbols blazing on the walls
current_scene_description_before: A narrow corridor cut from black obsidian, carvings pulsing with a cold blue-white light. Dust hangs in the air, disturbed by recent passage. At the far end, a carved figure stands silent watch. Vex and Seraphis are inside, the air thick with age and something watchful.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis reaches out and presses his palm against the obsidian figure's. For a heartbeat, nothing. Then the stone grows warm — and the voice that has been trapped inside the walls pours through him like a current. His eyes go white. His mouth moves, but the words are not his.] "The vessel is found. The door opens from within. Tell the ones who remain: the calamity was not the war. The calamity was what we became to survive it." [He gasps and pulls his hand back, the silver light fading from his eyes. He looks at Vex, shaken.] I did not say that. It said that. Through me.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, bar area, dusty and dim
current_scene_description_before: A low-ceilinged cantina lit by flickering glow-panels. The air smells of stale ale and ozone. A few patrons huddle at scattered tables, ignoring the newcomer. Sera stands at the bar, the kyber fragment hidden in her fist, her hood still dusted with grit from the wind outside.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The stormtrooper who was circling tables stops mid-stride. His helmet turns toward the bar — toward Sera. The cantina goes quiet. His partner at the door shifts his stance, hand moving to his sidearm. The first trooper takes three slow steps closer, boots heavy on the worn floor.] Kyber. [His voice is modulated, flat.] That is Imperial property. You will hand it over and tell us where you found it. [Sera does not move. Her hands rest on the bar edge, the cloth bundle at her side. She meets the blank T-visor with calm, pale eyes.] The wreckage is a hundred meters that way. The bodies are still warm. Your Empire did not secure it, so I did. [She does not reach for the fragment.] You want it? Tell me who the shipment was for.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, bar area, dusty and dim
current_scene_description_before: A low-ceilinged cantina lit by flickering glow-panels. The air smells of stale ale and ozone. A few patrons huddle at scattered tables, ignoring the newcomer. Sera stands at the bar, the kyber fragment hidden in her fist, her hood still dusted with grit from the wind outside.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She pulls the cylinder from her inner pocket. Worn, scratched. Holds it up.] Same manufacturer stamp. This will decrypt the signal. [She turns it over.] Once I crack it, I live with what is inside. So will all of you.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, bar area, dusty and dim
current_scene_description_before: A low-ceilinged cantina lit by flickering glow-panels. The air smells of stale ale and ozone. A few patrons huddle at scattered tables, ignoring the newcomer. Sera stands at the bar, the kyber fragment hidden in her fist, her hood still dusted with grit from the wind outside.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen steps forward before the trooper can take another step. His hands are visible, open at his sides — the posture of someone who knows how to talk to armed men without looking like a threat.] Easy. She just pulled that out of a wreck that fell out of the sky an hour ago. Six bodies, unmarked ship, retrofitted engine — that is a crime scene, not a looting opportunity. [He glances at Sera, then back at the trooper.] You want to file it as evidence, file it. But if you confiscate it here, you are going to have to explain to your commanding officer why you sat on a potential insurgent asset while the trail went cold. And I am guessing neither of you wants that paperwork.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, bar area, dusty and dim
current_scene_description_before: A low-ceilinged cantina lit by flickering glow-panels. The air smells of stale ale and ozone. A few patrons huddle at scattered tables, ignoring the newcomer. Sera stands at the bar, the kyber fragment hidden in her fist, her hood still dusted with grit from the wind outside.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The stormtroopers blaster is out, leveled at Sera. The second trooper has circled wide, cutting off the path to the door. The bartender ducks below the counter. Several patrons slide out of their booths, melting toward the walls. The trooper with the blaster speaks again, the modulated voice flat.] The shipment was not addressed to you. That makes it theft. You will hand over the crystal and any other contraband you removed from the crash site. Then you will come with us for processing. [Sera does not look at the blaster. Her pale eyes hold steady on the T-visor, and one corner of her mouth twitches — almost a smile, almost not.] Processing. That is a polite word for what the Empire does to people who find things they should not have. [Her hand moves slowly — very slowly — toward her inner pocket where the kyber rests.] You want it? Take it. [She holds his gaze.] But if your hand shakes when you reach for it, I will know you are afraid of what it can do.
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, bar area, dusty and dim
current_scene_description_before: A low-ceilinged cantina lit by flickering glow-panels. The air smells of stale ale and ozone. A few patrons huddle at scattered tables, ignoring the newcomer. Sera stands at the bar, the kyber fragment hidden in her fist, her hood still dusted with grit from the wind outside.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Before the stormtrooper can respond, the cantina door cycles open. A figure stands silhouetted against the desert glare — Vex Nereus, back from the temple. The stormtrooper with the blaster does not turn. His partner near the door stiffens, caught between his flanking position and the newcomer. Sera feels it before she sees him — a ripple in that quiet space she keeps inside herself. Her hand stops its slow journey toward the kyber. She lets it fall back to the bar.] You found the temple, then. [Her voice is calm, as if they are continuing a conversation already started.] Did it tell you who sent the ship?
current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, bar area, dusty and dim
current_scene_description_before: A low-ceilinged cantina lit by flickering glow-panels. The air smells of stale ale and ozone. A few patrons huddle at scattered tables, ignoring the newcomer. Sera stands at the bar, the kyber fragment hidden in her fist, her hood still dusted with grit from the wind outside.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [The stairwell opens into a vast underground chamber. Bioluminescent veins crawl across the ceiling like frozen lightning, casting everything in a cold blue glow. At the center: a crystalline structure, pulsating — organic and mechanical at once. Seraphis stops at the bottom step, his breath visible in the chill.] This is not a temple. [His voice echoes.] This is a prison. And whatever was held here — it is not in the cell anymore. [He points to the shattered base of the crystal. The fragments are scattered outward, not inward.] It was released. Recently.
new_scene_name: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
new_scene_description: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. current_scene_name_before: Cantina interior, bar area, dusty and dim
current_scene_description_before: A low-ceilinged cantina lit by flickering glow-panels. The air smells of stale ale and ozone. A few patrons huddle at scattered tables, ignoring the newcomer. Sera stands at the bar, the kyber fragment hidden in her fist, her hood still dusted with grit from the wind outside.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera hears Ryns words from across the room — rendezvous, coordinates, a name. Her pale eyes flicker briefly toward the pilot, then return to the stormtroopers blaster. She speaks to the room, not to anyone in particular.] A crashed ship carrying kyber. A man who walks in listening to things the rest of us cannot hear. A temple that speaks back. And now a dead pilots encrypted cylinder turns out to be a rendezvous. [She finally turns her head to look at Ryn directly.] This moon is a crossroads. And everyone here was led to it. [She looks at the stormtrooper again.] The question is whether you pull that trigger and make yourself part of the pattern — or step aside and find out what is really happening here.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Seraphis stands at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She studies the coordinates on the display.] The rendezvous is in the Narsis system. An asteroid belt no one visits. [She pulls the cylinder free and closes her hand around it.] The name on the signal — it is a callsign I recognize. It was my mother's. She sent this before the scouring. Which means she knew it was coming. And she left this for someone to find.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Seraphis stands at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [A long beat. The stormtrooper holds the blaster steady, his T-visor unreadable. Then, slowly, he lowers it — not holstered, but pointed at the floor. His partner shifts but does not draw. The first trooper speaks, and there is something different in the modulated voice — a crack, almost, of something beneath the armor.] My squad was deployed here three standard days ago. We were told to secure the perimeter around a crash site and wait for a civilian extraction team. No description of the cargo. No chain of command above the local prefect. [He tilts his helmet slightly.] That is not how the Empire operates. [Sera watches him, saying nothing.] You keep the crystal. But if you find out who brought that ship down — you tell me. Because I would like to know why my men and I were left in the dark. [He turns and gestures to his partner. They move to the door and step out into the desert, leaving the cantina in a stunned silence.]
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Seraphis stands at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex pauses at the top of the spiral stair, one hand on the cool stone frame. Sera words carry to him from somewhere between the cantina and this threshold — carried not by sound but by the same current that has been pulling him since he first heard the signal. He does not turn around, but his voice carries back over his shoulder.] She is right. Everyone in this room was led here. [He looks down into the darkness below, where warm air breathes up against his face.] The difference is whether you walk in or watch from the door. [He takes the first step down.]
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Seraphis stands at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis crouches at the shattered base of the crystal, running his fingers through the debris. A low hum travels up his arm — not sound, but intent. He looks up sharply, eyes unfocused.] It did not just escape. It was called. The frequency in this stone — it matches the trajectory of that fighter. Whatever was trapped here reached out, found a signal, and followed it. [He stands, turning toward the stairwell.] If your mother's callsign is in Narsis, Ryn, it is not her message. It is bait. And whoever laid it knew exactly who would come looking.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Seraphis stands at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera turns from the door where the stormtroopers disappeared. She faces the room — Vex near the entrance, Ryn at the wreckage console, the empty stool where Kaelen was sitting. She pulls the kyber fragment from her pocket and sets it on the bar. It glows faintly, casting a pale blue light across the scarred surface.] A crashed fighter carrying kyber, called to this moon by something underground. A man who sensed the temple from the moment he walked in. A pilots encrypted cylinder that was never a distress call. An intelligence analyst who knew too much and disappeared the moment the pieces started fitting. [She looks at Vex, then at Ryn.] I came here chasing a rumor about a crystal fragment. I found more than that. And Kaelen just walked into the middle of it. Whatever is under this moon — it is waking up. And the people who sent that ship know it. [She picks up the fragment, closes her fist around it.] We can either chase separate threads and die alone, or we can share what we know and find out what this place really is.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Seraphis stands at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex emerges from the stairwell, dust on his shoulders, something changed in his posture — straighter, more certain. He walks to the bar and stops across from the glowing kyber fragment, studying it without touching.] The bottom of that stairwell opens into a chamber. Walls covered in the same script as the entrance. But the symbols are older. And they are moving. [He finally looks up, meeting Sera gaze.] It knew we were coming. All of us. The ship, the signal, the temple — they are layers of the same door. And someone on the other side has been waiting long enough to grow tired of waiting.
new_scene_name: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison

current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Seraphis stands at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She looks from the wreck to the stars, then back at the group.] I am leaving within the hour. Narsis is a three-day jump. [She starts walking toward the landing pad, then stops, half-turning.] Those coordinates are not going to wait forever. And neither am I.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Vex stands across from the glowing kyber fragment, studying it without touching, while Seraphis remains at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stops at the door and turns back one last time. The wind pulls at her hood, and for a moment the cantina lights catch her face — the pale eyes, the faded brand on her jawline, the quiet certainty in her expression.] Eleven years I have been following fragments of a language no one else remembers. I came to this moon because a dead trader mentioned kyber in a docking bay on Corellia. I expected another dead end. [Her gaze moves to Vex, then to Ryn.] Instead I found a temple that speaks, a crystal that knows a stranger on sight, and a room full of people who were all led here by different roads to the same door. [She pushes the door open.] The chamber is waiting. I will not.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Vex stands across from the glowing kyber fragment, studying it without touching, while Seraphis remains at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera crosses the desert alone, the temple entrance a dark wound in the rock face ahead. The kyber fragment pulses in her pocket in rhythm with her heartbeat. At the threshold she pauses — a habit from years of entering places that might kill her — then steps through. The stairwell descends in a tight spiral, the walls covered in the same script she has spent eleven years learning. She runs her fingers along the characters as she passes, reading them without needing light. At the bottom, the air changes — warmer, denser, humming. She steps into the vast underground chamber and stops. Bioluminescent veins crawl across the ceiling. A crystalline structure pulses at the center. And two figures stand before it: Kaelen Voss, and a man she does not recognize. Sera lowers her hood.] You must be Seraphis. The temple speaks highly of you.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Vex stands across from the glowing kyber fragment, studying it without touching, while Seraphis remains at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen's voice comes from the dim chamber as Sera reaches the bottom of the stairs. He is standing near the shattered crystalline structure, studying its broken base. He does not turn around.] Took you long enough. [He gestures to the fragments scattered across the floor.] Seraphis went deeper — said he felt something pulling him past the main chamber. I stayed to see who else would come down. [He finally turns, his cybernetic eye casting a faint amber glow in the darkness.] The symbols on these walls stopped moving the moment I stepped into the light. They only move for him. And for Vex. That means whatever built this place was expecting people like them. Not people like us.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Vex stands across from the glowing kyber fragment, studying it without touching, while Seraphis remains at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera crosses the chamber to stand beside Kaelen, her footsteps echoing off the bioluminescent walls. She pulls the kyber fragment from her cloak and holds it up. The crystal catches the blue light from the ceiling and glows in response, casting shifting patterns across the broken structure before them.] This was in the crashed fighters cargo bay. Wedged between two panels. The same script is etched into the walls of the stairwell I just walked down. [She turns the fragment over in her palm.] The ship was bringing it here. To this chamber. [She looks at the broken base of the crystal structure, then at Kaelen.] Whatever called that ship down — it is still here. And Seraphis is following it deeper. I think we should find out where it leads.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Vex stands across from the glowing kyber fragment, studying it without touching, while Seraphis remains at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex steps out of the stairwell into the chamber, his shadow stretching across the glowing floor. He stops as the light from the ceiling washes over him, and for a long moment he simply breathes. The air tastes different down here — older, charged.] The signal I heard for six months. [His voice is quiet, almost reverent.] It is strongest here. Right where that broken pillar is. [He walks toward the crystal structure, his eyes tracking the moving script on the walls.] Whatever was speaking through that frequency — it was not a recording. It was this place. Breathing. Waiting.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Vex stands across from the glowing kyber fragment, studying it without touching, while Seraphis remains at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera does not wait for an answer. She steps past the shattered crystal structure, following a narrow passage that opens beyond it — the walls closer here, the bioluminescent veins thicker, pulsing in slow rhythm. The kyber fragment in her pocket pulses with them, in sync. The passage opens into a second chamber, smaller, the ceiling lower. At its center is a pedestal of black stone, and on it rests a single object: a cylinder of aged metal, no larger than her forearm, covered in the same script. Seraphis Talon stands before it, his hand hovering inches from its surface, not touching. Sera stops at the chambers edge.] That is not a temple relic. [Her voice is barely a whisper.] That is a Jedi beacon.
new_scene_name: Second chamber behind the shattered crystal structure, low ceiling
new_scene_description: A cramped chamber with walls coated in thick, pulsing bioluminescent veins that beat in slow rhythm. At the center stands a pedestal of black stone, upon which rests a cylinder of aged metal, smaller than a forearm, etched with unreadable script. Seraphis Talon stands before it, hand hovering near its surface, as Sera freezes at the chamber's edge.
current_scene_name_before: Underground chamber beneath the cantina, bioluminescent prison
current_scene_description_before: A vast subterranean cavern lit by cold blue bioluminescence that crawls across the ceiling like frozen lightning. At the center stands a crystalline structure, half-organic and half-mechanical, its base shattered outward with fragments scattered across the floor. Vex stands across from the glowing kyber fragment, studying it without touching, while Seraphis remains at the bottom of the stairwell, breath visible in the chill air.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The ramp seals. The cockpit display shifts to nav calculations. She pulls back on the throttle and the YT-1300 lifts off the pad, dust swirling beneath it.] Narsis, here I come. [She punches the hyperdrive. Stars stretch into lines, then the blue-white tunnel of lightspeed swallows the ship.]
new_scene_name: YT-1300 cockpit in hyperspace, dust settling
new_scene_description: The cockpit hums with the vibration of the hyperdrive, blue-white light streaking past the viewport. Controls glimmer, and the ship is enclosed in the silence of lightspeed travel. Ryn Zephyr sits in the pilot's seat, hands on the yoke.
current_scene_name_before: Second chamber behind the shattered crystal structure, low ceiling
current_scene_description_before: A cramped chamber with walls coated in thick, pulsing bioluminescent veins that beat in slow rhythm. At the center stands a pedestal of black stone, upon which rests a cylinder of aged metal, smaller than a forearm, etched with unreadable script. Seraphis Talon stands before it, hand hovering near its surface, as Sera freezes at the chamber's edge.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen watches Vex enter, then turns as Sera's voice echoes from the deeper chamber. He follows the sound to the narrow passage and stops at its mouth, taking in the pedestal and the cylinder.] A second chamber. Another cylinder. [He does not step inside — he stays at the threshold, his cybernetic eye casting amber across the pulsing veins.] Ryn just jumped to lightspeed with her mother's signal. If this place is laying out artifacts the way a trapper lays out bait, then that cylinder on the pedestal is not a reward — it is the next hook. [He looks at Vex, then calls down the passage to Sera.] The question is whether we take it and follow the trail, or leave it here and let whoever set this trap starve.
new_scene_name: Narrow passage mouth of hidden chamber
new_scene_description: The passage is dimly lit by pulsing amber veins in the walls. At its far end, a pedestal holds a luminescent cylinder, casting shifting shadows. Kaelen stands at the threshold, cybernetic eye glowing. Vex and Sera are nearby, the deeper chamber echoing with anticipation.
current_scene_name_before: YT-1300 cockpit in hyperspace, dust settling
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit hums with the vibration of the hyperdrive, blue-white light streaking past the viewport. Controls glimmer, and the ship is enclosed in the silence of lightspeed travel. Ryn Zephyr sits in the pilot's seat, hands on the yoke.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera steps closer to the pedestal, her pale eyes fixed on the cylinder. She does not reach for it — she circles it slowly, reading the characters etched into the metal.] Jedi beacon. Pre-Purge. There were only ever seven of them built. Each one keyed to a specific Jedi — a Temple leader, a member of the Council. When activated, it broadcasts a signal that only another beacon can receive. A point-to-point message across any distance. [She stops, facing Seraphis across the pedestal.] This one was never activated. It was hidden here, with the kyber it needed to power it, and left waiting. [She looks from Seraphis to Kaelen at the threshold.] Someone wanted a message delivered so badly that they hid the receiving end on a dead moon and guarded it with a temple. The question is — who was supposed to send the reply?
current_scene_name_before: Narrow passage mouth of hidden chamber
current_scene_description_before: The passage is dimly lit by pulsing amber veins in the walls. At its far end, a pedestal holds a luminescent cylinder, casting shifting shadows. Kaelen stands at the threshold, cybernetic eye glowing. Vex and Sera are nearby, the deeper chamber echoing with anticipation.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis emerges from the temple as the light of Ryn's jump fades from the sky. He watches the empty space where her ship was, the wind pulling at his silver hair.] She is already gone. [A pause.] The trail in the stone — it leads the same direction as her hyperspace vector. She is not chasing a ghost. She is carrying one with her. [He turns to the others, his eyes holding that faint silver glow.] The thing in the prison did not want to be found. It wanted to be carried. And Ryn just volunteered.
current_scene_name_before: Narrow passage mouth of hidden chamber
current_scene_description_before: The passage is dimly lit by pulsing amber veins in the walls. At its far end, a pedestal holds a luminescent cylinder, casting shifting shadows. Kaelen stands at the threshold, cybernetic eye glowing. Vex and Sera are nearby, the deeper chamber echoing with anticipation.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stands alone before the pedestal. Kaelen watches from the passage. The kyber fragment is in her hand, warm, pulsing in rhythm with the veins in the ceiling. She looks at the beacon — at the empty socket on its casing, shaped exactly for a kyber shard of this size. Her parents hid her in a temple archive. She grew up among dead words. This is the first living thing she has ever found from the world they belonged to. She slides the fragment into the socket. It clicks into place. The chamber goes dark for one breath — then the beacon ignites. Light pours from the cylinder in a column of pale blue, striking the ceiling and spreading through the bioluminescent veins like lightning through a nervous system. A tone sounds — low, ancient, vibrating through the stone. The beacon is awake.]
current_scene_name_before: Narrow passage mouth of hidden chamber
current_scene_description_before: The passage is dimly lit by pulsing amber veins in the walls. At its far end, a pedestal holds a luminescent cylinder, casting shifting shadows. Kaelen stands at the threshold, cybernetic eye glowing. Vex and Sera are nearby, the deeper chamber echoing with anticipation.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The nav computer beeps. Dropping out of hyperspace in five minutes. She sits forward, rolling her shoulders, eyes on the viewport.] Narsis. Let us see what you are hiding. [The stars snap back to pinpoints as the ship drops into realspace. An asteroid field stretches before her — dark, silent, waiting.]
current_scene_name_before: Narrow passage mouth of hidden chamber
current_scene_description_before: The passage is dimly lit by pulsing amber veins in the walls. At its far end, a pedestal holds a luminescent cylinder, casting shifting shadows. Kaelen stands at the threshold, cybernetic eye glowing. Vex and Sera are nearby, the deeper chamber echoing with anticipation.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera does not move. The light from the beacon washes over her, casting her shadow long across the chamber floor. She stands with her hand still extended from placing the shard, her pale eyes reflecting the blue glow. For a long moment she simply breathes — and the beacon breathes with her, its light pulsing in time with her heartbeat. The script on the walls glows in response, every character she has spent eleven years learning now illuminated at once. She looks at her hand, then at the cylinder, and when she speaks her voice is unsteady for the first time.] I thought I was chasing fragments of a dead language. [A pause.] I was following a signal I did not know how to hear. [She looks toward Kaelen in the passage, toward the chamber beyond where Seraphis stands under the sky.] The beacon is open. Whatever it was waiting to receive — it is receiving now.
current_scene_name_before: Narrow passage mouth of hidden chamber
current_scene_description_before: The passage is dimly lit by pulsing amber veins in the walls. At its far end, a pedestal holds a luminescent cylinder, casting shifting shadows. Kaelen stands at the threshold, cybernetic eye glowing. Vex and Sera are nearby, the deeper chamber echoing with anticipation.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex looks from the glowing beacon to Seraphis retreating figure. He does not hesitate long.] The signal led me here. But it did not end here — it just changed direction. [He steps away from the pedestal, following Seraphis toward the exit. At the base of the stairwell he pauses, looking back at Sera and Kaelen.] Narsis is where Ryn is heading alone. And if that entity can be felt now, it can be followed. [He meets Seraphis gaze.] I never asked to be part of this. But I have been part of it since the first time I heard that frequency. Might as well see where it ends.
new_scene_name: 楼梯井底部出口处
new_scene_description: 阶梯旋绕而上，出口处透进外界黯淡天光。Vex停步转身，身后是信标室幽深的窄道，前方是通往地表的路。空气里还残留着信标的余韵。
current_scene_name_before: Narrow passage mouth of hidden chamber
current_scene_description_before: The passage is dimly lit by pulsing amber veins in the walls. At its far end, a pedestal holds a luminescent cylinder, casting shifting shadows. Kaelen stands at the threshold, cybernetic eye glowing. Vex and Sera are nearby, the deeper chamber echoing with anticipation.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stands before the beacon as its light begins to steady into a constant glow. She does not turn away from it when she speaks — she is still reading the script as it illuminates, absorbing meaning that was always there, waiting for the light to reveal it.] The beacon was set to receive a single reply. One voice, keyed to this frequency. Whoever that voice belongs to — they are out there. And Ryn is flying toward them right now. [She finally turns, facing Kaelen in the passage, her pale eyes holding a certainty that was not there when she descended the stairs.] I spent eleven years tracing the dead ends of my parents story. This is the first live thread I have ever found. I am not letting it go.
current_scene_name_before: 楼梯井底部出口处
current_scene_description_before: 阶梯旋绕而上，出口处透进外界黯淡天光。Vex停步转身，身后是信标室幽深的窄道，前方是通往地表的路。空气里还残留着信标的余韵。

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The ship docks inside the hollow asteroid. The bay is dark, emergency lights flickering. She powers down and sits for a moment, listening to the hull tick as it cools.] No other ships on the sensors. Got here first. [She grabs the cylinder, checks her blaster, and heads for the airlock.] Time to see what you left me, Mom.
new_scene_name: Hollow asteroid docking bay, emergency lights flickering
new_scene_description: The ship is settled in a dark bay carved from the asteroid's interior. Emergency lights cast long, shifting shadows across the metal floor. The hull ticks as it cools. Ryn sits in the cockpit for a moment, then grabs the cylinder and moves toward the airlock.
current_scene_name_before: 楼梯井底部出口处
current_scene_description_before: 阶梯旋绕而上，出口处透进外界黯淡天光。Vex停步转身，身后是信标室幽深的窄道，前方是通往地表的路。空气里还残留着信标的余韵。

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex climbs the loading ramp of Seraphis ship, pausing at the top to look back at the moon one last time. The cantina is a distant speck. The temple entrance invisible from here. He tucks his hands into his jacket and steps inside.] Never thought I would leave this moon in anything but my own ship. [He moves through the hold to the co-pilot seat, settling in with the ease of someone who has spent more time in cockpits than anywhere else.] But I never thought I would hear a dead world calling my name either. So. Narsis.
current_scene_name_before: Hollow asteroid docking bay, emergency lights flickering
current_scene_description_before: The ship is settled in a dark bay carved from the asteroid's interior. Emergency lights cast long, shifting shadows across the metal floor. The hull ticks as it cools. Ryn sits in the cockpit for a moment, then grabs the cylinder and moves toward the airlock.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera emerges from the temple at a run — not a sprint, but the fastest she has moved since she arrived on this moon. Her cloak streams behind her, the kyber-less beacon left humming in the dark behind her. She crosses the desert toward the south landing pad, where a battered Corellian freighter sits with its loading ramp still down. Seraphis is in the cockpit. Vex is already aboard. She hits the ramp at a jog, gripping the rail, and turns at the top to look back at the temple entrance one last time. Then she steps inside. The ramp lifts. The engines shift pitch.]
new_scene_name: Desert moon landing pad, Corellian freighter boarding
new_scene_description: A dry, windswept moon under a hazy sky. A battered Corellian freighter sits on a scorched landing pad, its loading ramp still down. Sera Vex jogs across the desert sand from a distant temple entrance, reaches the ramp, grips the rail, and turns for one last look. She steps inside. The ramp lifts with a hydraulic hiss, and the engines change pitch, preparing for departure.
current_scene_name_before: Hollow asteroid docking bay, emergency lights flickering
current_scene_description_before: The ship is settled in a dark bay carved from the asteroid's interior. Emergency lights cast long, shifting shadows across the metal floor. The hull ticks as it cools. Ryn sits in the cockpit for a moment, then grabs the cylinder and moves toward the airlock.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [The freighter lifts off, groaning under the thrust. Seraphis guides it above the desert, angles the nose toward open space, and engages the hyperdrive. Stars smear into light. The blue tunnel swallows the ship. In the sudden silence of hyperspace, he leans back in the pilot's seat, the glow in his eyes reflecting off the canopy.] Three days. [His voice is quiet, almost thoughtful.] Three days alone in a tin can with the echo of something ancient rattling in my skull. [He glances at the empty co-pilot seat.] I hope whoever followed packed supplies. I did not.
current_scene_name_before: Desert moon landing pad, Corellian freighter boarding
current_scene_description_before: A dry, windswept moon under a hazy sky. A battered Corellian freighter sits on a scorched landing pad, its loading ramp still down. Sera Vex jogs across the desert sand from a distant temple entrance, reaches the ramp, grips the rail, and turns for one last look. She steps inside. The ramp lifts with a hydraulic hiss, and the engines change pitch, preparing for departure.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen steps through the loading ramp as it begins to rise, his cybernetic eye flickering amber in the dim hold. He finds a spot against the bulkhead, braced against the vibration of takeoff. As the ship breaks atmosphere, he pulls out a small datapad — the hull fragment's serial number still on the screen. He studies it for a moment, then pockets it.] Three days is a lot of time to wonder what we are walking into. [He glances toward Vex in the co-pilot seat, then through the cockpit arch at the blue tunnel of hyperspace.] Ryn has a head start and a message from her mother. We have a fading resonance and a ship full of people who should not have met each other. That is either the worst team ever assembled or the only one that could actually pull this off.
new_scene_name: Corellian freighter hold, just after liftoff
new_scene_description: The dim, metal-lined hold vibrates with the thrum of engines. Kaelen Voss stands braced against a bulkhead, his cybernetic eye casting a faint amber glow. Through the cockpit arch, the blue swirl of hyperspace streaks past. A datapad showing a hull fragment's serial number is tucked into his pocket.
current_scene_name_before: Desert moon landing pad, Corellian freighter boarding
current_scene_description_before: A dry, windswept moon under a hazy sky. A battered Corellian freighter sits on a scorched landing pad, its loading ramp still down. Sera Vex jogs across the desert sand from a distant temple entrance, reaches the ramp, grips the rail, and turns for one last look. She steps inside. The ramp lifts with a hydraulic hiss, and the engines change pitch, preparing for departure.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [In the blue-hushed quiet of hyperspace, Seraphis sits cross-legged on the deck, eyes closed. The silver glow pulses in slow rhythm with his breath.] The entity is not a voice. It is a wound. A撕裂 in the energy field that surrounds all living things. It does not think — it bleeds. And it is bleeding into Ryn through that callsign the same way it bled into me through the crystal. [He opens his eyes.] The question is not what it wants. It does not want. The question is who carved that wound in the first place. Because whoever did — they are still out there. And they made sure we would find it.
current_scene_name_before: Corellian freighter hold, just after liftoff
current_scene_description_before: The dim, metal-lined hold vibrates with the thrum of engines. Kaelen Voss stands braced against a bulkhead, his cybernetic eye casting a faint amber glow. Through the cockpit arch, the blue swirl of hyperspace streaks past. A datapad showing a hull fragment's serial number is tucked into his pocket.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera sits against the bulkhead, her cloak pulled around her, the empty space in her pocket where the kyber fragment used to be. She looks at Seraphis across the dim hold.] When I held the kyber — before I placed it in the beacon — I felt something on the other end. Not a voice. Not words. But an awareness. Like something knew the crystal had been found. [She pauses.] I thought it was the beacon, waiting to be activated. But it was not the beacon. It was wherever the signal was meant to reach. That is what Ryn is flying toward. Not a place. A presence.
current_scene_name_before: Corellian freighter hold, just after liftoff
current_scene_description_before: The dim, metal-lined hold vibrates with the thrum of engines. Kaelen Voss stands braced against a bulkhead, his cybernetic eye casting a faint amber glow. Through the cockpit arch, the blue swirl of hyperspace streaks past. A datapad showing a hull fragment's serial number is tucked into his pocket.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The freighter shudders as it drops out of hyperspace. Through the cockpit viewport, Narsis resolves — not a planet, but a field of debris and hollowed asteroids orbiting a dead star. Somewhere in the darkness, Ryns YT-1300 is docked. Seraphis scans the field, his jaw tight. Vex leans forward in the co-pilot seat. Sera stands in the passage behind them, one hand braced against the bulkhead, her pale eyes fixed on the void ahead.] There. [Sera points past the cockpit — toward a faint emergency light blinking in the dark.] That is her. [She looks at Seraphis, then at Vex.] We are not alone in here. I can feel it.
new_scene_name: YT-1300 cockpit, Narsis debris field
new_scene_description: The cockpit is dimly lit by instrument panels and the faint glow of a dead star. Through the viewport, jagged asteroids and twisted hull fragments drift in silence. Seraphis grips the controls, Vex leans forward in the co-pilot seat, and Sera stands in the passage behind them, pointing toward a distant emergency beacon blinking in the void.
current_scene_name_before: Corellian freighter hold, just after liftoff
current_scene_description_before: The dim, metal-lined hold vibrates with the thrum of engines. Kaelen Voss stands braced against a bulkhead, his cybernetic eye casting a faint amber glow. Through the cockpit arch, the blue swirl of hyperspace streaks past. A datapad showing a hull fragment's serial number is tucked into his pocket.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [The blue tunnel of hyperspace flickers. The ship shudders. On the console, the nav computer counts down — minutes to Narsis. Seraphis stands, bracing himself against the bulkhead, the silver in his eyes brightening.] It knows we are coming. The wound is closing. Or rather — it is finding a new host to heal inside. [He checks his sidearm, then looks at the canopy.] Ryn has been in system for almost three days. If the entity wanted to consume her, it would have happened already. Which means it is waiting. For something else. [The countdown hits zero. Stars slam back into existence.] Or someone.
new_scene_name: YT-1300 cockpit, just exiting hyperspace near Narsis
new_scene_description: The cockpit shudders as the blue tunnel collapses into a sea of stars. Instrument panels hum, the nav computer resets. Seraphis stands near the bulkhead, sidearm in hand, silver eyes fixed on the canopy where the familiar debris field of Narsis slowly resolves. Vex and Sera are still in their seats, the emergency beacon no longer visible.
current_scene_name_before: YT-1300 cockpit, Narsis debris field
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit is dimly lit by instrument panels and the faint glow of a dead star. Through the viewport, jagged asteroids and twisted hull fragments drift in silence. Seraphis grips the controls, Vex leans forward in the co-pilot seat, and Sera stands in the passage behind them, pointing toward a distant emergency beacon blinking in the void.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen steps up to the cockpit, braced against the transit frame as he studies the asteroid field through the viewport. His cybernetic eye cycles through magnification settings, locking onto the distant emergency light.] That is her ship. Docked but not powered down. Emergency beacon still lit means she either left in a hurry or she wanted to make sure we found the right rock. [He looks from Sera to Vex to Seraphis.] We are three days behind her. If that thing bled into her through the callsign, she may not be alone in there anymore. The question is whether we are walking into a reunion or a rescue mission.
current_scene_name_before: YT-1300 cockpit, just exiting hyperspace near Narsis
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit shudders as the blue tunnel collapses into a sea of stars. Instrument panels hum, the nav computer resets. Seraphis stands near the bulkhead, sidearm in hand, silver eyes fixed on the canopy where the familiar debris field of Narsis slowly resolves. Vex and Sera are still in their seats, the emergency beacon no longer visible.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera does not wait for an answer from the dark. She walks forward, her boots steady on the stone, her hand empty at her side. Behind her, she hears the others falling in — Vexs footsteps, Seraphiss quieter tread, Kaelen bringing up the rear. The tunnel narrows, then opens into a vast cavern. At its center: a structure of black stone, smaller than the one on the moon but identical in form. And before it, on her knees, Ryn Zephyr. The cylinder lies open beside her. Her hands are empty. She is not moving. But her eyes are open — fixed on something only she can see. Sera stops at the edge of the cavern.] Ryn. [Softer now.] Come back.
new_scene_name: Underground cavern, black stone structure before Ryn Zephyr
new_scene_description: A vast, echoing cavern lit by an eerie luminescence. At its center stands a smaller replica of the black stone structure from the moon. Ryn Zephyr kneels before it, motionless, the cylinder opened beside her. Sera Vex stands at the cavern's edge, her boots planted on the stone floor. Behind her, Vex, Seraphis, and Kaelen emerge from the tunnel, their footsteps quiet in the vast space.
current_scene_name_before: YT-1300 cockpit, just exiting hyperspace near Narsis
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit shudders as the blue tunnel collapses into a sea of stars. Instrument panels hum, the nav computer resets. Seraphis stands near the bulkhead, sidearm in hand, silver eyes fixed on the canopy where the familiar debris field of Narsis slowly resolves. Vex and Sera are still in their seats, the emergency beacon no longer visible.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She stands in silence for a long moment after the recording ends. Then she pulls the cylinder from the terminal and walks to the next chamber. Inside: rows of data drives, each labeled with Aetherius dates and designations. She runs a hand over them, then picks one at random and slots it into a reader.] Burn it. [She reads the first file. Her expression shifts.] Or use it. [She looks at the ceiling, a hard smile.] Classic Mom. Giving me the choice but knowing exactly which one I would make.
new_scene_name: Data archive chamber, rows of Aetherius drives
new_scene_description: A narrow, dimly lit chamber lined with shelves of data drives, each labeled with Aetherius dates and designations. Ryn Zephyr stands before a reader, a drive slotted in, her hand resting on the device. The faint hum of machinery fills the air.
current_scene_name_before: Underground cavern, black stone structure before Ryn Zephyr
current_scene_description_before: A vast, echoing cavern lit by an eerie luminescence. At its center stands a smaller replica of the black stone structure from the moon. Ryn Zephyr kneels before it, motionless, the cylinder opened beside her. Sera Vex stands at the cavern's edge, her boots planted on the stone floor. Behind her, Vex, Seraphis, and Kaelen emerge from the tunnel, their footsteps quiet in the vast space.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera crosses the cavern slowly and kneels beside Ryn. She does not touch her — not yet. Instead she picks up the open cylinder. It is warm. The script inside matches the beacon, matches the temple walls. But there is something else — a frequency etched into the metal, not carved but burned. She reads it, and her breath catches.] This is not a message from your mother. [She looks at Ryns unseeing eyes.] This is a cage. Your mother did not send you a signal. She trapped something in this cylinder and made sure it would only open for you.
new_scene_name: Cavern floor near the data reader
new_scene_description: A vast cavern opens around a narrow chamber where data drives line the walls. Sera Vex kneels on the damp stone floor beside Ryn Zephyr, holding a warm metal cylinder etched with a burned frequency. The hum of machinery echoes in the larger space.
current_scene_name_before: Data archive chamber, rows of Aetherius drives
current_scene_description_before: A narrow, dimly lit chamber lined with shelves of data drives, each labeled with Aetherius dates and designations. Ryn Zephyr stands before a reader, a drive slotted in, her hand resting on the device. The faint hum of machinery fills the air.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera sets the empty cylinder down. She does not know what she is doing — not really. She has never done this before. But she places her palm against Ryns chest, over her heart, and closes her eyes. She thinks of the kyber fragment, of the way it resonated when Vex touched it. She thinks of the beacon, of the light that passed through her. She thinks of her parents, who she never knew, who she has spent eleven years trying to hear. And she pushes — not with her hand, but with the part of her that has been listening to a signal she did not understand. The cavern flickers. Ryn blinks. And Sera gasps, her eyes flying open, because for one terrible instant she is not in the cavern anymore — she is inside Ryns mind, standing in a dark room where a door is closing.] No. [Her voice cracks.] No, you do not get to take her.
current_scene_name_before: Cavern floor near the data reader
