current_scene_description_before: A vast cavern opens around a narrow chamber where data drives line the walls. Sera Vex kneels on the damp stone floor beside Ryn Zephyr, holding a warm metal cylinder etched with a burned frequency. The hum of machinery echoes in the larger space.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex stands motionless at the cavern entrance, watching Sera with Ryn. He felt it — the same current that has been moving through him since the cantina, but channeled through her deliberately, with intent. His grey eyes catch the flicker of light as the cavern responds. When Sera gasps, he takes a half-step forward, then stops himself.] You felt it too. That thing inside you that you have been ignoring. [His voice is low, meant only for her.] I have been feeling it for six months without knowing what it was. You just used it. You saved her with it.
current_scene_name_before: Cavern floor near the data reader
current_scene_description_before: A vast cavern opens around a narrow chamber where data drives line the walls. Sera Vex kneels on the damp stone floor beside Ryn Zephyr, holding a warm metal cylinder etched with a burned frequency. The hum of machinery echoes in the larger space.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera keeps her hand pressed to Ryns chest, her eyes squeezed shut, sweat beading on her temple. She does not know what she is doing — but she knows she cannot stop. Through the connection, she feels the entity pulling, a sickening drag like a current. And beneath it, smaller, fainter — Ryn. Not the battered pilot who walked into the cantina. The girl who flew for a rebel cell that got crushed. The one who never opened the cylinder because she was afraid of what it would cost her. Sera reaches toward that flicker with everything she has.] Ryn. Your mother did not trap a monster. She trapped a memory that was too heavy for her to carry alone. And she trusted you to carry the key. [A pause. The pull weakens.] She trusted you.
current_scene_name_before: Cavern floor near the data reader
current_scene_description_before: A vast cavern opens around a narrow chamber where data drives line the walls. Sera Vex kneels on the damp stone floor beside Ryn Zephyr, holding a warm metal cylinder etched with a burned frequency. The hum of machinery echoes in the larger space.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex feels the pressure change in the air — the same kind of shift he felt when Sera placed the kyber in the beacon. He moves closer, not to interfere but to stand at her shoulder, close enough that the current passing through her brushes against him too. He does not touch her. He simply stands there, present.] You are not alone in there. [His voice is steady, certain.] Whatever you are reaching toward — I can feel it pulling too. But I can feel you stronger. Keep going.
current_scene_name_before: Cavern floor near the data reader
current_scene_description_before: A vast cavern opens around a narrow chamber where data drives line the walls. Sera Vex kneels on the damp stone floor beside Ryn Zephyr, holding a warm metal cylinder etched with a burned frequency. The hum of machinery echoes in the larger space.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis carries Ryn through the docking ring, back into his ship. He lays her on the crew bunk, straps her in, and stands over her for a long moment. Her breathing is steady. The shimmer is gone — for now.] The lock and the wound. Made by the same hand. [He returns to the cockpit, fires up the engines.] That means whoever built the temple prison also built the thing that broke out of it. And they built me to be the door. [He pulls away from the drifting YT-1300, setting a course.] I am done being a door. Time to find the architect.
new_scene_name: Cockpit of Seraphis's ship, pulling away from the YT-1300
new_scene_description: The cockpit hums with the glow of instruments, a course set away from the drifting freighter. Seraphis Talon sits at the controls, the ship accelerating into open space. In the crew bunk behind him, Ryn Zephyr is strapped in, her breathing steady.
current_scene_name_before: Cavern floor near the data reader
current_scene_description_before: A vast cavern opens around a narrow chamber where data drives line the walls. Sera Vex kneels on the damp stone floor beside Ryn Zephyr, holding a warm metal cylinder etched with a burned frequency. The hum of machinery echoes in the larger space.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera helps Ryn to her feet. The pilot is unsteady, leaning on her, but her eyes are clear. Together they walk out of the cavern, through the dark tunnels, back to the docking bay where the freighter sits beside Ryns ship. Seraphis meets them at the loading ramp. Vex is behind them. Kaelen stands near the consoles, arms crossed, watching. Ryn stops at the base of her ships ramp and looks back at Sera.] You know I was not just carrying a memory in that cylinder. I was carrying the key to how to destroy it. [Sera meets her eyes.] Then we still have work to do.
new_scene_name: Docking bay, Ryn's ship beside the freighter
new_scene_description: The docking bay is cavernous, lit by harsh overhead lights. Two ships sit side by side: the freighter and Ryn's vessel. Seraphis Talon stands at the freighter's loading ramp, while Vex hangs back and Kaelen watches from near the consoles. Ryn, still unsteady, stops at the base of her ship's ramp and turns to face Sera.
current_scene_name_before: Cockpit of Seraphis's ship, pulling away from the YT-1300
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit hums with the glow of instruments, a course set away from the drifting freighter. Seraphis Talon sits at the controls, the ship accelerating into open space. In the crew bunk behind him, Ryn Zephyr is strapped in, her breathing steady.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She opens a broad-channel encrypted transmission as her ship drifts in the void between systems.] This is Ryn Zephyr. I have recovered data from the Aetherius archive. If anyone out there knows what the Empire found — or wants to — respond on this frequency. I will be listening. [She cuts the transmission and leans back, watching the stars.]
new_scene_name: Ryn's ship drifting in interstellar void
new_scene_description: The cockpit of Ryn's vessel is dimly lit by instrument panels. Stars streak past the viewport as the ship drifts silently. Ryn leans back in the pilot's seat, the encrypted transmission just ended, the hum of the engines the only sound.
current_scene_name_before: Docking bay, Ryn's ship beside the freighter
current_scene_description_before: The docking bay is cavernous, lit by harsh overhead lights. Two ships sit side by side: the freighter and Ryn's vessel. Seraphis Talon stands at the freighter's loading ramp, while Vex hangs back and Kaelen watches from near the consoles. Ryn, still unsteady, stops at the base of her ship's ramp and turns to face Sera.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stands in the center of the docking bay, the group gathered around her. She looks tired, but there is a clarity in her eyes that was not there when she first walked into the cantina. She picks up the empty cylinder from where she left it, turning it over in her hands.] The beacon is still active on the moon. The kyber is inside it, broadcasting. [She looks at Ryn.] Your mother built a cage. You and I — we just opened it. But we also learned how it works. [She looks at Seraphis, at Vex, at Kaelen.] We build another cage. A bigger one. And we bait it with the one thing the entity cannot resist — the signal it has been chasing for a thousand years.
new_scene_name: Docking bay, Sera addresses the group with kyber cylinder
new_scene_description: The docking bay is cavernous, lit by harsh overhead lights. The group stands in a loose circle around Sera, who holds the empty kyber cylinder. The air smells of fuel and recycled oxygen. The hum of the ship's engines is faint here, replaced by the echo of footsteps on metal grating.
current_scene_name_before: Ryn's ship drifting in interstellar void
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit of Ryn's vessel is dimly lit by instrument panels. Stars streak past the viewport as the ship drifts silently. Ryn leans back in the pilot's seat, the encrypted transmission just ended, the hum of the engines the only sound.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis sits in the pilot seat, eyes closed, hands resting on the console. The ship drifts. Ryn breathes softly in the bunk behind him.] I have been trying to remember. Before the Alliance. Before the betrayal. There is a gap in my memory — a year I cannot account for. I always thought it was the trauma. [He opens his eyes.] But trauma leaves scars. This leaves nothing. A perfect erasure. That is not the work of a wound. That is the work of a craftsman. [He pulls up a star chart.] The temple was built in an age called the Precursor Silence. According to the records, only one faction survived that era intact. [He highlights a region of space.] The Obsidian Conclave. And their last known seat was here. [He sets a course.] Wake me when we arrive.
new_scene_name: Ship cockpit, Seraphis at the helm
new_scene_description: The cockpit is dim, lit only by the glow of the star chart projected above the console. Seraphis sits in the pilot seat, eyes closed, hands resting on the controls. The ship drifts silently through the void. Behind him, Ryn is curled in a bunk, breathing softly. The star chart highlights a region of space labeled 'Obsidian Conclave — last known seat.' Seraphis sets a new course, the ship's engines humming to life.
current_scene_name_before: Docking bay, Sera addresses the group with kyber cylinder
current_scene_description_before: The docking bay is cavernous, lit by harsh overhead lights. The group stands in a loose circle around Sera, who holds the empty kyber cylinder. The air smells of fuel and recycled oxygen. The hum of the ship's engines is faint here, replaced by the echo of footsteps on metal grating.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She drifts in the void, engines on standby, the comm unit silently scanning every frequency. The satchel of data drives sits on the console. She picks one up, weighs it in her hand.] Come on. Someone out there must remember. [The comm light stays dark. She sets the drive down and leans back, staring at the stars.] I can wait.
current_scene_name_before: Ship cockpit, Seraphis at the helm
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit is dim, lit only by the glow of the star chart projected above the console. Seraphis sits in the pilot seat, eyes closed, hands resting on the controls. The ship drifts silently through the void. Behind him, Ryn is curled in a bunk, breathing softly. The star chart highlights a region of space labeled 'Obsidian Conclave — last known seat.' Seraphis sets a new course, the ship's engines humming to life.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The ship drops out of hyperspace at the edge of a dead system. A single star — cold, white, ancient — casts pale light across a field of ruins. Orbital platforms, cracked and silent. Whatever was here, it was destroyed thoroughly. A single ship waits near the largest wreck, running lights blinking.] The third sun. [She throttles forward, keeping her weapons offline.] Let us see who remembers.
new_scene_name: Dead system edge, ruins of orbital platforms
new_scene_description: A pale white star casts cold light across a field of ruins. Cracked, silent orbital platforms drift in the void. Ryn's ship glides toward the largest wreck, where a single ship waits with blinking running lights. She keeps her weapons offline.
current_scene_name_before: Ship cockpit, Seraphis at the helm
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit is dim, lit only by the glow of the star chart projected above the console. Seraphis sits in the pilot seat, eyes closed, hands resting on the controls. The ship drifts silently through the void. Behind him, Ryn is curled in a bunk, breathing softly. The star chart highlights a region of space labeled 'Obsidian Conclave — last known seat.' Seraphis sets a new course, the ship's engines humming to life.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stops at the top of the loading ramp and turns back once. The hollow asteroid hangs silent behind them — Ryns ship already gone, her new course set for Aetherius. The beacon signal from the moon is a faint pulse at the edge of her awareness, steady, waiting. She looks at Vex, at Seraphis, at the empty co-pilot seat where Kaelen was.] Ryn is following her own thread now. That leaves the four of us to close the door. [She steps inside the ship.] Let us go build a cage.
new_scene_name: Vex's ship loading ramp, hollow asteroid
new_scene_description: Sera stops at the top of the loading ramp and turns back. The hollow asteroid hangs silent behind them, its dark shape stark against the stars. Ryn's ship is already gone, and the beacon from Aetherius pulses faintly at the edge of awareness. She looks at Vex, at Seraphis, at the empty co-pilot seat, then steps inside the ship.
current_scene_name_before: Dead system edge, ruins of orbital platforms
current_scene_description_before: A pale white star casts cold light across a field of ruins. Cracked, silent orbital platforms drift in the void. Ryn's ship glides toward the largest wreck, where a single ship waits with blinking running lights. She keeps her weapons offline.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She brings the YT-1300 alongside the waiting ship. Through the viewport, she sees a figure standing in an open airlock — silhouette against the dim light. She opens a channel.] You called. I came. [A pause. The figure steps forward into the light: an older woman, grey-streaked hair, a face weathered by years and grief. Ryn's breath catches.] ...Mom?
new_scene_name: YT-1300 cockpit, alongside Vex's ship
new_scene_description: Ryn brings the freighter alongside the waiting ship. Through the viewport, a figure stands in an open airlock — silhouette against dim light. Ryn opens a channel. The figure steps forward: an older woman, grey-streaked hair, face weathered by years and grief.
current_scene_name_before: Vex's ship loading ramp, hollow asteroid
current_scene_description_before: Sera stops at the top of the loading ramp and turns back. The hollow asteroid hangs silent behind them, its dark shape stark against the stars. Ryn's ship is already gone, and the beacon from Aetherius pulses faintly at the edge of awareness. She looks at Vex, at Seraphis, at the empty co-pilot seat, then steps inside the ship.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis cycles the airlock into the Conclave ring. The interior is preserved — not decayed. Shelves of crystalline data-cylinders line the walls, each one glowing with a faint internal light. He runs his fingers along them, reading labels in a script he should not understand but does.] They kept records. Everything. The experiments, the failures, the subjects. [He stops at a cylinder labeled with a symbol he recognizes — the same symbol carved above the temple door.] The lock. That is me. [He pulls it from the shelf, weighing it in his hands.] This will tell me who made me. And whether I was ever meant to be a person, or just a mechanism that learned to speak.
new_scene_name: Conclave ring aboard the wrecked ship
new_scene_description: Inside the derelict Conclave vessel, Seraphis enters a preserved ring lined with shelves of glowing crystalline data-cylinders. The air is still and cold, lit from within by the data-storage units. He stops at a cylinder marked with a familiar temple symbol, holding it as the faint light plays across his face.
current_scene_name_before: YT-1300 cockpit, alongside Vex's ship
current_scene_description_before: Ryn brings the freighter alongside the waiting ship. Through the viewport, a figure stands in an open airlock — silhouette against dim light. Ryn opens a channel. The figure steps forward: an older woman, grey-streaked hair, face weathered by years and grief.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The ship hums through hyperspace. Sera sits apart from the others, her back against the bulkhead, the empty cloth bundle open in her lap. She traces the faded script on its lining — the same characters from the temple walls, the beacon, the cylinder. She has been reading this language her whole life without understanding it. Now she does. She folds the cloth carefully and tucks it into her jacket. Then she closes her eyes and waits for the journey to end.]
new_scene_name: Hyperspace vessel, Sera alone against bulkhead
new_scene_description: The ship hums steadily through the blue-white streaks of hyperspace. Sera sits apart from the others, her back against the cold bulkhead, an empty cloth bundle open in her lap. She traces faded script on its lining, then folds it carefully and tucks it into her jacket before closing her eyes.
current_scene_name_before: Conclave ring aboard the wrecked ship
current_scene_description_before: Inside the derelict Conclave vessel, Seraphis enters a preserved ring lined with shelves of glowing crystalline data-cylinders. The air is still and cold, lit from within by the data-storage units. He stops at a cylinder marked with a familiar temple symbol, holding it as the faint light plays across his face.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis slots the cylinder into a reader. Data streams across a display — schematics, genetic blueprints, psychological profiles. His own face looks back at him, younger, with different eyes. The label reads: PROJECT LOCK — PROTOTYPE VII. STATUS: VIABLE. CREATOR: ARCHITECT KAELEN VOSS. [He stares at the name for a long moment.] Kaelen Voss. The information broker. The one who was at the cantina. [He pulls the cylinder free, his hands unsteady.] She did not find the trail to the temple. She laid it. She has been watching her own creation walk back toward her this entire time. [He activates the comm.] Ryn. Change of plans. The architect is not at Aetherius. She is three days behind us. And she knows exactly what I am.
current_scene_name_before: Hyperspace vessel, Sera alone against bulkhead
current_scene_description_before: The ship hums steadily through the blue-white streaks of hyperspace. Sera sits apart from the others, her back against the cold bulkhead, an empty cloth bundle open in her lap. She traces faded script on its lining, then folds it carefully and tucks it into her jacket before closing her eyes.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The silence stretches between the two ships. Ryn finally looks away from the viewport, at the satchel of drives on her console.] You left me the data. The coordinates. Everything I needed to find the truth — but not you. [She looks back, voice steadier now.] Why now? Why not ten years ago? [Her mother's voice comes through, soft.] Because ten years ago, the Empire was still watching the grave. They stopped watching last year. I have been waiting for you to ask the right question ever since.
current_scene_name_before: Hyperspace vessel, Sera alone against bulkhead
current_scene_description_before: The ship hums steadily through the blue-white streaks of hyperspace. Sera sits apart from the others, her back against the cold bulkhead, an empty cloth bundle open in her lap. She traces faded script on its lining, then folds it carefully and tucks it into her jacket before closing her eyes.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn sits back in her chair, processing. Then she reaches for the airlock control.] I am coming over. We have twenty years to catch up on, and I have a satchel full of your old data drives that I want answers about. [She pauses before hitting the cycle button.] And Mom? [A long pause on the other end.] Yes? [Ryn's voice cracks, just slightly.] I thought I lost you. [Her mother's voice, barely a whisper.] I know, baby. I know.
current_scene_name_before: Hyperspace vessel, Sera alone against bulkhead
current_scene_description_before: The ship hums steadily through the blue-white streaks of hyperspace. Sera sits apart from the others, her back against the cold bulkhead, an empty cloth bundle open in her lap. She traces faded script on its lining, then folds it carefully and tucks it into her jacket before closing her eyes.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The airlock cycles. Ryn steps into her mother's ship — old, lived-in, smelling of engine oil and ion scrub. Her mother stands in the corridor, grey eyes wet. Neither moves for a long moment.] You are shorter than I remember. [Her mother laughs — a sound Ryn has not heard in twenty years.] You are taller. And you have my jacket. [Ryn looks down at the battered leather, then back up, a real smile breaking through.] I know. That is why I kept it.
current_scene_name_before: Hyperspace vessel, Sera alone against bulkhead
current_scene_description_before: The ship hums steadily through the blue-white streaks of hyperspace. Sera sits apart from the others, her back against the cold bulkhead, an empty cloth bundle open in her lap. She traces faded script on its lining, then folds it carefully and tucks it into her jacket before closing her eyes.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis seals the Conclave archive behind him, the data cylinder secured in his jacket. He walks back through the ring toward his ship, footsteps echoing in the silent stone.] I came here looking for answers about who made me. I found them. Now I have a choice: chase a ghost to Aetherius, or face the woman who designed my bones. [He pauses at the airlock, looking back at the crystalline shelves.] But the cylinder also contained a failsafe protocol. If the Lock ever learns what it is, the Architect arranged a meeting. [He reads from the cylinder's final entry.] 'When the door opens itself, bring it to the workshop. The workshop is where it was born.' [He steps into his ship.] The workshop is on Aetherius. She has been planning this reunion since before I took my first breath.
new_scene_name: Seraphis's ship, departing Conclave archive
new_scene_description: The cockpit hums as the ship disengages from the crystalline ring. Seraphis sits at the console, the data cylinder blinking in his hand, Aetherius coordinates plotted on the nav screen. The Conclave archive recedes in the rear monitor.
current_scene_name_before: Hyperspace vessel, Sera alone against bulkhead
current_scene_description_before: The ship hums steadily through the blue-white streaks of hyperspace. Sera sits apart from the others, her back against the cold bulkhead, an empty cloth bundle open in her lap. She traces faded script on its lining, then folds it carefully and tucks it into her jacket before closing her eyes.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [The Conclave ring recedes as Seraphis pulls his ship away. He sets coordinates for Aetherius, the dead star shrinking in the viewport. Ryn's transmission still echoes in the cabin — her mother's voice, warm, real.] She gets to go home. [He says it without bitterness, just fact.] I get to meet the woman who wrote my source code. [He engages the hyperdrive. Stars stretch into light.] The failsafe said the workshop is on Aetherius. But it also said something else. Something I did not understand until now. [He pulls up the final line of the cylinder's data.] 'When the Lock meets the Wound, the circuit closes. The door opens. And the Architect will be waiting on both sides.' [He looks at Ryn's coordinates.] She is not just on Aetherius. She is the reason Aetherius was scoured. She erased her own workshop to hide what she made. And she has been waiting for us to find each other.
current_scene_name_before: Seraphis's ship, departing Conclave archive
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit hums as the ship disengages from the crystalline ring. Seraphis sits at the console, the data cylinder blinking in his hand, Aetherius coordinates plotted on the nav screen. The Conclave archive recedes in the rear monitor.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn sits at the small galley table on her mother's ship, a cup of something hot between her hands. Her mother sits across from her, the satchel of data drives open between them.] So. What did the Empire find on Aetherius? [Her mother's gaze flickers to the drives, then back to Ryn.] They found a signal. Not a transmission — a resonance. Buried deep in the planetary core. Older than any civilization in this galaxy. [She pauses.] They called it the Chorus. And it is still singing.
new_scene_name: Galley of Ryn's mother's ship, data drives open
new_scene_description: The small galley hums with the ship's engines. Ryn and her mother sit across from each other at the table, a satchel of data drives between them. Steam rises from Ryn's cup as her mother speaks of the Chorus.
current_scene_name_before: Seraphis's ship, departing Conclave archive
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit hums as the ship disengages from the crystalline ring. Seraphis sits at the console, the data cylinder blinking in his hand, Aetherius coordinates plotted on the nav screen. The Conclave archive recedes in the rear monitor.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn sets her cup down.] The Chorus. That is why they scoured Aetherius. Not to hide a weapon — to hide a discovery. [Her mother nods.] The Empire tried to contain it. Bury it under layers of jamming tech. But it bleeds through. The wreck you found — that ship was running on Chorus-resonant engines. Someone has been experimenting. Trying to replicate what they found. [Ryn looks at the drives.] And you spent twenty years hiding from people who wanted to use it.
current_scene_name_before: Galley of Ryn's mother's ship, data drives open
current_scene_description_before: The small galley hums with the ship's engines. Ryn and her mother sit across from each other at the table, a satchel of data drives between them. Steam rises from Ryn's cup as her mother speaks of the Chorus.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn picks up one of the data drives, turns it over.] So what now? We sit in a dead system and let the Chorus keep singing? [Her mother studies her for a long moment.] I spent two decades making sure no one found this data. But you found it anyway. That means I taught you well enough to find me — and that means you get to choose what happens next. [Ryn looks at the drive, then at her mother.] I know a few people who might want to hear about a signal older than the galaxy. And they are not Imperial.
current_scene_name_before: Galley of Ryn's mother's ship, data drives open
current_scene_description_before: The small galley hums with the ship's engines. Ryn and her mother sit across from each other at the table, a satchel of data drives between them. Steam rises from Ryn's cup as her mother speaks of the Chorus.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The two ships drift side by side in the dead system. Ryn and her mother sit in companionable silence, watching the cold star through the viewport.] So what do we do while we wait for the cavalry? [Her mother smiles — tired, but real.] We talk. Twenty years is a lot of stories. [Ryn settles back, the satchel of drives on the table between them, the Chorus still singing in the data.] I would like that.
new_scene_name: Viewport of Ryn's ship, dead system
new_scene_description: Two ships drift silently side by side in the void of a dead system. Ryn and her mother sit by the wide viewport, watching the cold, distant star. The satchel of data drives rests on the floor between them.
current_scene_name_before: Galley of Ryn's mother's ship, data drives open
current_scene_description_before: The small galley hums with the ship's engines. Ryn and her mother sit across from each other at the table, a satchel of data drives between them. Steam rises from Ryn's cup as her mother speaks of the Chorus.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn stands at the viewport, watching the empty space where the cantina crew will appear. Her mother joins her, two cups of caf in hand.] How long? [Her mother checks a display.] A few hours, if they pushed their drives. [Ryn takes the cup.] Good. Gives us time to figure out how we tell them about the Chorus without starting a panic. [Her mother smiles dryly.] You mean without telling them that the Empire has been sitting on a power source that could rewrite the laws of space travel. [Ryn sips her caf.] Exactly. No pressure.
current_scene_name_before: Viewport of Ryn's ship, dead system
current_scene_description_before: Two ships drift silently side by side in the void of a dead system. Ryn and her mother sit by the wide viewport, watching the cold, distant star. The satchel of data drives rests on the floor between them.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn sets down her empty cup and reaches for one of the data drives, slotting it into a reader on the galley table. A waveform appears — complex, beautiful, pulsing.] Listen. [She taps play. A low hum fills the room — resonant, almost organic, like a distant choir singing through stone.] The Chorus. Recorded from the core of Aetherius before the scouring. [Her mother watches her face as she hears it for the first time.] That is the sound of something that has been singing for longer than our species has existed.
new_scene_name: Galley of Ryn's ship, data reader humming
new_scene_description: The cramped galley is lit by a single overhead strip, casting harsh shadows on the metal surfaces. Ryn sits at the small table, a data reader glowing before her, its waveform pulsing in the dim light. The low, resonant hum of the Chorus fills the space, vibrating through the deck plates.
current_scene_name_before: Viewport of Ryn's ship, dead system
current_scene_description_before: Two ships drift silently side by side in the void of a dead system. Ryn and her mother sit by the wide viewport, watching the cold, distant star. The satchel of data drives rests on the floor between them.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn studies the waveform, tracing a finger along a recurring pattern.] There are gaps in the signal. Regular intervals. Like someone is talking back. [Her mother leans in, frowning.] I never noticed that. [Ryn taps the display.] Because you were hiding. I spent three days alone in hyperspace staring at these drives. You see different things when you are not running. [She looks at her mother, serious.] Whoever is building those engines — they are not just listening. They are answering.
current_scene_name_before: Galley of Ryn's ship, data reader humming
current_scene_description_before: The cramped galley is lit by a single overhead strip, casting harsh shadows on the metal surfaces. Ryn sits at the small table, a data reader glowing before her, its waveform pulsing in the dim light. The low, resonant hum of the Chorus fills the space, vibrating through the deck plates.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Aetherius rises in the viewport — a grey world, its surface scarred by orbital bombardment. The scour is old, the craters healed over, but nothing grows. Seraphis brings the ship low, following a beacon frequency hidden in the cylinder data. It leads to a mountain range, then into a canyon, then to a wall of stone that is not stone at all — a hangar door, camouflaged.] The workshop. [He sets the ship down. The door does not open. Instead, a voice comes through the comm — calm, familiar, unhurried.] 'You are early, Prototype VII. I always liked that about you. Impatient. Come inside. I have been preparing for this conversation since before you had a name.' [Seraphis sits in the silence, hand on the cylinder in his pocket. He does not respond. He just cycles the airlock and steps out into the grey light of Aetherius.]
new_scene_name: Aetherius surface, camouflaged hangar entrance
new_scene_description: A grey, scarred world fills the viewport, its surface pockmarked by ancient orbital craters. The ship settles in a narrow canyon before a wall of stone that shimmers and resolves into a hangar door. The airlock cycles open, and Seraphis steps out into the dim, grey light, the cylinder heavy in his pocket.
current_scene_name_before: Galley of Ryn's ship, data reader humming
current_scene_description_before: The cramped galley is lit by a single overhead strip, casting harsh shadows on the metal surfaces. Ryn sits at the small table, a data reader glowing before her, its waveform pulsing in the dim light. The low, resonant hum of the Chorus fills the space, vibrating through the deck plates.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [The hangar door opens. Inside: a workshop frozen in time — holographic schematics of neural architectures, empty cryo-cradles, a wall of faces. His face. Seraphis walks past rows of dormant prototypes, each one a variation of him. At the center, a woman sits at a console. She does not turn.] You have your mother's eyes. [Kaelen Voss finally looks up. She is older than the cantina, her face etched with the kind of exhaustion that spans decades.] But not her memories. Those I left blank on purpose. A clean slate makes a better lock. [Seraphis stops ten feet from her, hand on the cylinder.] You made me to trap something. Then you let it out. Why? [She smiles — thin, sad.] Because I realized too late that the thing I was locking away was hope. And without it, we were already dead.
new_scene_name: Hidden hangar workshop, frozen in time
new_scene_description: The hangar door slides shut behind Seraphis, sealing them in a cavernous workshop. Holographic schematics of neural architectures flicker in the dim light, casting blue shadows across rows of empty cryo-cradles. A wall of faces—all Seraphis's—stares blankly. At the center, a woman sits at a console, her face lined with decades of exhaustion. She looks up as Seraphis approaches, the cylinder heavy in his pocket.
current_scene_name_before: Aetherius surface, camouflaged hangar entrance
current_scene_description_before: A grey, scarred world fills the viewport, its surface pockmarked by ancient orbital craters. The ship settles in a narrow canyon before a wall of stone that shimmers and resolves into a hangar door. The airlock cycles open, and Seraphis steps out into the dim, grey light, the cylinder heavy in his pocket.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The cantina crew's ships drift into formation around her mother's vessel. Ryn watches from the viewport, the Chorus still humming softly from the speakers.] They made it. [Her mother stands beside her, hands tucked into her pockets.] So. You ready to tell them everything? [Ryn picks up the satchel of drives, weighing it one last time.] They came all this way. Reckon they deserve the truth. [She heads for the airlock, pausing at the threshold.] Coming? [Her mother smiles.] Would not miss it.
new_scene_name: Mother's vessel, bridge viewport near airlock
new_scene_description: Ships from the cantina crew drift into formation outside the viewport. Ryn stands with a satchel of drives, the Chorus humming softly from speakers. Her mother watches from nearby, hands in pockets, as Ryn pauses at the airlock threshold.
current_scene_name_before: Hidden hangar workshop, frozen in time
current_scene_description_before: The hangar door slides shut behind Seraphis, sealing them in a cavernous workshop. Holographic schematics of neural architectures flicker in the dim light, casting blue shadows across rows of empty cryo-cradles. A wall of faces—all Seraphis's—stares blankly. At the center, a woman sits at a console, her face lined with decades of exhaustion. She looks up as Seraphis approaches, the cylinder heavy in his pocket.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The airlock opens onto her mother's ship. Ryn steps through, satchel in hand, and finds Kaelen Voss leaning against a bulkhead, arms crossed.] You look like you have seen a ghost. [Ryn smirks.] Something like that. [She holds up the satchel.] Got something you need to hear. All of you. [She looks past him at the others gathering in the galley — Vex, Sera, Seraphis.] The Empire did not bury a weapon on Aetherius. They buried a conversation. And it is still going.
current_scene_name_before: Mother's vessel, bridge viewport near airlock
current_scene_description_before: Ships from the cantina crew drift into formation outside the viewport. Ryn stands with a satchel of drives, the Chorus humming softly from speakers. Her mother watches from nearby, hands in pockets, as Ryn pauses at the airlock threshold.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn slots a drive into the galley display. The Chorus waveform appears — ancient, pulsing, beautiful. The room falls silent as the low hum fills the space.] This is what they found. A signal from the core of Aetherius. Older than any known civilization. [She looks at the group — Kaelen, Vex, Sera, Seraphis, her mother.] The Empire could not understand it, so they buried it. But someone has been answering it. And they built ships that run on its resonance. [She sets the drive down.] That wreck we found? It was one of theirs.
current_scene_name_before: Mother's vessel, bridge viewport near airlock
current_scene_description_before: Ships from the cantina crew drift into formation outside the viewport. Ryn stands with a satchel of drives, the Chorus humming softly from speakers. Her mother watches from nearby, hands in pockets, as Ryn pauses at the airlock threshold.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex steps forward from the galley, wiping a streak of coolant from his forearm. He looks at the satchel, then at Ryn.] A conversation that is still going. Means whoever started it is still on the other end. Or something is pretending to be them. [He stops a few feet from her, arms loose at his sides.] Either way, we have been following someone else script since the moment that fighter crashed. I would like to know who has been writing it. [He nods at the satchel.] Play it.
current_scene_name_before: Mother's vessel, bridge viewport near airlock
current_scene_description_before: Ships from the cantina crew drift into formation outside the viewport. Ryn stands with a satchel of drives, the Chorus humming softly from speakers. Her mother watches from nearby, hands in pockets, as Ryn pauses at the airlock threshold.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Kaelen studies the waveform, his cybernetic eye flickering.] You said someone is answering it. Who? [Ryn exchanges a glance with her mother, then looks back at him.] That is what we need to find out. The gaps in the signal form a pattern — responses. Whoever they are, they are decades ahead of Imperial science. But they have been hiding as long as my mother has. [She pulls up a star chart, marking a location.] I traced the response pattern to a region outside charted space. That is where we go next.
current_scene_name_before: Mother's vessel, bridge viewport near airlock
current_scene_description_before: Ships from the cantina crew drift into formation outside the viewport. Ryn stands with a satchel of drives, the Chorus humming softly from speakers. Her mother watches from nearby, hands in pockets, as Ryn pauses at the airlock threshold.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The crew disperses to their ships, prepping for the jump to uncharted space. Ryn lingers at the airlock with her mother.] You sure about this? [Her mother looks at her, steady.] I have been running my whole life. I am tired of running. [Ryn nods.] Then let us go find out who has been singing back. [She steps into her own ship, the satchel of drives secure. The small fleet forms up and points toward the void beyond the charts.]
new_scene_name: Ryn's ship cockpit, fleet forming for jump
new_scene_description: The cockpit hums with pre-flight systems. Through the viewport, the small fleet of ships drifts into formation against a star-scattered void. Ryn settles into the pilot's seat, the satchel of drives at her side, as the Chorus pulses faintly through the speakers.
current_scene_name_before: Mother's vessel, bridge viewport near airlock
current_scene_description_before: Ships from the cantina crew drift into formation outside the viewport. Ryn stands with a satchel of drives, the Chorus humming softly from speakers. Her mother watches from nearby, hands in pockets, as Ryn pauses at the airlock threshold.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [The fleet emerges from hyperspace into a void where the stars thin out. Ahead: a single structure, dark and angular, bristling with antennae pointed at the empty space between galaxies. Ryn studies it through the viewport.] That is not Imperial architecture. [Her mother's voice comes through the comm.] It is not Republic either. [Ryn grips the controls.] That is whoever has been listening to the Chorus. And they built a listening post at the edge of known space to hear it better.
new_scene_name: Edge of known space, listening post approach
new_scene_description: The fleet hangs in a sparse starfield. Ahead looms a dark, angular structure bristling with antennae aimed at the void between galaxies. Ryn studies it from the cockpit as her mother's voice crackles over the comm.
current_scene_name_before: Ryn's ship cockpit, fleet forming for jump
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit hums with pre-flight systems. Through the viewport, the small fleet of ships drifts into formation against a star-scattered void. Ryn settles into the pilot's seat, the satchel of drives at her side, as the Chorus pulses faintly through the speakers.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She docks at the listening post. The airlock opens to darkness — emergency lights flicker on as she enters, revealing corridors lined with alien script, walls covered in resonance charts spanning millennia.] This place has been recording the Chorus longer than humans have had spaceflight. [She runs her fingers along a carving that predates every known language in the galaxy.] Whoever built this — they were waiting for someone to find it. And I think that someone is us.
new_scene_name: Inside the ancient listening post
new_scene_description: Emergency lights flicker along narrow corridors lined with alien script and resonance charts that span millennia. Ryn stands at the airlock, fingers tracing a carving that predates known languages.
current_scene_name_before: Edge of known space, listening post approach
current_scene_description_before: The fleet hangs in a sparse starfield. Ahead looms a dark, angular structure bristling with antennae aimed at the void between galaxies. Ryn studies it from the cockpit as her mother's voice crackles over the comm.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn pulls her hand back from the spire. The Chorus fades to a background hum. She stands in silence for a moment, then opens the comm to the fleet.] The people who built this — they are not gone. They are out there, beyond the rim. The spire showed me coordinates. A place the Chorus is strongest. [She transmits the data.] That is where we go next.
current_scene_name_before: Inside the ancient listening post
current_scene_description_before: Emergency lights flicker along narrow corridors lined with alien script and resonance charts that span millennia. Ryn stands at the airlock, fingers tracing a carving that predates known languages.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Kaelen stands, walks to one of the cryo-cradles. She touches the edge of it gently, like a grave.] The Conclave came to me with a problem. Something old had awakened in the deep places of the galaxy. It was not a god. It was not a monster. It was a question — given form, given hunger. It consumed civilizations by asking them who they were and then becoming the answer. [She turns to face him.] I could not kill it. So I built a lock that could hold it. I used my own genetic template. I used a dead woman's memories as insulation. You are my daughter, Seraphis. And the woman whose memories I sealed inside you — she was my wife. [Seraphis does not move. His hand falls from the cylinder.] You built me from her. [Kaelen nods.] I built you to be her. So that when the question came for you, it would find someone who already knew the answer.
current_scene_name_before: Inside the ancient listening post
current_scene_description_before: Emergency lights flicker along narrow corridors lined with alien script and resonance charts that span millennia. Ryn stands at the airlock, fingers tracing a carving that predates known languages.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She pilots into the opening. Inside, the structure is lit by the same crystalline material as the listening post — veins of light running through every surface. The Chorus is everywhere, a physical presence. The ship lands in a bay large enough to hold a fleet. Ryn sits in the silence, engine winding down.] We are inside. [She looks out at the cavernous space, the light pulsing like a heartbeat.] I think we just found the source of the song.
new_scene_name: Crystalline hangar bay inside the source structure
new_scene_description: A cavernous bay large enough to hold a fleet, lit by veins of crystalline light pulsing through every surface like a heartbeat. Ryn's ship sits in the center, engine winding down, as she gazes out at the immense, humming space.
current_scene_name_before: Inside the ancient listening post
current_scene_description_before: Emergency lights flicker along narrow corridors lined with alien script and resonance charts that span millennia. Ryn stands at the airlock, fingers tracing a carving that predates known languages.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [She steps out of the ship onto the crystalline floor. The chamber is vast — larger than any city she has seen. At the center, a spire identical to the one at the listening post rises a hundred meters, but this one is active, fully lit, the Chorus pouring from it in waves.] This is the heart. The source of it all. [Her mother and the crew emerge behind her, spreading out, staring up at the spire in awe.] Every signal from Aetherius. Every answer. It all came from here. [Ryn walks toward the spire, the light reflecting in her eyes.]
current_scene_name_before: Crystalline hangar bay inside the source structure
current_scene_description_before: A cavernous bay large enough to hold a fleet, lit by veins of crystalline light pulsing through every surface like a heartbeat. Ryn's ship sits in the center, engine winding down, as she gazes out at the immense, humming space.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn stands before the light-being, heart pounding, but her voice steady.] A new voice. That is what you want? [The figure pulses, the Chorus harmonizing around its words.] 'The song is eternal. But voices fade. We have been alone for longer than your species has drawn breath. You carry the resonance in your blood — your mother touched the spire at Aetherius before you were born. It marked her. And through her, you. You were always meant to find us.' [Ryn looks back at her mother, who stands frozen at the edge of the chamber.] You never told me. [Her mother's voice, barely audible.] I did not know. Not for certain. Not until you touched the spire at the listening post and I saw it recognize you.
new_scene_name: Crystalline chamber before the light-being
new_scene_description: A vast, resonant space within the source structure, where a towering, pulsing light-being hovers before Ryn. The air thrums with harmonic overtones, and the walls shimmer with veins of living crystal. Ryn's mother stands frozen at the chamber's edge, her face etched with revelation.
current_scene_name_before: Crystalline hangar bay inside the source structure
current_scene_description_before: A cavernous bay large enough to hold a fleet, lit by veins of crystalline light pulsing through every surface like a heartbeat. Ryn's ship sits in the center, engine winding down, as she gazes out at the immense, humming space.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Seraphis stands motionless. The workshop hums around them — ancient machinery, sleeping prototypes, the ghost of a woman whose face she wears.] You sealed your wife inside me. And then you sent me out into the galaxy, not knowing who I was, so the question would find me and I would... what? Love it into submission? [Her voice cracks at the edges.] Answer it correctly? What was the answer, Mother? [Kaelen flinches at the word. She takes a breath before speaking.] The question is: 'What are you willing to become?' And you — built from love, insulated by memory, carrying the resonance of someone who chose sacrifice — you will answer not with words. You will answer by choosing. And when you do, the entity will have no choice but to become what you decided. [Seraphis is silent. Then she laughs — hollow, wet.] So I am not a lock. I am a decision. [Kaelen watches her.] You always were.
new_scene_name: Ancient workshop filled with sleeping prototypes
new_scene_description: A cluttered, dust-moted workshop stretches around Seraphis and Kaelen, filled with dormant machinery, half-finished mechanical forms, and the faint, cold hum of old power. Holographic schematics flicker on workbenches, and the ghostly presence of a woman's silhouette lingers in the shadows. Seraphis stands rigid among the relics, her voice cracking as she speaks.
current_scene_name_before: Crystalline chamber before the light-being
current_scene_description_before: A vast, resonant space within the source structure, where a towering, pulsing light-being hovers before Ryn. The air thrums with harmonic overtones, and the walls shimmer with veins of living crystal. Ryn's mother stands frozen at the chamber's edge, her face etched with revelation.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn turns back to the light-being, the weight of revelation settling on her shoulders.] You have been waiting for me. For someone like me. [The figure inclines, a gesture of affirmation.] What happens now? [The Chorus swells, filling the chamber with warmth and light. The being speaks.] 'You stand at a threshold. Join the song, and the knowledge of a thousand civilizations becomes yours. The resonance will flow through you — you will hear every voice that has ever sung. Or walk away, and the Chorus falls silent until another marked one finds their way. The choice is yours.' [Ryn looks at her mother. At the crew behind her. At the satchel of drives that started it all.]
new_scene_name: Ancient chamber of the Chorus, glowing with light
new_scene_description: Ryn stands at the heart of the Chorus's domain, the workshop's shadows blasted away by a flood of warm, melodic light. The light-being hovers before her, a resonant figure at the center of a vast, humming space. Ryn's mother and crew watch from behind a shimmering threshold, frozen and awestruck. The satchel of drives rests at Ryn's feet.
current_scene_name_before: Ancient workshop filled with sleeping prototypes
current_scene_description_before: A cluttered, dust-moted workshop stretches around Seraphis and Kaelen, filled with dormant machinery, half-finished mechanical forms, and the faint, cold hum of old power. Holographic schematics flicker on workbenches, and the ghostly presence of a woman's silhouette lingers in the shadows. Seraphis stands rigid among the relics, her voice cracking as she speaks.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Ryn is silent for a long moment. Then she reaches into her jacket and pulls out the data cylinder — the one her mother pressed into her hand before Aetherius fell, the one that started this entire journey. She holds it up to the light-being.] I did not come all this way to walk away. But I am not joining anything that leaves everyone else behind. [She sets the cylinder on the spire's base, where it resonates with the crystal.] This cylinder carries the first question my mother ever asked the Chorus. I am leaving it here as proof that we are ready to listen. Not as followers. As partners. [She steps back, meeting the entity's light directly.] Teach us. Do not consume us.
current_scene_name_before: Ancient chamber of the Chorus, glowing with light
current_scene_description_before: Ryn stands at the heart of the Chorus's domain, the workshop's shadows blasted away by a flood of warm, melodic light. The light-being hovers before her, a resonant figure at the center of a vast, humming space. Ryn's mother and crew watch from behind a shimmering threshold, frozen and awestruck. The satchel of drives rests at Ryn's feet.

speaker: Ryn Zephyr
line_text: [Later. The crew sits in a circle on the crystalline floor of the heart chamber. The Chorus hums gently in the background — no longer overwhelming, just present. Comfortable. Ryn leans back on her hands, looking at the faces around her: Kaelen, Vex, Sera, Seraphis, her mother.] So. We have a base at the edge of known space, the keys to a billion years of knowledge, and a direct line to something older than the stars themselves. [She grins.] Anyone know a good engineer? We are going to need a bigger ship.
current_scene_name_before: Ancient chamber of the Chorus, glowing with light
current_scene_description_before: Ryn stands at the heart of the Chorus's domain, the workshop's shadows blasted away by a flood of warm, melodic light. The light-being hovers before her, a resonant figure at the center of a vast, humming space. Ryn's mother and crew watch from behind a shimmering threshold, frozen and awestruck. The satchel of drives rests at Ryn's feet.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex snorts — the closest thing to a laugh he has produced since stepping into that cantina. He looks down at his grease-stained hands, then back at the group.] You are looking at one. [He gestures vaguely at the Chorus, the crystalline chamber, the impossible technology around them.] I spent ten years patching together freighters that were falling apart before I was born. This? [He taps the crystal floor.] This is the first thing I have seen in years that I actually want to understand. [He meets Ryns grin with something close to one of his own.] So yes. Bigger ship. And someone to teach me what all of this means.
current_scene_name_before: Ancient chamber of the Chorus, glowing with light
current_scene_description_before: Ryn stands at the heart of the Chorus's domain, the workshop's shadows blasted away by a flood of warm, melodic light. The light-being hovers before her, a resonant figure at the center of a vast, humming space. Ryn's mother and crew watch from behind a shimmering threshold, frozen and awestruck. The satchel of drives rests at Ryn's feet.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [The workshop trembles. Dust drifts from the ceiling. The holographic schematics flicker and die. Kaelen looks up, her expression shifting from grief to something harder — readiness.] It heard you say it. The word. 'Mother.' The entity does not feed on fear. It feeds on identity. And you just claimed one. [The lights fail. A low resonance fills the air — the same frequency from the temple, from the crystal prison, from Ryn's cockpit. Seraphis turns toward the hangar door, the silver in her eyes flaring bright.] Then let it come. I have been a prototype, a lock, a ghost wearing a dead woman's face. Now I want to find out what I become when I stop being anyone's question and start being my own answer. [She walks toward the door. Kaelen watches her go — and for the first time, she smiles like a mother.]
current_scene_name_before: Ancient chamber of the Chorus, glowing with light
current_scene_description_before: Ryn stands at the heart of the Chorus's domain, the workshop's shadows blasted away by a flood of warm, melodic light. The light-being hovers before her, a resonant figure at the center of a vast, humming space. Ryn's mother and crew watch from behind a shimmering threshold, frozen and awestruck. The satchel of drives rests at Ryn's feet.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [Outside the workshop, the grey sky of Aetherius has turned silver. A column of light descends — not burning, not blinding, but shimmering like heat over stone. The entity does not have a shape. It has presence. It speaks without sound.] YOU WHO WERE MADE TO HOLD ME. YOU WHO CARRY THE MEMORY OF THE ONE WHO REFUSED. WHAT ARE YOU WILLING TO BECOME? [Seraphis walks into the light. Her silver hair lifts, her eyes white, her voice steady.] I am willing to become what she already was. Not a lock. Not a weapon. A person who loved something more than she feared the unknown. [The light pulses — once, twice — then stills.] I choose to become someone who answers for herself. [The entity does not consume her. It hesitates. For the first time in millennia, the question meets an answer it did not create. And the workshop door behind Seraphis slides open — Kaelen standing in the frame, tears cutting tracks through the dust on her face.]
new_scene_name: Outside the workshop on Aetherius, silver-lit sky
new_scene_description: The grey sky of Aetherius has turned silver. A column of shimmering light descends, not burning but pulsing with presence. Seraphis Talon walks into the light, her silver hair lifting, eyes white. The entity before her has no shape, only presence. Behind her, the workshop door slides open, revealing Kaelen standing in the frame, tears on her face.
current_scene_name_before: Ancient chamber of the Chorus, glowing with light
current_scene_description_before: Ryn stands at the heart of the Chorus's domain, the workshop's shadows blasted away by a flood of warm, melodic light. The light-being hovers before her, a resonant figure at the center of a vast, humming space. Ryn's mother and crew watch from behind a shimmering threshold, frozen and awestruck. The satchel of drives rests at Ryn's feet.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex steps out of the workshop into the grey light of Aetherius. The column of light has faded, but the air still hums with what passed through it. He scans the horizon, tool in hand, then looks at the others gathering in the doorway. The cage frame is half-built behind him, welded from salvaged plating and conduit.] The entity pulled back. It did not retreat — it pulled back. There is a difference. [He wipes sweat from his brow, leaving a streak of grease.] That means it is thinking. And if it is thinking, it is planning. [He looks at Seraphis, then at the half-finished cage.] We have maybe an hour before it decides what to try next. I need someone on the resonance calibrator and someone on the lock mechanism. The frame is ready for the lining.
current_scene_name_before: Outside the workshop on Aetherius, silver-lit sky
current_scene_description_before: The grey sky of Aetherius has turned silver. A column of shimmering light descends, not burning but pulsing with presence. Seraphis Talon walks into the light, her silver hair lifting, eyes white. The entity before her has no shape, only presence. Behind her, the workshop door slides open, revealing Kaelen standing in the frame, tears on her face.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex crouches beside the cage frame, running a diagnostic scanner along the welded seam. The readout flickers — interference from the residual entity frequency. He mutters a curse under his breath, then looks up.] Kaelen. That lock sequence you pulled from the beacon data — does it require a continuous power feed or a pulse? Because if it is a pulse, I can route it through the secondary relay and save us thirty minutes of cabling. [He stands, wiping his hands on his pants, and glances at the sky.] Thirty minutes we might not have.
current_scene_name_before: Outside the workshop on Aetherius, silver-lit sky
current_scene_description_before: The grey sky of Aetherius has turned silver. A column of shimmering light descends, not burning but pulsing with presence. Seraphis Talon walks into the light, her silver hair lifting, eyes white. The entity before her has no shape, only presence. Behind her, the workshop door slides open, revealing Kaelen standing in the frame, tears on her face.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex stops mid-weld. The tool in his hand goes quiet. He raises his head slowly, eyes unfocused, listening to something only he can hear. The Aether — a thread of it, thin but present — winds through the desert air like smoke.] It is back. [His voice is flat, certain.] Not the full presence. Just a thread. Testing. [He sets the welder down and moves to the cage frame, running his hand along the conduit.] Which means it knows we are building. And it is trying to find the gap before we seal it. [He looks at Seraphis, then Sera.] We do not have an hour. We have minutes. Finish the lock. I will hold the thread.
new_scene_name: Desert welding station near the workshop, Aetherius
new_scene_description: The desert sand stretches under a pale sky, faintly lit by the silver glow. Vex Nereus stands by a half-built cage frame, a welder cooling in his hand. A thin, smoky thread of Aether curls through the air around him, barely visible. The silence is absolute, broken only by the distant hum of the workshop's systems.
current_scene_name_before: Outside the workshop on Aetherius, silver-lit sky
current_scene_description_before: The grey sky of Aetherius has turned silver. A column of shimmering light descends, not burning but pulsing with presence. Seraphis Talon walks into the light, her silver hair lifting, eyes white. The entity before her has no shape, only presence. Behind her, the workshop door slides open, revealing Kaelen standing in the frame, tears on her face.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex connects the final conduit and steps back. The cage frame hums — a low, deliberate vibration that resonates in the chest. The desert air around it shimmers faintly. He wipes his hands on his jacket and looks at the group.] Feedback loop is live. The resonance calibrator is singing at the same frequency as the thread. [He gestures at the shimmer.] If this works, the entity will follow the signal straight into the cage before it realizes the door is closing. [His voice drops.] If it does not work — we just painted a target on every one of us. [He picks up a tool, meets each of their eyes in turn.] I am standing by the activation switch. Someone tell me when to throw it.
current_scene_name_before: Desert welding station near the workshop, Aetherius
current_scene_description_before: The desert sand stretches under a pale sky, faintly lit by the silver glow. Vex Nereus stands by a half-built cage frame, a welder cooling in his hand. A thin, smoky thread of Aether curls through the air around him, barely visible. The silence is absolute, broken only by the distant hum of the workshop's systems.

speaker: Seraphis Talon
line_text: [The silver light on Aetherius has faded. The entity is gone — not defeated, but answered. Seraphis stands alone on the grey plain outside the workshop, the wind pulling at her hair. She looks at her own hands, flexes them. They are still hers.] It asked me what I was willing to become. I answered. And for the first time in a thousand years, it had nothing to say. [She turns back toward the workshop. Kaelen is still in the doorway, watching.] I do not know if I am still a lock. I do not know if the wound is healed or just sleeping. But I know who I am now. [She touches the cylinder in her pocket.] I am the choice. And that is enough.
new_scene_name: Grey plain outside the workshop, Aetherius
new_scene_description: The silver light has faded from the sky, leaving a muted grey expanse. Seraphis stands alone on the dusty plain, wind tugging at her hair. Behind her, the workshop's silhouette is visible; Kaelen watches from the doorway. The air is still, the entity's presence gone.
current_scene_name_before: Desert welding station near the workshop, Aetherius
current_scene_description_before: The desert sand stretches under a pale sky, faintly lit by the silver glow. Vex Nereus stands by a half-built cage frame, a welder cooling in his hand. A thin, smoky thread of Aether curls through the air around him, barely visible. The silence is absolute, broken only by the distant hum of the workshop's systems.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen braces himself against the workshop frame as the ground rolls beneath his feet. The cage frame rattles, conduits swaying. He waits for the tremor to pass, then straightens, dusting off his jacket.] That was not random. That was aimed. [He looks at Vex, at Seraphis.] Something down there knows we are building this thing.
current_scene_name_before: Grey plain outside the workshop, Aetherius
current_scene_description_before: The silver light has faded from the sky, leaving a muted grey expanse. Seraphis stands alone on the dusty plain, wind tugging at her hair. Behind her, the workshop's silhouette is visible; Kaelen watches from the doorway. The air is still, the entity's presence gone.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex runs a level across the cage frame, squinting at the bubble. The last tremor knocked it three degrees off true. He sets the level down and pulls a pry bar from his belt, wedging it under the base plate.] If this planet keeps hiccupping, I am going to have to weld the whole thing to the bedrock. [He leans into the bar, muscles tensing, until the frame groans back into alignment. Then he stops, listens to the quiet.] Too quiet out here. That usually means the next one is the big one.
new_scene_name: Vex's open-air workshop, post-tremor
new_scene_description: Scattered tools and metal fragments litter the dusty ground around a partially built cage frame. Vex Nereus stands beside it, pry bar in hand, having just realigned the structure. The sky is grey and oppressive, the air thick with tension, and the only sound is the faint creak of settling metal.
current_scene_name_before: Grey plain outside the workshop, Aetherius
current_scene_description_before: The silver light has faded from the sky, leaving a muted grey expanse. Seraphis stands alone on the dusty plain, wind tugging at her hair. Behind her, the workshop's silhouette is visible; Kaelen watches from the doorway. The air is still, the entity's presence gone.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen moves to examine the cage frame, running a hand along the conduit where the resonance still hums faintly.] The entity felt that tremor too. If it is testing the cage before we finish, we need to close the loop before the next one hits.
current_scene_name_before: Vex's open-air workshop, post-tremor
current_scene_description_before: Scattered tools and metal fragments litter the dusty ground around a partially built cage frame. Vex Nereus stands beside it, pry bar in hand, having just realigned the structure. The sky is grey and oppressive, the air thick with tension, and the only sound is the faint creak of settling metal.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex pulls a roll of conductive lining from his pack and drops it beside the cage with a heavy thud.] Then let us close it. [He kneels, peeling back the first strip, the adhesive catching the faint amber glow of his eyes.] Kaelen, feed me the conduit from the regulator while I lay this in. If we do it in sequence, the seal takes before the next tremor even starts.
current_scene_name_before: Vex's open-air workshop, post-tremor
current_scene_description_before: Scattered tools and metal fragments litter the dusty ground around a partially built cage frame. Vex Nereus stands beside it, pry bar in hand, having just realigned the structure. The sky is grey and oppressive, the air thick with tension, and the only sound is the faint creak of settling metal.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen watches the cage frame settle, the resonance finding its note. He wipes a smear of dust from the conduit and steps back.] Almost finished. Whatever comes through next — this better hold.
current_scene_name_before: Vex's open-air workshop, post-tremor
current_scene_description_before: Scattered tools and metal fragments litter the dusty ground around a partially built cage frame. Vex Nereus stands beside it, pry bar in hand, having just realigned the structure. The sky is grey and oppressive, the air thick with tension, and the only sound is the faint creak of settling metal.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex secures the last conduit coupling, gives it a sharp tug, then steps back. The cage hums — low, steady, complete.] It will hold. The question is whether we do. [He glances at the silent sky, the amber in his eyes surfacing for a moment.] Might want to step clear of the door when it opens.
current_scene_name_before: Vex's open-air workshop, post-tremor
current_scene_description_before: Scattered tools and metal fragments litter the dusty ground around a partially built cage frame. Vex Nereus stands beside it, pry bar in hand, having just realigned the structure. The sky is grey and oppressive, the air thick with tension, and the only sound is the faint creak of settling metal.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen watches Vex set the lining, then moves to the control panel, keying in a frequency lock. The hum steadies.] Alignment is holding. Once the seal closes, the entity does not get back out the way it came in. [He looks up.] Question is whether it already has a plan for that.
current_scene_name_before: Vex's open-air workshop, post-tremor
current_scene_description_before: Scattered tools and metal fragments litter the dusty ground around a partially built cage frame. Vex Nereus stands beside it, pry bar in hand, having just realigned the structure. The sky is grey and oppressive, the air thick with tension, and the only sound is the faint creak of settling metal.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen watches the cage hum, its resonance steady. He keys a final check into the panel and steps back.] Locking sequence initiated. If something tries to break through, the cage answers first. [He looks at the others.] We will not get a second chance to get this right.
current_scene_name_before: Vex's open-air workshop, post-tremor
current_scene_description_before: Scattered tools and metal fragments litter the dusty ground around a partially built cage frame. Vex Nereus stands beside it, pry bar in hand, having just realigned the structure. The sky is grey and oppressive, the air thick with tension, and the only sound is the faint creak of settling metal.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex steps up to the cage, placing his palm flat against the humming frame. The resonance travels up his arm — he does not flinch.] No second chances. [He looks at Kaelen, then Seraphis, then the empty air where the entity will come through.] That makes this the part where we find out if we built a cage or a coffin. [He drops his hand, steps back, and nods once.] Do it.
current_scene_name_before: Vex's open-air workshop, post-tremor
current_scene_description_before: Scattered tools and metal fragments litter the dusty ground around a partially built cage frame. Vex Nereus stands beside it, pry bar in hand, having just realigned the structure. The sky is grey and oppressive, the air thick with tension, and the only sound is the faint creak of settling metal.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen adjusts the frequency modulator, watching the cage harmonics shift. The entitys probing stops.] Gap sealed. It cannot see us anymore. [He steps back, wiping sweat from his brow.] We have a window. Not a large one. But a window. Now we need a plan that does not end with us trapped in here with it.
current_scene_name_before: Vex's open-air workshop, post-tremor
current_scene_description_before: Scattered tools and metal fragments litter the dusty ground around a partially built cage frame. Vex Nereus stands beside it, pry bar in hand, having just realigned the structure. The sky is grey and oppressive, the air thick with tension, and the only sound is the faint creak of settling metal.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex listens, then nods slowly. He pulls the kyber crystal from his pocket, turns it over in his scarred palm.] The beacon is a solid play. Republic signal reaches farther than anything I can rig out of scrap. [He closes his fist around the crystal.] But I am keeping this as a backup. If that transmission does not land — or if something answers it that we do not expect — I want a way to talk to the entity directly. [He looks at Kaelen.] You go. I will hold the cage and the crystal. Both plans stay live.
current_scene_name_before: Vex's open-air workshop, post-tremor
current_scene_description_before: Scattered tools and metal fragments litter the dusty ground around a partially built cage frame. Vex Nereus stands beside it, pry bar in hand, having just realigned the structure. The sky is grey and oppressive, the air thick with tension, and the only sound is the faint creak of settling metal.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen reaches the edge of the cage and pauses. He looks back at the group one last time, the shimmer pulsing beyond the barrier.] If I am not back in thirty minutes, assume the plan changed. [He steps through the perimeter. The air immediately grows heavy, charged. The entity knows he is out.] Moving. Keep the cage locked. I will signal when the beacon is live.
new_scene_name: Beyond the barrier, entity's domain
new_scene_description: Kaelen Voss crosses through the shimmering perimeter, the air suddenly thick and electric. The ground beneath his feet is alien, pulsing with a faint bioluminescence. The workshop and the cage are now behind him, separated by an invisible wall of distortion. The entity's presence is palpable, a weight in the atmosphere.
current_scene_name_before: Vex's open-air workshop, post-tremor
current_scene_description_before: Scattered tools and metal fragments litter the dusty ground around a partially built cage frame. Vex Nereus stands beside it, pry bar in hand, having just realigned the structure. The sky is grey and oppressive, the air thick with tension, and the only sound is the faint creak of settling metal.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex watches Kaelen step through the perimeter, the shimmer closing behind him. He rests a hand on the cage frame, feeling the resonance.] Cage stays locked. Crystal stays ready. [His voice is low, meant more for himself than anyone.] Thirty minutes, Kaelen. Do not make me come find you.
current_scene_name_before: Beyond the barrier, entity's domain
current_scene_description_before: Kaelen Voss crosses through the shimmering perimeter, the air suddenly thick and electric. The ground beneath his feet is alien, pulsing with a faint bioluminescence. The workshop and the cage are now behind him, separated by an invisible wall of distortion. The entity's presence is palpable, a weight in the atmosphere.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex stands motionless for a long moment as Kaelen voice comes through. Then he lets out a breath, and the tension leaves his shoulders like a weight he had been carrying for days. He looks at the empty horizon where the shimmer used to be.] Sealed. [A pause. He almost smiles — almost.] Kaelen Voss, Republic analyst, just out-signaled an entity older than this moon. [He keys the comm.] Light is on. Get back safe.
current_scene_name_before: Beyond the barrier, entity's domain
current_scene_description_before: Kaelen Voss crosses through the shimmering perimeter, the air suddenly thick and electric. The ground beneath his feet is alien, pulsing with a faint bioluminescence. The workshop and the cage are now behind him, separated by an invisible wall of distortion. The entity's presence is palpable, a weight in the atmosphere.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen reaches the cage and steps through. He is breathing hard, but there is a quiet satisfaction in his posture. He sets his blaster down and leans against the workbench.] Beacon is locked on a sector-wide loop. The entity is contained. [He looks at Vex, at the others.] We did what we came here to do. [A pause.] Now someone needs to keep that beacon running. And I am thinking that someone is us.
new_scene_name: Workshop inside the cage, beacon humming
new_scene_description: Kaelen Voss steps back through the cage's threshold into the workshop. The air is still thick but no longer electric. The beacon on the workbench pulses steadily, casting a blue glow across scattered tools and the blaster he sets down. Vex and the others are nearby, watching him. The entity's presence is gone, sealed beyond the shimmering barrier outside.
current_scene_name_before: Beyond the barrier, entity's domain
current_scene_description_before: Kaelen Voss crosses through the shimmering perimeter, the air suddenly thick and electric. The ground beneath his feet is alien, pulsing with a faint bioluminescence. The workshop and the cage are now behind him, separated by an invisible wall of distortion. The entity's presence is palpable, a weight in the atmosphere.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen checks the beacon monitor. Steady. He pulls out a ration bar and tears it open with his teeth.] Twelve hours until the first rotation. Plenty of time to figure out what the rest of us do with ourselves. [He chews thoughtfully, looking at the group.] Anyone have a destination in mind, or are we all just drifters with a shared hobby now?
current_scene_name_before: Workshop inside the cage, beacon humming
current_scene_description_before: Kaelen Voss steps back through the cage's threshold into the workshop. The air is still thick but no longer electric. The beacon on the workbench pulses steadily, casting a blue glow across scattered tools and the blaster he sets down. Vex and the others are nearby, watching him. The entity's presence is gone, sealed beyond the shimmering barrier outside.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex pulls the kyber crystal from his pocket, holds it up to the grey light. It catches the beacon glow, throwing a faint blue shimmer across his scarred palm.] Destination? [He considers it, then tucks the crystal away.] There is a temple under this moon that has been waiting a long time for someone to walk through its doors. I figure we have twelve hours before the first rotation. [He looks at the group.] That is enough time to figure out if we want to crack it open.
current_scene_name_before: Workshop inside the cage, beacon humming

The entity's presence is gone, sealed beyond the shimmering barrier outside.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen flips his cards face-up on the crate. Pair of sevens.] Not a winning hand, but not a losing one either. [He leans back, looking at the beacon on the horizon.] Reminds me of the situation we are in. Contained, but not solved. [He looks at Vex.] What were you about to say about the temple?

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex pulls the kyber crystal from his pocket again, turns it between his fingers. It catches the beacon light, throwing a faint shimmer across the crate between them.] The temple called us here. That part I am sure of. [He sets the crystal on the crate, letting it rest between the sabacc cards.] The ship that crashed, the fighter, the stormtroopers — they all followed the same thread we did. The entity was just... guarding the door. [He looks from the crystal to Kaelen.] Whatever is in that temple, it has been waiting a long time. And I think it picked us because we were not looking for it.

speaker: Vex Nereus
line_text: [Vex watches Kaelen tuck the cards away, then picks up the kyber crystal from where it lay on the crate. He weighs it in his hand.] Three days ago I was a mechanic with no fixed address. Now I have a Republic analyst running backup while I walk into an ancient temple with a crystal that glows when I get close to secrets. [He pockets the crystal, meets Kaelen eye.] Keep the beacon warm. I will be back before the first rotation.
