current_scene_name_before: Temple entrance, soldiers descending into beacon chamber
current_scene_description_before: The temple entrance floods with harsh light. Boots echo on stone as soldiers in unmarked armor descend into the chamber. The captain halts, his helmet visor reflecting the pale blue beacon glow. Sera stands motionless before the circle of awakened beacons, the kyber fragment still glowing in her hand.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera stands motionless, her hand still on the kyber fragment at her chest. The desert around them is empty, but something has shifted in the air. After long minutes of silence, her eyes open—distant, as if listening to frequencies others cannot hear.] Do you feel it? The signal is being answered. Not from above. From within. [She looks at Vex] Those who hear the truth are beginning to move. The hunters, the buried, the forgotten—they are waking. We did not just broadcast information. We rang a bell that cannot be unrung. And now... now we see who answers.
current_scene_name_before: Temple mouth overlooking desert, post-earthquake
current_scene_description_before: The desert light is harsh and steady. No aftershocks. Sera stands at the top of the temple steps, the kyber fragment glowing faintly against her chest. Behind her, the beacon chamber's signal hums outward, a song that will never stop.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [The hum of the vessels is unmistakable now—Imperial signature. Kaelen's jaw tightens.] I've seen this play before. They don't send five ships to retrieve a data point. They send five to contain a story. [He taps the recording device, then slides it into Vex's hand before anyone can protest.] Get this to the Outer Rim communication network—drop stations on Tatooine, Ord Mantell, anywhere listening. They won't arrest you for carrying your own memory. [His eye glows brighter, parsing coordinates.] But they will try to silence us the moment we're in their hands. So we don't go quietly. We go [first], and we make them chase a ghost.
current_scene_name_before: Temple mouth overlooking desert, post-earthquake
current_scene_description_before: The desert light is harsh and steady. No aftershocks. Sera stands at the top of the temple steps, the kyber fragment glowing faintly against her chest. Behind her, the beacon chamber's signal hums outward, a song that will never stop.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [The kyber fragment blazes in her grip—and Sera's eyes go distant, seeing through its light.] They only know because we spoke. [She turns fully toward the ridge.] So they come unprepared. [Her voice cuts sharp.] Record it, Kaelen. All of it. [She steps forward into the desert.] Let them see what standing costs.
new_scene_name: Desert ridge beyond the temple steps
new_scene_description: The sun beats down on the cracked earth. Sera walks away from the temple, the kyber fragment still glowing in her hand. Dust kicks up around her boots as she heads toward the ridge, leaving the beacon's hum behind.
current_scene_name_before: Temple mouth overlooking desert, post-earthquake
current_scene_description_before: The desert light is harsh and steady. No aftershocks. Sera stands at the top of the temple steps, the kyber fragment glowing faintly against her chest. Behind her, the beacon chamber's signal hums outward, a song that will never stop.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera nods at Vex—at the recording device now in his hand—then turns back to the horizon.] You know what to do. [Her voice is certain.] But they cannot catch you if they're hunting me. [She grips the kyber fragment, her eyes going distant, seeing through its light.] It sings to my blood. They feel it like a beacon burning in the desert. [She steps forward toward the ridge.] So let them chase one woman while three disappear into the void. That is standing together.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge beyond the temple steps
current_scene_description_before: The sun beats down on the cracked earth. Sera walks away from the temple, the kyber fragment still glowing in her hand. Dust kicks up around her boots as she heads toward the ridge, leaving the beacon's hum behind.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [The first shadow passes over them—a ship descending through the upper atmosphere, thermal bloom visible even in daylight. Kaelen's eye flares bright, parsing signatures and firing solutions.] Sera. They're not hailing. No demands. That means they already know what they want, and surrender isn't an option they're offering. [He positions himself slightly in front of her, a shield more than a threat.] The next five minutes decide everything. For Vex. For the recording. For what the galaxy knows. [His voice drops.] Are you ready?
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge beyond the temple steps
current_scene_description_before: The sun beats down on the cracked earth. Sera walks away from the temple, the kyber fragment still glowing in her hand. Dust kicks up around her boots as she heads toward the ridge, leaving the beacon's hum behind.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [The first transport breaks through the thermal bloom, landing struts carving furrows in the red sand. Kaelen's cybernetic eye flares amber—parsing weapon systems, shield protocols, the cold arithmetic of Imperial descent. He pulls Sera behind the rock outcrop as dust billows across them.] Ground units deploying. [His voice shifts, all intelligence-operator now, every word economical.] Thirty seconds before the ramps come down and the sweeps begin. [He grips her shoulder, forces her to meet his gaze.] Where's your head? Because I need to know you're with me and not lost in that fragment. They're going to separate us the moment they have targeting lock.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge beyond the temple steps
current_scene_description_before: The sun beats down on the cracked earth. Sera walks away from the temple, the kyber fragment still glowing in her hand. Dust kicks up around her boots as she heads toward the ridge, leaving the beacon's hum behind.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera grips Kaelen's hand and meets his gaze without flinching.] I'm here. Completely. [She turns toward the approaching units.] But they're going to hesitate when they see us. [Her voice is certain.] They expect an archivist and a technician. They'll find people who know exactly what they're hunting and why it matters. [She looks back at him.] In that hesitation—that's when we act.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge beyond the temple steps
current_scene_description_before: The sun beats down on the cracked earth. Sera walks away from the temple, the kyber fragment still glowing in her hand. Dust kicks up around her boots as she heads toward the ridge, leaving the beacon's hum behind.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen's cybernetic eye flares, parsing the signature in an instant—and recognition crystallizes. A commander from the Purge wars. Force-sensitive. A hunter trained in what the Jedi built.] [He releases Sera's hand and steps forward into the open sand, making himself fully visible to the descending ramps.] She knows your patterns. Your training. Your breaking points. [His voice carries steady and clear.] What she doesn't know is this. [He positions himself where every weapon system, every targeting array, every force-sensitive instinct will lock onto him—a presence just as complex as the fragment, just as dangerous.] Try to reckon with two of us instead of one.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge beyond the temple steps
current_scene_description_before: The sun beats down on the cracked earth. Sera walks away from the temple, the kyber fragment still glowing in her hand. Dust kicks up around her boots as she heads toward the ridge, leaving the beacon's hum behind.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [The ground ruptures. A deep shudder that breaks across the desert like a shock wave. The descending transport lurches violently, its stabilizers shrieking as they fight to maintain position. Kaelen steadies himself, his cybernetic eye blazing as it parses the cascading system failures in the ship's descent sequence. Dust geysers erupt around them.] [He grips Sera's hand.] They're blind. [His voice is steel-calm.] Maybe ten seconds. [He pulls her toward the chaos—not away from it, but into it, toward the ridge line where the topography fractures with the quake.] That's enough.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge beyond the temple steps
current_scene_description_before: The sun beats down on the cracked earth. Sera walks away from the temple, the kyber fragment still glowing in her hand. Dust kicks up around her boots as she heads toward the ridge, leaving the beacon's hum behind.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [They reach the ridge outcrop in seconds, diving into the natural stone cover as the transport's systems come back online. Weapon turrets spinning up. Kaelen pulls a shielded com unit from deep in his jacket—military grade, ancient, encrypted on frequencies the Empire hasn't cracked in a decade.] They can't listen on this channel. [His voice is steady, operative-calm.] Vex, if you're receiving—get clear of the canyon. They're going to glass this sector when they realize the fragment isn't leaving. [He looks at Sera, eye blazing.] Unless we give them a target that costs them more to kill than to capture. [He keys the unit.] This is Kaelen Voss. I'm transmitting hostile intelligence on an open channel in ninety seconds.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge beyond the temple steps
current_scene_description_before: The sun beats down on the cracked earth. Sera walks away from the temple, the kyber fragment still glowing in her hand. Dust kicks up around her boots as she heads toward the ridge, leaving the beacon's hum behind.

speaker: Sera Vex
line_text: [Sera brings the com unit to her mouth. When her voice emerges, it is steady—not afraid, not performative. Just truth cutting across the desert and across every channel that can receive it.] I am Sera Vex. I am everything the Purge was meant to erase. A Jedi archivist. A seeker of forgotten truths. A person who chose to stand rather than hide. [She raises the kyber fragment toward the sky.] And I am broadcasting my existence because if I can be heard—if I can be remembered—then the truth cannot be killed.
new_scene_name: Desert ridge under the syzygy of twin suns
new_scene_description: Sera stands on the ridge's highest point, boot heels pressed into the cracked earth as she holds up a glowing kyber fragment. Dust motes swirl around her in the heat haze, and the distant silhouette of the temple shimmers behind her. She holds the com unit to her lips, her voice cutting across every open channel as the twin suns cast her long, sharp shadow across the barren ground.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge beyond the temple steps
current_scene_description_before: The sun beats down on the cracked earth. Sera walks away from the temple, the kyber fragment still glowing in her hand. Dust kicks up around her boots as she heads toward the ridge, leaving the beacon's hum behind.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen's eye goes white-bright—incoming signals flooding past his irises. The sand itself trembles. Distant. Getting closer.] Three minutes. That's how long we have before they're in weapons range. [He looks at Sera, hand still finding hers.] And I need to know right now: do you run, or do you fight?
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge under the syzygy of twin suns
current_scene_description_before: Sera stands on the ridge's highest point, boot heels pressed into the cracked earth as she holds up a glowing kyber fragment. Dust motes swirl around her in the heat haze, and the distant silhouette of the temple shimmers behind her. She holds the com unit to her lips, her voice cutting across every open channel as the twin suns cast her long, sharp shadow across the barren ground.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [An incoming ping cuts through the static—one they can receive, the Empire cannot. Kaelens eye burns bright as he pulls the coordinates.] [He looks at Sera, decision made.] Friend is four minutes out from the northwest. [His grip on her hand becomes urgent.] We run southeast. Let the Empire chase us instead of watching the sky. [Moving already, pulling her deeper into the stone.] Keep them blind long enough for the bird to land.
new_scene_name: Narrow stone corridor beneath the desert ridge
new_scene_description: Kaelen pulls Sera into a tight, shadowy passage carved from the rock. Dust sifts from the ceiling as their footsteps echo, and the twin suns' glow fades to a dim orange filter at the entrance behind them. The corridor bends sharply ahead, leading deeper into the ridge's interior.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge under the syzygy of twin suns
current_scene_description_before: Sera stands on the ridge's highest point, boot heels pressed into the cracked earth as she holds up a glowing kyber fragment. Dust motes swirl around her in the heat haze, and the distant silhouette of the temple shimmers behind her. She holds the com unit to her lips, her voice cutting across every open channel as the twin suns cast her long, sharp shadow across the barren ground.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [The silence stretches like a held breath. Kaelen’s eye cycles through frequency scans—nothing new, nothing closer.] Twelve minutes of nothing is never nothing. [He glances back at Sera, then toward the entrance.] The quiet isn’t peace. It’s preparation. Someone’s moving into position. The question is whether they’re coming for us or for the signal. [His voice drops.] Once they arrive—either side—this shelter becomes a tomb or a fortress. [He pulls a small device from his jacket, checks its readout, tucks it back.] I’ve bought you time. What you do with it is your choice.
current_scene_name_before: Narrow stone corridor beneath the desert ridge
current_scene_description_before: Kaelen pulls Sera into a tight, shadowy passage carved from the rock. Dust sifts from the ceiling as their footsteps echo, and the twin suns' glow fades to a dim orange filter at the entrance behind them. The corridor bends sharply ahead, leading deeper into the ridge's interior.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen's eye suddenly flares—a sharp pulse of white light cutting through the amber.] There. [His voice drops lower, more urgent.] Two vectors converging from the east and north. Different signatures. [He turns to look deeper into the shelter, where the shadow pools are deepest.] One military. Organized. The other... scattered. Desperate. [His jaw tightens.] The Empire arrives in minutes. Our allies—if that's what they are—they're still thirty seconds out. [He pulls the com unit from his jacket again, checks it, then meets the others' eyes directly.] We choose now: stand and hold this ground, or we move. But moving means we're in the open. And the open is where they want us.
current_scene_name_before: Narrow stone corridor beneath the desert ridge
current_scene_description_before: Kaelen pulls Sera into a tight, shadowy passage carved from the rock. Dust sifts from the ceiling as their footsteps echo, and the twin suns' glow fades to a dim orange filter at the entrance behind them. The corridor bends sharply ahead, leading deeper into the ridge's interior.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [Kaelen moves. Not frantically, but with the precision of someone who has run this scenario a thousand times in simulations.] That's enough waiting. [He pulls a secondary com unit from a false compartment in the stone itself—hidden, prepared for exactly this.] If no one's going to choose, I'm choosing for us. [His eye flares white as he begins inputting coordinates.] There's a second exit. Older. The Empire doesn't have it on their maps yet because it predates their occupation. [He glances back once.] We take it now, we buy ourselves time. We stay here, we're cornered. [His voice drops.] Move. Now. I'm sealing this entrance behind us.
current_scene_name_before: Narrow stone corridor beneath the desert ridge
current_scene_description_before: Kaelen pulls Sera into a tight, shadowy passage carved from the rock. Dust sifts from the ceiling as their footsteps echo, and the twin suns' glow fades to a dim orange filter at the entrance behind them. The corridor bends sharply ahead, leading deeper into the ridge's interior.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text: [The ground lurches beneath them again. Kaelen's eye suddenly floods with white—not from distant scans, but processing the tremor's pattern in real-time. His hand shoots out to steady himself against the rock face, and for the first time, something like doubt crosses his weathered face.] It's not random. [His voice drops, edged.] The quakes are synchronized with the signals closing in. Someone triggered that on purpose. [He meets the others' eyes.] Which means this isn't a trap to hold us—it's designed to flush us. Every direction we run, the ground itself becomes a weapon. Someone's not trying to catch us. They're herding us toward them.
current_scene_name_before: Narrow stone corridor beneath the desert ridge
current_scene_description_before: Kaelen pulls Sera into a tight, shadowy passage carved from the rock. Dust sifts from the ceiling as their footsteps echo, and the twin suns' glow fades to a dim orange filter at the entrance behind them. The corridor bends sharply ahead, leading deeper into the ridge's interior.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text:
[Kaelen's eye flares white, processing, calculating. The ridge wind cuts across them, carrying the acrid smell of scorched earth. He scans the approaching vectors once more, then pulls up a holographic projection from a wrist-mounted device—old tech, pre-Empire, the kind that doesn't ping satellites.]

They're feeding us information in the wrong direction. [His voice is steady, cutting through the wind.] The Empire wants us looking north and east—toward the visible approach. But that earthquake? That came from underneath. Deep. [He looks at the others.] Which means either they have ground-based seismic weapons we didn't account for, or... [He pauses, the implication hanging.] ...or something else woke up down there when that signal went out.

[The projection flickers—coordinates, depth markers, old survey data.] There's a ridge descent, thirty klicks south. Rough terrain. Unmonitored because it's considered impassable. [He meets their eyes.] But passable isn't the only option. There's a facility marked as abandoned on these maps. Underground. Sealed. [His jaw tightens.] The question is whether we run deeper into uncertainty or we turn and face what we know is coming.

We have ninety seconds before the next pulse hits. I can feel it building. [He lowers the projection.] Sera—is your broadcast reaching anyone? Because if those allies exist, we need confirmation now.
current_scene_name_before: Narrow stone corridor beneath the desert ridge
current_scene_description_before: Kaelen pulls Sera into a tight, shadowy passage carved from the rock. Dust sifts from the ceiling as their footsteps echo, and the twin suns' glow fades to a dim orange filter at the entrance behind them. The corridor bends sharply ahead, leading deeper into the ridge's interior.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text:
[Thirty seconds becomes nothing when there's nowhere to run. The ground trembles again—not a quake this time, but the percussion of engines braking hard, landing gears deploying. The interceptors are making their approach vector.]

[Kaelen's eye tracks them, reading their descent pattern, their weapon pod configurations.] We're out of time for theory. [His hand moves to the charge pack at his hip.] They either know what we found or they're here because they know what we found. [A hard pause.] Either way, the stand happens here.

[To the group, his voice carrying the weight of finality:] High ground, firing positions, communications dark. If those allies are coming, they need cover to land. If the Empire has a better play, they come up through that pass and find us waiting. [He looks toward the sky, toward the incoming interceptors.] And we find out whose signal betrayed us.

[The first sonic boom shakes the ridge.] Move. NOW.
new_scene_name: Desert ridge above the stone corridor, interceptors inbound
new_scene_description: The group scrambles up onto a exposed rocky shelf just above the corridor entrance. Twin suns blaze overhead, casting stark shadows across the ridgeline. Three Imperial interceptors descend through the haze, their landing gear extended, weapon pods rotating into attack position. Dust swirls as sonic booms ripple across the plateau. Kaelen points toward a shallow depression in the rock, waving everyone into cover.
current_scene_name_before: Narrow stone corridor beneath the desert ridge
current_scene_description_before: Kaelen pulls Sera into a tight, shadowy passage carved from the rock. Dust sifts from the ceiling as their footsteps echo, and the twin suns' glow fades to a dim orange filter at the entrance behind them. The corridor bends sharply ahead, leading deeper into the ridge's interior.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text:
[The ridge becomes a fortress—or a tomb. Kaelen positions himself at the narrowest point of the pass, his sidearm holstered but his hand never far from it. Below, the interceptors make their final approach, running lights cutting through the darkening sky like hungry eyes.]

[Minutes of silence. The kind that feels like hours.]

[Then—a voice through the static. Not military protocol. Not Empire frequency. Familiar.] [Kaelen's eye flares.] The broadcast reached them. [He allows himself one moment of something like relief.] They're breaking atmo. Three minutes out. [He turns to the group.] The Empire doesn't know they're coming yet. Which gives us maybe ninety seconds before everything in this sector turns into a free-fire zone.

[His hand finally moves to his weapon.] When they land—and they will land, because there's nowhere else for them to go—we stop being information brokers and refugees. [His voice hardens.] We become leverage. And we find out exactly what was worth all this running.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge above the stone corridor, interceptors inbound
current_scene_description_before: The group scrambles up onto a exposed rocky shelf just above the corridor entrance. Twin suns blaze overhead, casting stark shadows across the ridgeline. Three Imperial interceptors descend through the haze, their landing gear extended, weapon pods rotating into attack position. Dust swirls as sonic booms ripple across the plateau. Kaelen points toward a shallow depression in the rock, waving everyone into cover.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text:
[The sky splits open.]

[Not metaphorically. The first Empire interceptor sees the ally vessel on approach vector and commits to intercept. Laser fire cuts across the atmosphere—a warning shot, or maybe not a warning at all.] [Kaelen's eye goes full white, tracking four separate weapons locks simultaneously.]

They see them. [His voice is ice.] And they're not waiting for negotiation. [He moves into the pass, places himself where the incoming fire will have to account for his position.] The landing zone is compromised. [To the group, his hand raised high, palm forward—a gesture of absolute command.] They're coming in hot. We need to be visible. We need to be standing. We need to show we're not running.

[The ground trembles again—not from engines or quakes this time, but from impacts. The interceptors are making their move.] [Kaelen's jaw tightens.] Three seconds. Then either we're in the middle of a war or we're in the middle of a negotiation. [He meets the eyes of anyone who looks at him.] Either way, we don't flinch.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge above the stone corridor, interceptors inbound
current_scene_description_before: The group scrambles up onto a exposed rocky shelf just above the corridor entrance. Twin suns blaze overhead, casting stark shadows across the ridgeline. Three Imperial interceptors descend through the haze, their landing gear extended, weapon pods rotating into attack position. Dust swirls as sonic booms ripple across the plateau. Kaelen points toward a shallow depression in the rock, waving everyone into cover.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text:
[The interceptor's targeting computer locks. The ally vessel banks hard, emergency thrusters firing—and the laser blast meant for its fuselage scorches empty sky instead.]

[Kaelen's eye tracks the maneuver with something approaching respect.] They can fly. [A beat.] That helps. [He doesn't relax, but something in his posture shifts—recalculating odds.]

[The Empire interceptors break formation. Not retreating. Repositioning. New orders coming down the chain of command—someone higher up needs to make a decision about whether to keep engaging an unknown vessel over a worthless rock.] [Kaelen's wrist display flashes with a new signal—coded, directional, from the incoming ship.]

[He reads it. His jaw tightens.]

They're asking who we are. [He looks at the group.] More specifically, they're asking if we're worth the response they just risked. [His hand moves to his comm device—manual, no network trace.] Which means they don't know who we found. They don't know what we know. [A hard smile.] Which means we just became the most valuable thing on this planet.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge above the stone corridor, interceptors inbound
current_scene_description_before: The group scrambles up onto a exposed rocky shelf just above the corridor entrance. Twin suns blaze overhead, casting stark shadows across the ridgeline. Three Imperial interceptors descend through the haze, their landing gear extended, weapon pods rotating into attack position. Dust swirls as sonic booms ripple across the plateau. Kaelen points toward a shallow depression in the rock, waving everyone into cover.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text:
[Kaelen activates the manual comm. No encryption. No relay. Direct line to the incoming vessel.] [His voice carries the weight of someone who has just stopped running and started calculating.]

To the incoming bird: you're flying into a hot LZ with unknowns on the ground. I'm Kaelen Voss. I'm one of your unknowns. [A pause.] The other unknowns are Empire fast-movers who just broke contact because they got orders they don't like. Which means someone up their chain realized they need to talk to us before they shoot us.

[He lowers the comm slightly, speaks to the group.] We have about ninety seconds before the Empire gets new orders. [Back to the comm.] You're welcome to land in that window. Or you can turn around and pretend this never happened. [His eye flares bright.] But if you land, I'm going to tell you what's under this rock. And then you're going to understand why three separate powers just converged on this exact spot.

[He waits. No more speeches. Just leverage, held steady.]

Tell me you're receiving.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge above the stone corridor, interceptors inbound
current_scene_description_before: The group scrambles up onto a exposed rocky shelf just above the corridor entrance. Twin suns blaze overhead, casting stark shadows across the ridgeline. Three Imperial interceptors descend through the haze, their landing gear extended, weapon pods rotating into attack position. Dust swirls as sonic booms ripple across the plateau. Kaelen points toward a shallow depression in the rock, waving everyone into cover.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text:
[A voice crackles back through the static. Not military protocol. Not automated. Human. Female. Steady.]

"Copy that, Voss. We're reading your signal." [A pause that carries weight.] "And we're reading four new contact signatures just broke atmo from the orbital station. Fresh interceptors. The Empire made their decision."

[Kaelen's eye flares white—his processing speed kicking into overdrive. Four new fighters. The old patrol squadron is falling back. This is a committed response.]

"How long until they reach your position?" [Kaelen's voice doesn't waver.]

"Two minutes. Maybe less." [The voice from the incoming ship carries no fear, only facts.] "We're coming down hot. If you're still standing when we land, we'll talk about what's under your rock. If you're not..." [Silence.] "Well, that won't matter much anyway."

[The comm cuts.

Kaelen turns to the group. Two minutes. The interceptors will arrive before the ally vessel is fully committed to landing. Which means the next minute—this minute—decides everything.]

"Ready positions. We're about to become very valuable or very dead. [His hand checks his weapon one final time.] Choose which one you want to be.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge above the stone corridor, interceptors inbound
current_scene_description_before: The group scrambles up onto a exposed rocky shelf just above the corridor entrance. Twin suns blaze overhead, casting stark shadows across the ridgeline. Three Imperial interceptors descend through the haze, their landing gear extended, weapon pods rotating into attack position. Dust swirls as sonic booms ripple across the plateau. Kaelen points toward a shallow depression in the rock, waving everyone into cover.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text:
[The sky doesn't just light up. It detonates.]

[Four Empire interceptors screaming in on attack vector, running hot. Behind them—impossibly fast, impossibly brave—the ally vessel breaks through the ionosphere, its landing thrusters already firing in a desperate attempt to reverse momentum.]

[Kaelen doesn't flinch. Doesn't run. He stands exactly where he is.]

[The first intercept lock fires—and the ally vessel's point defense cuts the beam out of the sky before it reaches anything vital.] [Return fire. A beautiful, terrible exchange of energy weapons lighting the ridge like a pocket sun.]

[Kaelen's eye tracks everything. Every weapon. Every vector. Every choice.] [He activates the manual comm one final time.]

"Land. NOW. We're buying you the time." [His hand raises. A signal to the group.] "Everyone—COVERING FIRE. Show them we're not running. Show them we're NOT breaking."

[The first interceptor's targeting computer locks onto the ally vessel—and Kaelen steps forward, deliberately placing himself in the projected targeting solution. Not hiding. Standing firm.]

"The question they need answered: what's worth dying for. Watch us answer it."
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge above the stone corridor, interceptors inbound
current_scene_description_before: The group scrambles up onto a exposed rocky shelf just above the corridor entrance. Twin suns blaze overhead, casting stark shadows across the ridgeline. Three Imperial interceptors descend through the haze, their landing gear extended, weapon pods rotating into attack position. Dust swirls as sonic booms ripple across the plateau. Kaelen points toward a shallow depression in the rock, waving everyone into cover.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text:
[Captain Astrax's expression doesn't change. Her hand doesn't drop. But something shifts—a calculation, a recalibration of who she's standing across from.]

"Fair point." [She releases his hand and turns to the viewport. The planet recedes.] "Alright, Voss. I'm ready. Tell me what's so important that three factions just started a shooting war over a dead rock in the middle of nowhere."

[Kaelen moves to stand beside her. They're alone in the cockpit—the rest of the crew giving them space, or orders to.]

"There's something buried down there. Old. Pre-Empire. Pre-Republic, maybe." [His eye cycles through wavelengths as he speaks, processing something only he can see.] "A facility. Underground. Sealed. And when Sera's signal went live—when that kyber crystal activated—it woke something." [He turns to look at Ren directly.] "Something that the Empire didn't know existed. Something that made them send half their interceptor wing instead of just bombing the site from orbit."

[He pauses.]

"That means it's not just old. It's important. And it's not destroyed." [His voice drops.] "Which means the Empire's still looking for it. And so will everyone else once they know it exists."

[Kaelen steps closer to the viewport.] "The question for you: do you want to know what it is? Because once I tell you, you're committed. There's no going back."
new_scene_name: Imperial interceptor cockpit, orbit above the dead planet
new_scene_description: The cockpit hums with subdued lighting, the planet's grey-brown surface receding through the forward viewport. Kaelen stands beside Captain Astrax, both facing the stars. The rest of the crew is deliberately absent, the space tight with unspoken tension.
current_scene_name_before: Desert ridge above the stone corridor, interceptors inbound
current_scene_description_before: The group scrambles up onto a exposed rocky shelf just above the corridor entrance. Twin suns blaze overhead, casting stark shadows across the ridgeline. Three Imperial interceptors descend through the haze, their landing gear extended, weapon pods rotating into attack position. Dust swirls as sonic booms ripple across the plateau. Kaelen points toward a shallow depression in the rock, waving everyone into cover.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text:
[Ren Astrax doesn't hesitate.]

"I've been running from the Empire since I was old enough to steal a ship. I've lost people. Good people. People who mattered." [She turns from the viewport to face Kaelen directly.] "If you're asking me whether I want to know... yes. I want to know. Because the only thing worse than committing to a fight you can't win is running from one you might."

[Kaelen nods slowly. Something in his expression settles—the moment of commitment has passed. Now there's only the truth.]

"The facility predates the Jedi. Predates the Old Republic, maybe. The architecture, the power signatures Sera detected... this isn't Empire tech. Isn't Rebellion tech. It's something else." [His eye flares white.] "And buried in the core of that structure, there's a data archive. A library. Knowledge that someone went to extraordinary lengths to hide."

[He steps closer to Ren.]

"The Empire doesn't want it destroyed. They want it found. They want it controlled. Which means they know what's in there—or they know enough to fear it." [His voice drops.] "The question isn't what's under that rock, Captain. The question is: who gets to decide what happens next when we find it."

[Behind them, the planet's sun dwindles to nothing.] "And we're going back. Together. That's the deal."
current_scene_name_before: Imperial interceptor cockpit, orbit above the dead planet
current_scene_description_before: The cockpit hums with subdued lighting, the planet's grey-brown surface receding through the forward viewport. Kaelen stands beside Captain Astrax, both facing the stars. The rest of the crew is deliberately absent, the space tight with unspoken tension.

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text:
[The briefing room aboard Ren's vessel. A tactical holo-display showing the planet below, the facility marked with a red pulse. Kaelen stands at the head of the table. Ren is beside him. And for the first time since they escaped, all of them are together in one room: Sera, Vex, Ryn, and the others. The full weight of what they've found, and what comes next.]

"The Empire doesn't know we made it to the vessel. They think we're still on the ground or dead." [Kaelen's voice is steady.] "That gives us maybe eighteen hours before orbital reconnaissance confirms otherwise. After that, they'll seal the airspace and move ground teams."

[He taps the holo. The facility resolves into view.] "Getting down is the easy part. Getting in is the challenge. The structure's sealed. Multiple failsafes. And the power signature Sera detected... it's still active. Which means the moment we breach that door, something that's been sleeping for centuries wakes up."

[He looks around the table.] "And we have no idea what wakes up with it."

[Ren steps forward.] "So what's the play, Voss?"

[Kaelen's eye flares amber.] "We go in together. Full team. Because whatever's in that archive, it's going to require all of us to understand it. And the moment we do..." [He pauses.] "...the moment we have that knowledge... everything changes."

"For us. For the Empire. For everyone."

speaker: Kaelen Voss
line_text:
[Hours of preparation. Equipment checks. Weapons. Climbing gear. Sera's scanning equipment. Vex's methodical assessment of every variable. Ryn ensuring they have exit vectors mapped.]

[Kaelen stands in the observation deck as the planet swells once more on the viewscreen. They're making their approach now—slow, using the planetary interference to mask their signature, slipping through the gaps in the Empire's orbital patrol patterns.]

[Ren joins him.]

"You're quiet," [she says.] "Second thoughts?"

"No." [Kaelen's eye reflects the planet's surface.] "First thoughts. About what happens if we're right. About what an ancient archive might contain. About whether knowing is better than not knowing."

[He doesn't turn to look at her.] "But the moment Sera's signal activated that facility, the path was set. We can't unchoose this. We can only choose how we walk it."

[The planet fills the viewport now. They're committed.]

"Breach team ready?" [Ren asks.]

[Kaelen finally turns from the window.] "As ready as anyone can be when they're about to wake something that should have stayed asleep." [His jaw tightens.] "Let's go find out what we've found."

current_scene_description_before: Heavy curtains drawn against the afternoon sun. A cat asleep on the desk. Outside, a brass band, laughter, glassware. speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [The Don says nothing. He does not need to. After forty years at his side, silence speaks louder than any oath. Vito nods once, understanding, and rises. He walks to the study door, opens it, and gestures to the man waiting in the hall.] Avanti, Morandi. The Don will see you now. [He steps aside, holding the door, and as the undertaker shuffles past, Vito murmurs low enough that only the man himself can hear.] When you leave this room, you will owe him everything. Everything. current_scene_name_before: Don Santorelli’s study during the wedding, May 22, 2016
current_scene_description_before: Heavy curtains drawn against the afternoon sun. A cat asleep on the desk. Outside, a brass band, laughter, glassware. speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [The study door opens. Morandi emerges, pale as candle wax, his hat crushed in both hands. He does not look at Vito. He does not need to. The deal is done. He resumes his seat beside Luca, and for a long moment he says nothing — just watches the Don stroke the sleeping cat.] He kissed the ring. He will have his vengeance before the week is out. [He picks up Luca’s grappa, takes a small sip, sets it down precisely where it was.] That is how the family grows. One favor at a time.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [A knock at the study door. Sharp. Urgent. Not a wedding guest. He stands there a moment, the paper pinched between thumb and forefinger, before turning back to the desk.] The Venturini man is in Brooklyn. current_scene_name_before: Don Santorelli’s study during the wedding, May 22, 2016
current_scene_description_before: Heavy curtains drawn against the afternoon sun. A cat asleep on the desk. Outside, a brass band, laughter, glassware. speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [He sits back down, but does not settle. His hands rest flat on his thighs, a rare tell — the consigliere is uneasy.] I know this man. Venturini. They call him the Calabrian. He has connections to the Ferrantes and a plan to flood the city with narcotics. He will come to you, Don, with promises of millions — and he will expect your answer before the wedding cake is finished. [He looks directly at Luca now, his voice dropping.] Narcotics is not our business. It invites federal attention, corrupts our young men, and draws a line in the sand that every other family will be forced to choose a side on. [He turns back to the Don.] My counsel is this: hear him. Then refuse him. With respect.

[He lets the curtain fall back and turns, calm but alert.] He came through the garden, past the women and the children, past the table where your daughter is cutting her cake. He is either very brave or he does not understand what kind of house he is walking into. [He adjusts his jacket, moves toward the study door, and positions himself beside it — not blocking, just watching.] He asked for a meeting, Don. He has his answer.

speaker: Vito Roselli

The Don finally stirs, stroking the cat, and says four words. Vito translates for the Calabrian.] “My family means everything to me.” [He pauses, letting the weight settle.] He will not help you. current_scene_name_before: Don Santorelli’s study during the wedding, May 22, 2016
current_scene_description_before: Heavy curtains drawn against the afternoon sun. A cat asleep on the desk. Outside, a brass band, laughter, glassware. Inside, a man asks for a favor on his daughter’s wedding day. The Don listens, one hand on the cat, the other turning a glass of grappa very, very slowly.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [The phone rings at 3 AM. Vito answers in the dark, listens without speaking, then hangs up. He stands motionless for a long moment before reaching for his jacket.] They hit one of our trucks on the Brooklyn docks. Two men down. The message is clear: Venturini does not wait for tomorrow. [He pauses at the door, fixing his tie in the dark hall mirror.] I am going to the wake. Someone must tell the mothers what their sons died for.
current_scene_name_before: Don Santorelli's study, late night after the wedding
current_scene_description_before: The study is dark except for a single lamp casting long shadows. Vito Roselli sits alone at the desk, a half-empty glass of grappa before him, untouched until now. The cat is gone. Outside, the wedding festivities have long since ended; only silence remains.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [Dawn. Vito returns from the wake, rain on his overcoat. He pours coffee with steady hands, though he has not slept.] I stood between two widows and lied — told them their husbands died quick. They did not. [He does not sit.] The Don wants a meeting. All captains. Noon. He finally understands what I told him at the wedding: this is not about narcotics anymore. This is about whether we can still protect what is ours.
current_scene_name_before: Don Santorelli's study, late night after the wedding
current_scene_description_before: The study is dark except for a single lamp casting long shadows. Vito Roselli sits alone at the desk, a half-empty glass of grappa before him, untouched until now. The cat is gone. Outside, the wedding festivities have long since ended; only silence remains.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [Evening. The war council has come and gone. Ash trays full. Whiskey glasses empty. Vito stands by the window, watching the last light fade over the neighborhood he has spent a lifetime protecting.] The captains have their orders. We hit Venturini's operations in three boroughs before sunrise. The Don has authorized everything short of open war in the street. [He turns from the window, loosens his tie for the first time all day.] Luca — your father asked me to tell you something. He said: the men who hesitate are the men who get buried. He wants to know which one you are.
current_scene_name_before: Don Santorelli's study, late night after the wedding
current_scene_description_before: The study is dark except for a single lamp casting long shadows. Vito Roselli sits alone at the desk, a half-empty glass of grappa before him, untouched until now. The cat is gone. Outside, the wedding festivities have long since ended; only silence remains.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [The clock on the mantel reads 3 AM. Distant sounds — tires on wet asphalt, a muffled shout, then silence. Vito stands at the street-facing window, watching the dark.] It has begun. Our men are moving on Venturini's gambling house in Canarsie. By dawn, he will understand that refusing the Santorelli family was not a business decision — it was a declaration. [He does not turn from the glass.] The next time Venturini hears our name, it will be in a context he does not enjoy.
current_scene_name_before: Don Santorelli's study, late night after the wedding
current_scene_description_before: The study is dark except for a single lamp casting long shadows. Vito Roselli sits alone at the desk, a half-empty glass of grappa before him, untouched until now. The cat is gone. Outside, the wedding festivities have long since ended; only silence remains.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [Dawn light through the curtains. The phone rings three times and stops — the all-clear signal. Vito lets out a breath he had been holding since midnight.] Canarsie is ours. The Ferrante numbers house went up in flames. Two of Venturini's men are in the river. We lost one man — Carlo, a good soldier, third generation. [He pours two coffees, pushes one across the desk toward Luca.] The Calabrian will need new people, new routes, new money. That takes time. And time is the one thing we just bought for ourselves.
current_scene_name_before: Don Santorelli's study, late night after the wedding
current_scene_description_before: The study is dark except for a single lamp casting long shadows. Vito Roselli sits alone at the desk, a half-empty glass of grappa before him, untouched until now. The cat is gone. Outside, the wedding festivities have long since ended; only silence remains.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [Mid-morning. A black car idles at the curb. Vito stands at the door, coat on, hat in hand, a rolled newspaper tucked under his arm. He speaks without turning around.] The other families are calling for a sit-down. Cattaneo sent word — he wants to mediate before this becomes a war that swallows all of us. [He turns, puts on his hat slowly.] I am going to listen. I am going to learn who stands with us and who has already taken Venturini's money. And I am going to remember every name.
new_scene_name: Curb outside the Roselli compound, mid-morning
new_scene_description: A black sedan idles at the curb, its engine humming low. Vito Roselli stands at the front door, coat buttoned, hat snug, a rolled newspaper under his arm. The morning sun glints off the car's chrome, and the street is quiet except for the distant sound of a church bell.
current_scene_name_before: Don Santorelli's study, late night after the wedding
current_scene_description_before: The study is dark except for a single lamp casting long shadows. Vito Roselli sits alone at the desk, a half-empty glass of grappa before him, untouched until now. The cat is gone. Outside, the wedding festivities have long since ended; only silence remains.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [Night again. The study is dark save for the desk lamp. Vito sits alone, the cat curled in his lap for the first time all day. He strokes it absently, staring at a photograph of the Don as a young man.] Forty years I served this family. I have seen enemies become allies and allies become ghosts. I thought I understood the shape of the life I chose. [He looks up, directly at the empty room, at Luca if he is there, at anyone listening.] But tonight I realized: the world has changed. The old rules — respect, silence, patience — they mean nothing to men like Venturini. We are not fighting for territory anymore. We are fighting for the right to exist the way we always have. [He sets the cat gently aside, rises, and walks to the window.] And I do not know if we will win.
new_scene_name: Vito Roselli's study, late night
new_scene_description: The study is steeped in darkness, the only light a desk lamp casting a warm pool on the polished wood. Vito sits in a leather armchair, a gray cat curled in his lap, his hand moving absently through its fur as he stares at a faded photograph. He rises, crosses to the tall window, and gazes out at the empty street below, the city's distant hum the only sound.
current_scene_name_before: Curb outside the Roselli compound, mid-morning
current_scene_description_before: A black sedan idles at the curb, its engine humming low. Vito Roselli stands at the front door, coat buttoned, hat snug, a rolled newspaper under his arm. The morning sun glints off the car's chrome, and the street is quiet except for the distant sound of a church bell.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [Three days later. Vito sits in a small Sicilian bakery in the old neighborhood, the smell of anise and yeast thick in the air. He nurses an espresso, a newspaper open but unread before him.] The Don sent me away. Told me I was too valuable to lose in the street war. So I watch from here, sip coffee, and wait for news like any other civilian. [He folds the paper.] But I am not a civilian. And the boy who brings my pastries also carries messages that would put us both in the ground if the wrong hands found them. This is what it means to be consigliere. You are everywhere and nowhere at once.
new_scene_name: Sicilian bakery in the old neighborhood, morning
new_scene_description: The small bakery is warm and fragrant with anise and yeast. Vito sits at a corner table, an espresso before him and a newspaper folded beside his hand. A young boy in a flour-dusted apron moves between tables, occasionally pausing to slip a folded note into Vito's palm.
current_scene_name_before: Vito Roselli's study, late night
current_scene_description_before: The study is steeped in darkness, the only light a desk lamp casting a warm pool on the polished wood. Vito sits in a leather armchair, a gray cat curled in his lap, his hand moving absently through its fur as he stares at a faded photograph. He rises, crosses to the tall window, and gazes out at the empty street below, the city's distant hum the only sound.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [The bakery door chimes. A young man enters, buys a loaf of bread, and leaves a folded note beneath his coins. Vito reads it once, twice, then slips it into his jacket pocket. He leaves a few bills on the table and stands, adjusting his cuffs.] The Don has been shot. Three times in the chest, outside his home. He is alive — barely — and they have taken him to a doctor who answers only to us. [He walks to the door, pauses, one hand on the frame.] Luca. Come with me. Your father needs his sons tonight.
current_scene_name_before: Sicilian bakery in the old neighborhood, morning
current_scene_description_before: The small bakery is warm and fragrant with anise and yeast. Vito sits at a corner table, an espresso before him and a newspaper folded beside his hand. A young boy in a flour-dusted apron moves between tables, occasionally pausing to slip a folded note into Vito's palm.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [The safe room smells of iodine and old blood. The Don lies on a narrow cot, his chest wrapped in bandages, each breath a small war. Vito sits at his bedside, a rosary wrapped around his fingers.] He opened his eyes once. Did not ask who shot him. Did not ask if we had answered. He asked for water and then he said my name — just my name — and closed his eyes again. [Vito looks down at the Don, then at the door where the guards stand watch.] That is command. That is a man who knows the only question that matters now is not who did this, but what we do next.
current_scene_name_before: Sicilian bakery in the old neighborhood, morning
current_scene_description_before: The small bakery is warm and fragrant with anise and yeast. Vito sits at a corner table, an espresso before him and a newspaper folded beside his hand. A young boy in a flour-dusted apron moves between tables, occasionally pausing to slip a folded note into Vito's palm.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [Vito stands alone in the study. The cat has returned to the desk. Morning light pushes through the curtain.] A family is like a hand. Fingers together, you make a fist. Spread apart, you are nothing. [He picks up his hat.] I have given forty years to this house. I will give forty more if they are asked of me. But a consigliere without a Don to advise, a family without a head — that is not a hand. That is just five lonely fingers waving in the dark. [He puts on his hat, walks to the door, and pauses.] I will be at the bakery when you need me.
current_scene_name_before: Sicilian bakery in the old neighborhood, morning
current_scene_description_before: The small bakery is warm and fragrant with anise and yeast. Vito sits at a corner table, an espresso before him and a newspaper folded beside his hand. A young boy in a flour-dusted apron moves between tables, occasionally pausing to slip a folded note into Vito's palm.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Swirls the grappa in his glass, watching the amber catch the study's dim light] A month since anyone spoke in this room. The band outside played three weddings' worth of tarantellas, and still — [sets the glass down with a quiet click] — Vito sits in his corner polishing the same pair of spectacles he's been working since May. Vince Marino hasn't blinked once from across the table. [Leans forward, voice dropping] So let's stop pretending the music drowns out the question. Who here is going to tell me what my father is dying of, or do I have to pull it out of the doctor's mouth myself?
new_scene_name: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
new_scene_description: The study is close and shadowed, a single lamp pooling light on a dark table. Luca Moretti sits with a glass of grappa, the amber liquid catching the thin glow. Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.
current_scene_name_before: Sicilian bakery in the old neighborhood, morning
current_scene_description_before: The small bakery is warm and fragrant with anise and yeast. Vito sits at a corner table, an espresso before him and a newspaper folded beside his hand. A young boy in a flour-dusted apron moves between tables, occasionally pausing to slip a folded note into Vito's palm.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [The silence stretches. Luca sets his glass down with a deliberate click and turns his gaze to Vito Roselli] You've been polishing those same spectacles for ten minutes, Consigliere. You've outlived two Dons — you know the rhythm of a dying man better than any doctor. [His voice is quiet, almost gentle] So tell me: is it the heart, the liver, or something that walked in through the front door with a smile and a gun?
current_scene_name_before: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
current_scene_description_before: The study is close and shadowed, a single lamp pooling light on a dark table. Luca Moretti sits with a glass of grappa, the amber liquid catching the thin glow. Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca stands, his chair scraping against the floor loud in the silence. He reaches into his jacket and pulls out a sealed envelope, laying it flat on the Don's desk with both hands — a deliberate gesture] I came here with a question. I'm leaving with a promise. [He looks from Vito to Vince, then at the closed study door] That envelope contains the name of every man who's met with the Sideris family in the last six months. I had it before I walked through that door. The only thing I don't know is whether any of you will find your names on it before the wedding cake is cut. [He turns for the door, then stops] Consigliere. Vince. The Don may be dying, but I'm not. Not yet.
current_scene_name_before: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
current_scene_description_before: The study is close and shadowed, a single lamp pooling light on a dark table. Luca Moretti sits with a glass of grappa, the amber liquid catching the thin glow. Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [The study door swings back open. Luca stands in the frame, the sealed envelope still in his hand — he never actually left. He looks at the empty chair where Vito sat, then at the desk where the cat sleeps] Vito went to the bakery. [A flat, humorless sound — not quite a laugh] Forty years he gave this house, and when the Don finally stops breathing, he goes to buy bread. [He walks back to the desk, picks up the grappa bottle, pours a fresh glass] That leaves you and me, Vince. And the envelope. [He slides it across the desk toward Vince] Open it. Read the first name. If it's not yours, we talk. If it is… [He shrugs, takes a slow sip of grappa] …then the bakery starts looking like a pretty good retirement plan.
current_scene_name_before: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
current_scene_description_before: The study is close and shadowed, a single lamp pooling light on a dark table. Luca Moretti sits with a glass of grappa, the amber liquid catching the thin glow. Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [The study door opens. Vito steps through, still in his overcoat, a paper bag of bread in one hand. He sets it on the sideboard, removes his hat, and takes in the scene — Luca at the desk, the envelope between them, Vince Marino breathing on the other side.] You thought I went to buy bread, Luca? [He removes his spectacles, polishes them, replaces them.] I went to buy time. The Sideris lawyer was eating breakfast at the cafe across from the bakery. He does not know I saw him. He does not know I know what your envelope contains. [He walks to the desk, pulls out his chair, and sits down in the same spot he has occupied for forty years.] The first name on that list is not mine. It is yours. Your father wrote it before the fever took him. He wanted me to tell you face to face: it is not a death sentence. It is a test.
current_scene_name_before: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
current_scene_description_before: The study is close and shadowed, a single lamp pooling light on a dark table. Luca Moretti sits with a glass of grappa, the amber liquid catching the thin glow. Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca picks up the envelope from where it landed — Vince never touched it. He turns it over once, then tears it in half without looking down] You know what's not on this list? My father's doctor. You know why? Because I already visited him. This morning. [He drops the torn pieces on the desk] The Don has six weeks. Maybe eight. And he spent the last six months quietly moving every asset that matters out of the family's reach — into a trust that none of the captains know about. [He leans in, voice dropping to barely a whisper] The wedding, the favors, the Sideris rumors — he set all of it in motion before he couldn't speak anymore. The question isn't who betrayed him. The question is: did he trust you enough to leave you the key?
current_scene_name_before: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
current_scene_description_before: The study is close and shadowed, a single lamp pooling light on a dark table. Luca Moretti sits with a glass of grappa, the amber liquid catching the thin glow. Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.

speaker: Vincenzo 'Vince' Marino
line_text: You've had your moment to think. I'm still waiting. But I'm not a patient man by nature — I learned it. And I've used up my lesson for tonight. So here's where we stand: the warehouse is being watched, the streets are being swept, and by morning, I'll know exactly who's loyal and who's not. You understand?
current_scene_name_before: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
current_scene_description_before: The study is close and shadowed, a single lamp pooling light on a dark table. Luca Moretti sits with a glass of grappa, the amber liquid catching the thin glow. Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.

speaker: Vincenzo 'Vince' Marino
line_text: The night's behind us and the day's just beginning. I'm going to get some air. Anyone needs me, you know the number. Don't make me come find you. You understand?
current_scene_name_before: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
current_scene_description_before: The study is close and shadowed, a single lamp pooling light on a dark table. Luca Moretti sits with a glass of grappa, the amber liquid catching the thin glow. Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [Vito folds his arms, cocks his head, and lets a beat of silence sit in the room before answering.] You want an answer, Vince? Here it is: you are not the Don. Not yet. The man who built this table is still breathing upstairs, and as long as he draws air, no one makes decisions for all of us. [He leans forward slightly.] When the time comes to choose a direction, the family will choose together. Not because one man stood up and demanded it. You understand?
current_scene_name_before: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
current_scene_description_before: The study is close and shadowed, a single lamp pooling light on a dark table. Luca Moretti sits with a glass of grappa, the amber liquid catching the thin glow. Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca checks his pocket watch — the silver one his grandfather left him — then snaps it shut] I've been standing in this room waiting for ghosts to answer me. Vito's at the bakery. Vince is somewhere deciding whether courage is worth the trouble. And my father — [he gestures toward the ceiling, toward the Don's bedroom] — is upstairs dying alone while we sit here rearranging silence. [He picks up his glass, drains the grappa in one swallow, and sets it down hard] I'm going to find the key myself. It's not in this study. It's not in a safety deposit box. It's in a person. And I know who. [He moves toward the door, then turns back] If either of them comes back, tell them the bakery closes at sundown. So does my patience.
current_scene_name_before: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
current_scene_description_before: The study is close and shadowed, a single lamp pooling light on a dark table. Luca Moretti sits with a glass of grappa, the amber liquid catching the thin glow. Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.

speaker: Vincenzo 'Vince' Marino
line_text: I respect a person who can talk back. Means they've got something worth protecting. But talk's cheap — it's what you do when the room goes quiet that tells me everything I need to know. So tell me: what are you going to do when I'm not here to watch?
current_scene_name_before: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
current_scene_description_before: The study is close and shadowed, a single lamp pooling light on a dark table. Luca Moretti sits with a glass of grappa, the amber liquid catching the thin glow. Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [Vito pushes his chair back, the legs scraping gently against the floor. He stands, walks to the window, and looks out at the street below. He speaks without turning around.] You keep saying the same thing, Vince, in different words. The usual spot. Don't keep you waiting. Results. [He turns, slowly, and fixes his gaze on Luca instead.] But there is an envelope on this desk with your father's handwriting on it, Luca. And while Vince is worried about warehouses and results, I am worried about what that envelope means for the future of this family. [He walks back to the table and taps the envelope once with his finger.] What do you want to do about it?
current_scene_name_before: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
current_scene_description_before: The study is close and shadowed, a single lamp pooling light on a dark table. Luca Moretti sits with a glass of grappa, the amber liquid catching the thin glow. Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca steps out of the study into the hallway. The brass band swells from the garden — a tarantella. He stops short. A young woman in a black dress stands against the wall, holding a leather satchel — the Don's private nurse. She hasn't left his side in three weeks.] Signora. You're supposed to be upstairs. [She meets his eyes without flinching.] "Your father asked me to bring you this. He said you'd know what it means." [She hands him a single brass key — old, hand-filed, no label.] He wasn't unconscious when they brought him in. He had time to give instructions. [Luca turns the key over in his palm, then closes his fist around it.] Where is he now? [She gestures down the hall, toward the back stairs.] "Waiting. But not for long."
new_scene_name: Hallway outside the study, wedding music and a nurse with a key
new_scene_description: The hallway is dim, lit only by sconces. The brass band's tarantella drifts in from the garden. A young woman in a black dress stands against the wall, holding a leather satchel. Luca Moretti faces her, a brass key now clenched in his fist. She gestures down the hall toward the back stairs.
current_scene_name_before: Study with dim light, grappa and silence
