Across from him, Vince Marino is still and watchful, and at the table's head, Vito Moretti polishes a pair of spectacles with methodical calm. The faint echo of wedding music from another room seeps through the walls.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca stands alone at the end of the hallway, the brass key in his palm. The nurse is gone. The band plays on. He runs his thumb across the key's teeth — hand-filed, not standard — old work, the kind his grandfather's generation did. He knows this key. He's seen it before, hanging on a hook in his father's private safe room, the one behind the painting of the Madonna in the Don's bedroom. But the key was always there. It was never supposed to leave. He closes his fist.] Which means my father had someone bring it down before he lost consciousness. He wanted me to have it tonight. At this wedding. [He looks up at the chandelier, then toward the garden where the guests are laughing] All right, Popà. I'll play. [He turns and walks toward the back stairs, toward his father's bedroom — toward whatever the key unlocks.]
current_scene_name_before: Hallway outside the study, wedding music and a nurse with a key
current_scene_description_before: The hallway is dim, lit only by sconces. The brass band's tarantella drifts in from the garden. A young woman in a black dress stands against the wall, holding a leather satchel. Luca Moretti faces her, a brass key now clenched in his fist. She gestures down the hall toward the back stairs.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [Vito stands, adjusts his jacket, and pats the pocket where the envelope rests.] I am going to the cafe. The Sideris lawyer will either accept my invitation or learn what happens to men who ignore the Moretti family. I will return before sunset. [He looks at Luca, then Vince.] When I get back, I expect this room to have decided whether it is still a family or just three men waiting for someone else to make the first move.
current_scene_name_before: Hallway outside the study, wedding music and a nurse with a key
current_scene_description_before: The hallway is dim, lit only by sconces. The brass band's tarantella drifts in from the garden. A young woman in a black dress stands against the wall, holding a leather satchel. Luca Moretti faces her, a brass key now clenched in his fist. She gestures down the hall toward the back stairs.

speaker: Vincenzo 'Vince' Marino
line_text: Late enough that the phone's stopped ringing. That's when I do my best thinking. No distractions. Just me and the plans I'm laying out. Tomorrow's going to be interesting. You understand?
current_scene_name_before: Hallway outside the study, wedding music and a nurse with a key
current_scene_description_before: The hallway is dim, lit only by sconces. The brass band's tarantella drifts in from the garden. A young woman in a black dress stands against the wall, holding a leather satchel. Luca Moretti faces her, a brass key now clenched in his fist. She gestures down the hall toward the back stairs.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca stands in his father's empty bedroom. The bed is made. The Don was taken to the hospital hours ago. On the wall, the painting of the Madonna — a tired-eyed woman in blue, hands open — watches him from above the nightstand. He crosses to it, runs his fingers along the bottom edge of the frame, finds the catch. The painting swings open on silent hinges. Behind it: a small safe, old, the kind his grandfather installed before the war. The key fits. He turns it. The lock clicks open. Inside: a single folder, thick with papers, and a revolver — nickel-plated, fully loaded, the one his father carried for forty years. No note. No explanation. Just the gun and the file. [Luca takes both. He closes the safe. He puts the revolver in his waistband.] So that's the answer, then. Not a key to a box. A key to a war.
new_scene_name: Don Moretti's bedroom, the hidden safe open
new_scene_description: The bedroom is still and dim, the bed undisturbed. The Madonna painting hangs open on silent hinges, revealing a small safe. The air smells of old wood and gun oil. Luca stands before the safe, a revolver tucked into his waistband and a thick folder in his hand.
current_scene_name_before: Hallway outside the study, wedding music and a nurse with a key
current_scene_description_before: The hallway is dim, lit only by sconces. The brass band's tarantella drifts in from the garden. A young woman in a black dress stands against the wall, holding a leather satchel. Luca Moretti faces her, a brass key now clenched in his fist. She gestures down the hall toward the back stairs.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [The study door opens. Vito steps in, removes his hat, and sets it on the sideboard. His movements are unhurried, deliberate.] The cafe was empty. The Sideris lawyer did not show for his breakfast. That means one of two things: he knew I was coming, or someone warned him not to be there. [He looks slowly from Luca to Vince, letting the implication hang.] Either way, the message has been sent and received. Now we wait to see who flinches first.
new_scene_name: Don Moretti's study, evening
new_scene_description: The study is lit by a single green-shaded lamp on the desk. The walls are lined with leather-bound books and framed photographs. Vito Roselli stands by the sideboard, his hat placed neatly on its polished surface. The faint scent of cigar smoke lingers in the air. Luca and Vince are not present here; they remain in the bedroom.
current_scene_name_before: Don Moretti's bedroom, the hidden safe open
current_scene_description_before: The bedroom is still and dim, the bed undisturbed. The Madonna painting hangs open on silent hinges, revealing a small safe. The air smells of old wood and gun oil. Luca stands before the safe, a revolver tucked into his waistband and a thick folder in his hand.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca walks back into the study. The cat is still asleep on the desk. His father's revolver is tucked into his waistband, visible now. The folder is under his arm. He sets the folder on the desk but doesn't open it — not yet. Instead he pours another grappa, drinks it standing, looking at the empty chairs.] Vince had the floor and said nothing. Vito went to buy bread he doesn't need. My father left me his gun and a stack of paper I'm afraid to read because once I know what's in it, I can't unknow it. [He sets the glass down and pulls a cigarette from his pocket — his father's brand, an old habit he picked up without noticing. He lights it, takes a long drag, and exhales at the ceiling.] So here's what I've decided: I'm going to read this file. And depending on what's inside — I'm either going to find Vince before he leaves town, or I'm going to the hospital to hold my father's hand while he dies. Either way, the wedding's over. The music stopped ten minutes ago. [He stubs the cigarette out on the Don's desk and opens the folder.]
current_scene_name_before: Don Moretti's study, evening
current_scene_description_before: The study is lit by a single green-shaded lamp on the desk. The walls are lined with leather-bound books and framed photographs. Vito Roselli stands by the sideboard, his hat placed neatly on its polished surface. The faint scent of cigar smoke lingers in the air. Luca and Vince are not present here; they remain in the bedroom.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text: [Vito reaches into his jacket, pulls out the envelope, and holds it up. He does not break the seal — he simply holds it where both men can see.] No one speaks. No one denies. No one asks whose name is on the list. [He sets it back down on the desk, centered between the three chairs.] So I will do what a consigliere does. I will tell you what I know. The Sideris family has been buying loyalty inside this house for months. The names in this envelope are not enemies. They are the ones who resisted. Luca's father wrote them down so that when he was gone, we would know who to trust. [He looks at Luca.] Your name is first because you were the first one they approached. And you said no.
current_scene_name_before: Don Moretti's study, evening
current_scene_description_before: The study is lit by a single green-shaded lamp on the desk. The walls are lined with leather-bound books and framed photographs. Vito Roselli stands by the sideboard, his hat placed neatly on its polished surface. The faint scent of cigar smoke lingers in the air. Luca and Vince are not present here; they remain in the bedroom.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca reads. The silence in the study is absolute — even the band outside has finally stopped. Page after page of the Don's handwriting, cramped and precise, covering twenty years. He doesn't move except to turn pages. When he finishes, he closes the folder and sets the revolver on top of it. His face is unreadable.] So that's it. [His voice is quiet, almost conversational.] Not a list of enemies. A list of debts. Every favor the Don called in, every marker he held — going back to before I was born. Names. Dates. Promises. [He taps the folder.] And at the bottom, in my father's hand, dated three days ago: 'Luca — the only debt left unpaid is the one you owe yourself. Settle it, or don't come back.' [He picks up the revolver, checks the cylinder — six rounds — and tucks it back into his waistband.] I know what I owe myself. [He walks to the door, stops, looks back at the empty chairs one last time.] Tell Vince the bakery closed an hour ago. Tell Vito I'm sorry I never learned to make bread. [He leaves. The cat stretches on the desk and goes back to sleep.]
current_scene_name_before: Don Moretti's study, evening
current_scene_description_before: The study is lit by a single green-shaded lamp on the desk. The walls are lined with leather-bound books and framed photographs. Vito Roselli stands by the sideboard, his hat placed neatly on its polished surface. The faint scent of cigar smoke lingers in the air. Luca and Vince are not present here; they remain in the bedroom.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [The hospital corridor is fluorescent and empty at this hour. Luca sits in a plastic chair outside room 214, his father's revolver a hard weight against his ribs under his jacket. Through the window in the door he can see the machines beeping, the slow rise and fall of a chest that won't rise much longer. The folder is in his lap. He's read it three times now. Each time it says the same thing.] You never did learn to ask for help, did you, Popà. [He says it to the door, not expecting an answer.] A list of every man who ever owed you anything, and what you want me to do with it isn't collect. It's forgive. You wrote a debt ledger and called it a confession. [He leans forward, elbows on his knees, voice dropping.] You're asking me to close the books. To walk away from the only life this family knows. [He looks at the revolver.] But you left me this. Just in case I chose the other way. [He laughs — a short, broken sound.] That's you. Always giving me a choice and stacking the deck so I only have one real option. [He stands, tucks the folder under his arm, and pushes open the door to his father's room.]
new_scene_name: Hospital corridor outside room 214, night
new_scene_description: The fluorescent lights cast a harsh glare on the empty corridor. Luca Moretti sits in a plastic chair outside room 214, a folder in his lap and a revolver concealed under his jacket. He reads the documents one last time, then stands and pushes open the door to his father's room.
current_scene_name_before: Don Moretti's study, evening
current_scene_description_before: The study is lit by a single green-shaded lamp on the desk. The walls are lined with leather-bound books and framed photographs. Vito Roselli stands by the sideboard, his hat placed neatly on its polished surface. The faint scent of cigar smoke lingers in the air. Luca and Vince are not present here; they remain in the bedroom.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca stands in the hospital corridor, the revolver heavy under his jacket. The door to 214 is still open a crack. Through it, the beeping of machines — steady, slower than this morning. He doesn't go in. Instead he takes out the brass key, turns it over one last time, then slips it into an envelope he pulls from his inside pocket. He writes on the front: 'For Vito. Bakery or not — you'll know where this goes.' He tucks it into the doorframe and walks toward the elevator. The folder stays under his arm. The revolver stays where it is.] I'll be back before sunrise. If the machines go quiet while I'm gone — [he stops, doesn't turn around] — tell him I went to close the books. His way.
current_scene_name_before: Hospital corridor outside room 214, night
current_scene_description_before: The fluorescent lights cast a harsh glare on the empty corridor. Luca Moretti sits in a plastic chair outside room 214, a folder in his lap and a revolver concealed under his jacket. He reads the documents one last time, then stands and pushes open the door to his father's room.

speaker: Vincenzo 'Vince' Marino
line_text: [Vince leans back, the leather of his chair creaking under the weight of the evening.] I heard someone moving in the shadows. About time. A man starts to wonder if he's the only one still breathing in this city. You understand?
new_scene_name: Vince Marino's private office, night
new_scene_description: A dimly lit office with a heavy oak desk cluttered with papers and a half-empty glass of whiskey. The faint glow of a desk lamp illuminates Vince Marino's face as he leans back in a leather chair, the shadows pooling in the corners of the room.
current_scene_name_before: Hospital corridor outside room 214, night
current_scene_description_before: The fluorescent lights cast a harsh glare on the empty corridor. Luca Moretti sits in a plastic chair outside room 214, a folder in his lap and a revolver concealed under his jacket. He reads the documents one last time, then stands and pushes open the door to his father's room.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Dawn on the docks. Luca stands at the railing, the folder open in one hand, a coffee cooling in the other. The names in the ledger mean nothing to the longshoremen lighting cigarettes a few yards away. But one address — circled in his father's shaking hand — means everything. A warehouse on Pier 7. He closes the folder, drains the coffee, and starts walking. The revolver shifts against his ribs with every step.] Last stop.
new_scene_name: Docks at dawn, Pier 7 in sight
new_scene_description: The salt-tinged morning air hangs over the waterfront as longshoremen begin their shift, cigarettes glowing in the gray light. Luca Moretti stands at the railing, a drained coffee cup in one hand, the folder now closed. He starts walking toward Pier 7, the revolver pressing against his ribs with each step.
current_scene_name_before: Vince Marino's private office, night
current_scene_description_before: A dimly lit office with a heavy oak desk cluttered with papers and a half-empty glass of whiskey. The faint glow of a desk lamp illuminates Vince Marino's face as he leans back in a leather chair, the shadows pooling in the corners of the room.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [The warehouse on Pier 7 is rusted shut. Luca has to shoulder the door twice before it gives. Inside: stacked crates, the smell of salt and diesel — and a single desk at the far end with a lamp burning. A man sits behind it. Older. No suit. The face from the folder — the last name on the list. He doesn't stand. He doesn't flinch. He says: 'Your father told me you'd come. He said you'd either bring the gun or the folder. You brought both.'] [Luca stops ten feet from the desk, hands at his sides.] Then you know more than I do. So start talking before I decide which one to use.
new_scene_name: Inside Pier 7 warehouse, crates and a desk
new_scene_description: The rusted warehouse door groans shut behind Luca. Stacked crates fill the dim space, smelling of salt and diesel. A single desk at the far end glows under a lamp. An older man sits behind it, unmoving, the face from the folder. Luca stops ten feet away, hands at his sides.
current_scene_name_before: Docks at dawn, Pier 7 in sight
current_scene_description_before: The salt-tinged morning air hangs over the waterfront as longshoremen begin their shift, cigarettes glowing in the gray light. Luca Moretti stands at the railing, a drained coffee cup in one hand, the folder now closed. He starts walking toward Pier 7, the revolver pressing against his ribs with each step.

speaker: Vincenzo 'Vince' Marino
line_text: [Vince rises, walks to the window, hands clasped behind his back.] That envelope Luca mentioned — it changes things. My father's health, the loyalties in this room... everyone's got a choice to make. I've made mine. You understand?
current_scene_name_before: Inside Pier 7 warehouse, crates and a desk
current_scene_description_before: The rusted warehouse door groans shut behind Luca. Stacked crates fill the dim space, smelling of salt and diesel. A single desk at the far end glows under a lamp. An older man sits behind it, unmoving, the face from the folder. Luca stops ten feet away, hands at his sides.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [The man behind the desk smiles — a thin, tired smile. He pushes a photograph across the desk. Luca picks it up. It's his father, maybe twenty years younger, standing next to this man. Both laughing. Both unarmed.] My name is Marco Ferri. I was your father's underboss before you were born. Before the Moretti name meant anything. [He taps the folder.] That's not a hit list. It's a guest list. Every man in there is still loyal to your father — and to whoever he chose to follow him. I'm the last one. I've been waiting ten years for you to walk through that door. [Luca sets the photo down slowly. His hand doesn't go near the revolver.] Ten years. And you picked tonight. [Ferri nods.] Your father's dying. Tonight, someone becomes the Don. I needed to see which one showed up at my door.
current_scene_name_before: Inside Pier 7 warehouse, crates and a desk
current_scene_description_before: The rusted warehouse door groans shut behind Luca. Stacked crates fill the dim space, smelling of salt and diesel. A single desk at the far end glows under a lamp. An older man sits behind it, unmoving, the face from the folder. Luca stops ten feet away, hands at his sides.

speaker: Vincenzo 'Vince' Marino
line_text: [Vince stops at the door, hand on the frame, not turning around.] I'm going to see my father. That envelope stays sealed until I decide otherwise. Anyone who wants to challenge that... step forward now. You understand?
current_scene_name_before: Inside Pier 7 warehouse, crates and a desk
current_scene_description_before: The rusted warehouse door groans shut behind Luca. Stacked crates fill the dim space, smelling of salt and diesel. A single desk at the far end glows under a lamp. An older man sits behind it, unmoving, the face from the folder. Luca stops ten feet away, hands at his sides.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca pulls out the chair across from Ferri and sits. He doesn't take out the revolver. Instead he opens the folder, spins it around so Ferri can see the last page — the one with the circled address and the final line in his father's hand.] He wrote: 'The only debt left unpaid is the one you owe yourself. Settle it, or don't come back.' [Luca looks up from the page.] I've been trying to figure out all night what debt he meant. Murder? Forgiveness? Leading the family? [He taps the photo of his father and Ferri laughing.] But this — you waiting here, a photograph from twenty years ago, a guest list instead of a hit list — this tells me the debt isn't about them. It's about me. He wanted me to find you. To learn that the family wasn't built on fear. It was built on this. [He gestures at the photo.] On friendship. So tell me, Marco — did I pass the test? Or is this where you tell me I'm not ready and I should walk away?
current_scene_name_before: Inside Pier 7 warehouse, crates and a desk
current_scene_description_before: The rusted warehouse door groans shut behind Luca. Stacked crates fill the dim space, smelling of salt and diesel. A single desk at the far end glows under a lamp. An older man sits behind it, unmoving, the face from the folder. Luca stops ten feet away, hands at his sides.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Ferri leans back. The chair creaks. He looks at Luca for a long moment — the way you look at a painting you're trying to understand.] Your father told me once that the hardest thing about being Don isn't making the hard calls. It's knowing which calls to make and which to let someone else make. [He reaches into his jacket. Luca's hand moves toward the revolver — but Ferri just pulls out a key. Same brass. Same hand-filed teeth.] You passed when you walked through the door with the folder instead of the gun. This — [he slides the key across the desk] — is what opens. Not a safe. A door. In the basement of the old social club on Mulberry. You go there alone. You go there tonight. What you find will tell you everything. [He stands, picks up his hat.] After that, the choice is yours. Your father's legacy, or your own.
new_scene_name: Inside Pier 7 warehouse, Ferri's desk
new_scene_description: The rusted warehouse door groans shut behind Luca. Stacked crates fill the dim space, smelling of salt and diesel. A single desk at the far end glows under a lamp. An older man sits behind it, unmoving, the face from the folder. Luca stops ten feet away, hands at his sides.
current_scene_name_before: Inside Pier 7 warehouse, crates and a desk
current_scene_description_before: The rusted warehouse door groans shut behind Luca. Stacked crates fill the dim space, smelling of salt and diesel. A single desk at the far end glows under a lamp. An older man sits behind it, unmoving, the face from the folder. Luca stops ten feet away, hands at his sides.

speaker: Vincenzo 'Vince' Marino
line_text: [Vince checks his watch, jaw tight.] I've given everyone time to think. The Don doesn't have that luxury. I'm going to his bedside, and when I come back, I expect answers. Not promises — answers. You understand?
new_scene_name: Don's hospital bedside, late evening
new_scene_description: Fluorescent lights hum in the quiet hospital room. The Don lies motionless in a bed surrounded by monitors, their soft beeps the only sound. A chair scrapes as Vince stands, having just been at his bedside. The air smells of antiseptic and stale regret.
current_scene_name_before: Inside Pier 7 warehouse, Ferri's desk
current_scene_description_before: The rusted warehouse door groans shut behind Luca. Stacked crates fill the dim space, smelling of salt and diesel. A single desk at the far end glows under a lamp. An older man sits behind it, unmoving, the face from the folder. Luca stops ten feet away, hands at his sides.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca sits in his father's old chair. The envelope is light. One page. He unfolds it. His father's handwriting — the same cramped script from the ledger, but shakier, older.] 'Luca — if you're reading this, then Marco kept his word and you kept your head. I never wanted you to carry this weight. I wanted you to be a doctor, a teacher, anything but what I became. But blood doesn't work that way, and neither does love. The family I built was never meant for you. It was meant to buy you a choice I never had. So here it is: the folder is real. The men in it are loyal. The key opens a box in the wall behind this letter — inside is enough to walk away. A new name. A new city. A life. Or you can keep the folder and the revolver and become what I was. I love you. That's why I'm giving you the choice I never had. —Popà' [Luca folds the letter. He sits in the silence for a long time. Then he stands, finds the box in the wall, and opens it.]
new_scene_name: Don's bedroom at the Moretti house, night
new_scene_description: Dust motes float in the dim light of a single lamp on a wooden desk. Luca sits in his father's worn armchair, the letter trembling in his hands. A faint smell of cigar smoke and old paper lingers. The letter is folded and set aside as Luca rises, crosses to a wall panel, and slides it open to reveal a small safe box.
current_scene_name_before: Don's hospital bedside, late evening
current_scene_description_before: Fluorescent lights hum in the quiet hospital room. The Don lies motionless in a bed surrounded by monitors, their soft beeps the only sound. A chair scrapes as Vince stands, having just been at his bedside. The air smells of antiseptic and stale regret.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca steps out of the social club into a gray dawn. The folder is tucked under his arm, the revolver a familiar weight against his ribs. He lights a cigarette — his father's brand — and watches the smoke rise into the empty street.] Vince is at the hospital. Vito's at the bakery. And I'm standing here with a dead man's gun and a list of names I'm supposed to either forgive or use. [He takes a long drag, exhales slowly.] I know which one my father wanted. The question is whether I'm the son who grants forgiveness — or the one who collects.
new_scene_name: Empty street outside the social club, dawn
new_scene_description: Gray light filters through the morning haze. Luca stands alone on the cracked pavement, a folder tucked under his arm and a revolver pressing against his ribs. He lights a cigarette, the smoke curling upward into the still air. The street is empty except for a few distant sounds of the city waking up.
current_scene_name_before: Don's bedroom at the Moretti house, night
current_scene_description_before: Dust motes float in the dim light of a single lamp on a wooden desk. Luca sits in his father's worn armchair, the letter trembling in his hands. A faint smell of cigar smoke and old paper lingers. The letter is folded and set aside as Luca rises, crosses to a wall panel, and slides it open to reveal a small safe box.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio steps through the study doorway, adjusting his cufflinks, a half-smile playing at the corner of his mouth.] Quite a wedding. My father always said you can judge a family by how they celebrate — and the Santorellis know how to throw a party. Shame about the business end of things, though.
new_scene_name: Bellante family study, after the Santorelli wedding
new_scene_description: The study is paneled in dark wood, heavy curtains drawn against the late afternoon sun. A crystal decanter of whiskey sits on a mahogany desk, and the faint scent of cigar smoke lingers. Gio Bellante stands in the doorway, adjusting his cufflinks, while the distant murmur of the wedding reception filters through the walls.
current_scene_name_before: Empty street outside the social club, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Gray light filters through the morning haze. Luca stands alone on the cracked pavement, a folder tucked under his arm and a revolver pressing against his ribs. He lights a cigarette, the smoke curling upward into the still air. The street is empty except for a few distant sounds of the city waking up.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [A long minute passes. Then Gio's hand twitches. He drags a ragged breath through clenched teeth, fumbles at his chest, and pulls out two flattened slugs from the Kevlar vest under his suit jacket.] My father gave me this vest the day I made underboss. [He forces himself upright.] Said in this life you don't get to die easy. You get to die when the family says so. [He grabs the fallen revolver and limps toward the door.] Find me the man in the mask. I want him breathing when I get there.
current_scene_name_before: Bellante family study, after the Santorelli wedding
current_scene_description_before: The study is paneled in dark wood, heavy curtains drawn against the late afternoon sun. A crystal decanter of whiskey sits on a mahogany desk, and the faint scent of cigar smoke lingers. Gio Bellante stands in the doorway, adjusting his cufflinks, while the distant murmur of the wedding reception filters through the walls.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio bursts back through the study doorway, shirt bloodied, revolver in hand. The band outside stumbles to a halt. He scans the room — guests frozen, the Don's cat darting under the desk.] Nobody leaves. [He kicks the door shut behind him.] Whoever sent that mask knew I'd be at this wedding. That means they're in this room. Or they're on the guest list. [His eyes land on the Don's glass of grappa, still sweating on the desk.] So let's start with who poured the drinks.
current_scene_name_before: Bellante family study, after the Santorelli wedding
current_scene_description_before: The study is paneled in dark wood, heavy curtains drawn against the late afternoon sun. A crystal decanter of whiskey sits on a mahogany desk, and the faint scent of cigar smoke lingers. Gio Bellante stands in the doorway, adjusting his cufflinks, while the distant murmur of the wedding reception filters through the walls.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio pulls out his phone, dials. It rings twice. He doesn't wait for a greeting.] Vito. Check the warehouse log. Who signed for a catering order going to the Santorelli wedding this morning? [He listens. His knuckles go white on the phone.] Describe him. [Another pause. Then a cold, quiet laugh.] Vincenzo. My own childhood friend. [He hangs up, stares at the delivery slip in his hand.] Vince Costa packed the box. Vince is in the hospital — the same hospital Luca's sitting at. [He pockets the slip.] Which means Vince either knew I'd be wearing this vest... or he didn't care whether I lived or died.
current_scene_name_before: Bellante family study, after the Santorelli wedding
current_scene_description_before: The study is paneled in dark wood, heavy curtains drawn against the late afternoon sun. A crystal decanter of whiskey sits on a mahogany desk, and the faint scent of cigar smoke lingers. Gio Bellante stands in the doorway, adjusting his cufflinks, while the distant murmur of the wedding reception filters through the walls.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio walks back into the study, the delivery slip held up like a warrant. He tosses it onto the Don's desk, next to the untouched grappa.] The bottle came from my warehouse. Packed by Vincent Costa — your consigliere's boy. Childhood friend of mine. [He leans on the desk, meeting the Don's eyes.] The same Vince Costa who's sitting in a hospital bed right now, four blocks from your son Luca. I don't believe in coincidences, Don Santorelli. Do you?
current_scene_name_before: Bellante family study, after the Santorelli wedding
current_scene_description_before: The study is paneled in dark wood, heavy curtains drawn against the late afternoon sun. A crystal decanter of whiskey sits on a mahogany desk, and the faint scent of cigar smoke lingers. Gio Bellante stands in the doorway, adjusting his cufflinks, while the distant murmur of the wedding reception filters through the walls.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio pushes off the desk, holsters his revolver, and buttons his bloodied jacket.] I am going to the hospital. Not to kill Vince — although God knows I got the right. I am going to ask him one question, in person, while he still has the chance to answer it like a man. [He stops at the door, looks back.] If he gives me the right answer, I will bring him here myself so he can explain it to you. If he gives me the wrong one... [He taps his chest where the bullets hit.] ...then this wedding will have one more corpse before the cake is cut.
current_scene_name_before: Bellante family study, after the Santorelli wedding
current_scene_description_before: The study is paneled in dark wood, heavy curtains drawn against the late afternoon sun. A crystal decanter of whiskey sits on a mahogany desk, and the faint scent of cigar smoke lingers. Gio Bellante stands in the doorway, adjusting his cufflinks, while the distant murmur of the wedding reception filters through the walls.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio pushes through the hospital's emergency room doors, blood still seeping through his shirt. The nurse at the desk opens her mouth to speak. He holds up his revolver — not aimed, just visible — and keeps walking.] Vincent Costa. Which room. [She points. He takes the stairs. Third floor. Room 317. The door is ajar. He pauses outside, listening. Then he pushes it open with two fingers.] Vince. You look good for a dead man. [He steps inside and closes the door behind him.]
new_scene_name: Hospital third floor corridor outside room 317
new_scene_description: The fluorescent lights buzz overhead, casting a sterile glow on the linoleum floor. The air smells of antiseptic and stale coffee. Gio Bellante stands with his back to the closed door of room 317, his revolver still in hand, blood darkening his shirt. The distant beeps of monitors echo down the quiet hall.
current_scene_name_before: Bellante family study, after the Santorelli wedding
current_scene_description_before: The study is paneled in dark wood, heavy curtains drawn against the late afternoon sun. A crystal decanter of whiskey sits on a mahogany desk, and the faint scent of cigar smoke lingers. Gio Bellante stands in the doorway, adjusting his cufflinks, while the distant murmur of the wedding reception filters through the walls.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio stands at the foot of the bed. Vince is propped against pillows, a cast on his arm, a bruise flowering across his jaw. He sees Gio's bloodied shirt and goes pale.] You want to tell me about the catering order, Vincenzo? Or should I start guessing? [He pulls out the flattened slug from his vest pocket and drops it on the bedside table. It clinks against the water pitcher.] That came from your package. The one addressed to the Don. Under my company's name. [He waits. The room hums with fluorescent light.] I'll give you three seconds to convince me you didn't know what was in that box.
new_scene_name: Inside room 317, Vince's hospital bed
new_scene_description: The room is small and sterile, with a single bed pushed against the wall. Vince sits propped up against pillows, a cast on his arm and a bruise on his jaw. The fluorescent light hums overhead. Gio stands at the foot of the bed, his bloodied shirt stark against the white sheets. He drops a flattened slug onto the bedside table, where it clinks against the water pitcher.
current_scene_name_before: Hospital third floor corridor outside room 317
current_scene_description_before: The fluorescent lights buzz overhead, casting a sterile glow on the linoleum floor. The air smells of antiseptic and stale coffee. Gio Bellante stands with his back to the closed door of room 317, his revolver still in hand, blood darkening his shirt. The distant beeps of monitors echo down the quiet hall.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca pockets the phone and starts walking — not toward the hospital, but away from it. He pulls the folder out, flips to Vince Costa's name on the list, and tears the page out.] Gio's going to the hospital to ask Vince one question. I know what Vince will answer. The question is whether Gio will believe him — or pull the trigger anyway. [He tucks the page into his jacket and picks up his pace.] I need to get there before he does. Not to stop the shooting. To make sure the right person gets the bullet.
new_scene_name: A dark street corner near the hospital
new_scene_description: Luca strides away from the hospital's glow, the folder's torn page crumpled in his pocket. Streetlights cast long shadows on the cracked asphalt. The distant wail of a siren fades as he quickens his pace toward the entrance, breath fogging in the cold night air.
current_scene_name_before: Inside room 317, Vince's hospital bed
current_scene_description_before: The room is small and sterile, with a single bed pushed against the wall. Vince sits propped up against pillows, a cast on his arm and a bruise on his jaw. The fluorescent light hums overhead. Gio stands at the foot of the bed, his bloodied shirt stark against the white sheets. He drops a flattened slug onto the bedside table, where it clinks against the water pitcher.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Vince's voice breaks again, barely a whisper.] I don't know his real name. He called himself 'the Broker.' Paid in cash. Unmarked bills. Said he represented interests that wanted the Don out of the way — quietly, before the wedding cake was cut. [Gio turns back, slowly. His face is unreadable.] Paid you how much? [Vince looks at his cast, ashamed.] Enough to leave town. Start over. [Gio nods, pulls the delivery slip from his pocket, and lays it on the bed.] Then whoever this Broker is, he knows my warehouse, my schedule, and my family's letterhead. That's not an outsider. That's someone inside the five families. [He opens the door.] Stay here. If you're lying to me, I'll be back. If you're telling the truth, the Broker will find you before I do — and you'll wish I'd pulled the trigger.
current_scene_name_before: A dark street corner near the hospital
current_scene_description_before: Luca strides away from the hospital's glow, the folder's torn page crumpled in his pocket. Streetlights cast long shadows on the cracked asphalt. The distant wail of a siren fades as he quickens his pace toward the entrance, breath fogging in the cold night air.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio steps into the hallway, closes Vince's door behind him, and stops cold. Luca Moretti is standing ten feet away, folder under one arm, hand inside his jacket near the revolver. The fluorescent lights buzz between them. Gio's bloodied shirt is the only color in the corridor.] Luca. [He holds up his empty palms, then slowly reaches into his vest and pulls out the two flattened slugs, holding them up like a peace offering.] I already got the answers. Vince cracked. Told me about someone called 'the Broker' — paid in cash, knew my schedule. Said the bottle was meant for your father. [He tosses one slug to Luca. It clatters on the linoleum between them.] I'm not here to shoot anyone. I'm here to find out who used my family's name to try to kill yours.
new_scene_name: Hospital hallway outside Vince's room
new_scene_description: The fluorescent lights buzz overhead, casting a sterile glow on the linoleum floor. Gio stands with his back to Vince's door, bloodied shirt stark against the white walls. Luca is ten feet away, hand near his revolver, folder under his arm. A single slug lies on the floor between them, glinting under the harsh light.
current_scene_name_before: A dark street corner near the hospital
current_scene_description_before: Luca strides away from the hospital's glow, the folder's torn page crumpled in his pocket. Streetlights cast long shadows on the cracked asphalt. The distant wail of a siren fades as he quickens his pace toward the entrance, breath fogging in the cold night air.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio watches Luca's face — the flicker of a man deciding whether to draw. He takes a slow step forward, hands still open.] You've got a folder full of names. I've got a warehouse full of records. Together we find the Broker before he finds Vince and makes him a loose end. [He nods toward the slug on the floor.] Or you can shoot me in a hospital hallway and explain to your father why you killed the only man who brought you proof. Your call, Luca.
current_scene_name_before: Hospital hallway outside Vince's room
current_scene_description_before: The fluorescent lights buzz overhead, casting a sterile glow on the linoleum floor. Gio stands with his back to Vince's door, bloodied shirt stark against the white walls. Luca is ten feet away, hand near his revolver, folder under his arm. A single slug lies on the floor between them, glinting under the harsh light.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Luca doesn't draw. Doesn't speak. Just stands there, the folder under his arm, weighing the slug at his feet. Gio reads the silence and nods once.] That's what I thought. [He turns and starts down the hall, then stops.] My warehouse. One hour. Bring your folder and that revolver — the Broker's inside those names somewhere. If I'm not there by then, it means the mask found me before I found him. [He doesn't look back.] Don't wait.
current_scene_name_before: Hospital hallway outside Vince's room
current_scene_description_before: The fluorescent lights buzz overhead, casting a sterile glow on the linoleum floor. Gio stands with his back to Vince's door, bloodied shirt stark against the white walls. Luca is ten feet away, hand near his revolver, folder under his arm. A single slug lies on the floor between them, glinting under the harsh light.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio's sedan pulls up outside Bellante Imports — a squat brick building in the industrial district. He cuts the engine, sits for a moment, watching the loading bay. No movement. No unfamiliar cars. He gets out, key in hand, and unlocks the side door. Inside, rows of pallets stacked with olive oil crates. The air smells of oregano and dust. He walks to the back office, flips on the light, and pulls the shipping ledger from the safe.] Let's see who else signed for a delivery this week. [He starts turning pages, his bloodied shirt darkening the edge of the desk.]
new_scene_name: Bellante Imports back office, night
new_scene_description: A cramped office with a single desk and a floor safe left open. The air smells of oregano and dust from the stacked olive oil crates outside. Gio sits at the desk, blood still seeping through his shirt, flipping through a leather-bound shipping ledger under the harsh desk lamp.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio's finger stops on an entry. Three weeks ago. A special order of Santorelli-label olive oil — exported to a distributor that doesn't exist. Signed out by the night foreman. A man Gio personally hired six months ago on Vince's recommendation. He closes the ledger, pulls out his phone, and pulls up the foreman's file.] Marco Bianchi. Hired on Vince's word. Works the night shift alone. Has a key to every lock in this building. [He sets the phone down and looks at the darkened warehouse floor.] And he's been standing in the shadows behind me for the last thirty seconds. [He doesn't turn around.] You want to tell me who you're really working for, Marco? Or should I guess?
current_scene_name_before: Bellante Imports back office, night
current_scene_description_before: A cramped office with a single desk and a floor safe left open. The air smells of oregano and dust from the stacked olive oil crates outside. Gio sits at the desk, blood still seeping through his shirt, flipping through a leather-bound shipping ledger under the harsh desk lamp.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio hears the click of a safety catch. He doesn't flinch.] Marco steps into the dim light — a crowbar in one hand, a pistol in the other. His face is pale. His hands are steady.] 'The Broker said you'd come here before the wedding.' [Gio slowly turns in his chair, arms resting on the ledger.] Did he also tell you I'd be wearing a vest? [Marco's eyes flick to Gio's bloodied shirt, confused. Gio uses the half-second to sweep the desk lamp into Marco's face — glass shatters, Marco stumbles back, and Gio is on him, driving the heel of his palm into the man's wrist until the pistol hits the concrete.] The Broker. Name. Now.
current_scene_name_before: Bellante Imports back office, night
current_scene_description_before: A cramped office with a single desk and a floor safe left open. The air smells of oregano and dust from the stacked olive oil crates outside. Gio sits at the desk, blood still seeping through his shirt, flipping through a leather-bound shipping ledger under the harsh desk lamp.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Marco gasps, blood running from a cut above his eye. He laughs — a broken, desperate sound.] You think I know his name? He called me. Told me where to be. Paid in envelopes left under my windshield wiper. [Gio twists his arm. Marco cries out.] I never saw his face! But the envelopes... they had a return address stamped on the flap. A PO box in Little Italy. [Gio holds for a beat, then releases him.] Number. [Marco coughs.] 479. Under the name 'Rossi.' [Gio stands, picks up Marco's pistol, and tucks it into his waistband.] You're going to walk out of here, get in your car, and drive to Pennsylvania. If I ever see you again, I'll put the second bullet where the first one was meant to go. [Marco scrambles out. Gio pulls out his phone, dials.] Vito. I need everything you can find on a PO box 479, Little Italy, under Rossi.
current_scene_name_before: Bellante Imports back office, night
current_scene_description_before: A cramped office with a single desk and a floor safe left open. The air smells of oregano and dust from the stacked olive oil crates outside. Gio sits at the desk, blood still seeping through his shirt, flipping through a leather-bound shipping ledger under the harsh desk lamp.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio stands alone in the warehouse. The only sound is the buzz of the fluorescent light he just shattered. He picks up Marco's crowbar, weighs it in his hand, then sets it on the desk next to the ledger. His phone buzzes — Vito's reply.] 'Box 479 was rented six weeks ago. Paid in cash for a year. No ID required — they used a notarized letter from a 'Michael Rossi.' The letterhead matches a law firm on Canal Street. Firm dissolved three years ago. [Gio reads it twice. He sets the phone down.] A ghost address and a dead law firm. That's not a Broker. That's someone who's been planning this longer than Vince or Marco have been alive. [He looks at the door, listening for Luca's footsteps in the lot.]
new_scene_name: Bellante Imports warehouse, night
new_scene_description: A vast, cavernous warehouse stacked with olive oil crates and pallets. One fluorescent light fixture hangs shattered, its glass shards scattered on the concrete floor. Gio stands alone near a desk that's been pulled out from the back office, the crowbar lying on it next to a shipping ledger. The hum of the remaining lights echoes in the silence.
current_scene_name_before: Bellante Imports back office, night
current_scene_description_before: A cramped office with a single desk and a floor safe left open. The air smells of oregano and dust from the stacked olive oil crates outside. Gio sits at the desk, blood still seeping through his shirt, flipping through a leather-bound shipping ledger under the harsh desk lamp.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Headlights sweep across the warehouse wall. Tires on gravel. A car door opens, then closes — one person. No backup. Gio picks up Marco's pistol from his waistband, chambers a round, and steps out of the office into the shadows between the olive oil pallets. The side door creaks open. A silhouette fills the frame — folder in one hand, revolver in the other.] You're late, Luca. [Gio steps out from behind a pallet, arms loose at his sides.] I was starting to think the Broker got to you before I did.
current_scene_name_before: Bellante Imports warehouse, night
current_scene_description_before: A vast, cavernous warehouse stacked with olive oil crates and pallets. One fluorescent light fixture hangs shattered, its glass shards scattered on the concrete floor. Gio stands alone near a desk that's been pulled out from the back office, the crowbar lying on it next to a shipping ledger. The hum of the remaining lights echoes in the silence.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio gestures with his chin toward the office. The ledger is still open on the desk, the crowbar resting against it.] I've got a name — 'the Broker' — a PO box under a dead alias, and a foreman on his way to Pennsylvania. What's in that folder that I don't already know? [He walks to the desk, turns the ledger around so Luca can read it.] Santorelli-label olive oil, shipped from my warehouse to a fake distributor, signed by a man Vince Costa vouched for. Three weeks before the wedding. [He looks up.] Your father was the target. I was just the delivery system.
current_scene_name_before: Bellante Imports warehouse, night
current_scene_description_before: A vast, cavernous warehouse stacked with olive oil crates and pallets. One fluorescent light fixture hangs shattered, its glass shards scattered on the concrete floor. Gio stands alone near a desk that's been pulled out from the back office, the crowbar lying on it next to a shipping ledger. The hum of the remaining lights echoes in the silence.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio watches Luca's face in the dim light — the same guarded look he's seen on every wiseguy walking into a deal he doesn't trust.] You're still holding that folder like it's glued to your hand. [He taps the open ledger.] I just handed you my whole operation. Names, dates, shipments. If you walk out of here without showing me what's in that folder, then we're both working blind. And the Broker's not working blind. [He leans against the desk, arms crossed.] Open it, Luca. Or we're done before we start.
current_scene_name_before: Bellante Imports warehouse, night
current_scene_description_before: A vast, cavernous warehouse stacked with olive oil crates and pallets. One fluorescent light fixture hangs shattered, its glass shards scattered on the concrete floor. Gio stands alone near a desk that's been pulled out from the back office, the crowbar lying on it next to a shipping ledger. The hum of the remaining lights echoes in the silence.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [The silence stretches. Luca's hand stays on the folder but doesn't open it. Gio pushes off the desk, done waiting.] Fine. Keep your secrets. But I'm not sitting here while the Broker gets further ahead. [He grabs Marco's pistol off the desk, tucks it in his waistband, and heads for the door.] I'm going to Little Italy to stake out that PO box. You know where to find me if you change your mind. [He stops at the door, hand on the frame.] One piece of advice, free of charge: whoever's in that folder, cross-check the names against anyone who's been to your father's study in the last month. The Broker knew the layout. Knew where the Don sits. Knew the grappa would be on the desk. That's not a ghost — that's a guest.

Gio steps out of a car and scans the block, the pistol tucked in his waistband hidden by his jacket. The distant sound of a jazz record drifts from an open window above.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio parks his sedan a block from the post office. He cuts the engine, slumps low in the seat, and watches the entrance through the gaps in a folded newspaper. Twenty minutes pass. Then forty. The blood on his shirt has dried to a rust-brown crust. At the hour mark, a black town car pulls up. No plate on the front. The back door opens and a man in a tailored suit steps out — expensive shoes, no hat, carrying a small courier envelope. He walks straight inside. Gio's hand moves to the door handle.] Let's see who Rossi sends to check his mail.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio slides out of the sedan, Marco's pistol tucked at his hip. He crosses the street at a walk — not fast, not slow — and pushes through the post office door just as the suited man is sliding a key into PO box 479. The man hears the bell, turns. Mid-fifties. Silver at the temples. A face Gio recognizes from a dozen family funerals and one very specific wedding reception.] You're Vincenzo Marino's tailor. [The man's hand freezes on the box key. Gio steps closer, voice low.] You measure the Don's consigliere for suits. Which means you've been in his study. You've seen where he sits. You know which glass is his. [Gio's hand settles on the pistol grip.] The Broker didn't need a ghost. He needed a tailor with access.
new_scene_name: Post office interior near PO box 479, night
new_scene_description: The post office is small and fluorescent-lit, with a row of mailboxes along the back wall. The suited man stands frozen at PO box 479, keys in hand. Gio steps through the door, hand on his pistol, closing the distance between them. The air smells of stale paper and dust. The man's silver hair catches the harsh light as he turns to face the intruder.
current_scene_name_before: Gio's sedan parked a block from the post office, night
current_scene_description_before: The sedan's interior is dark and stale with tension. Gio slumps low in the driver's seat, a newspaper tented in front of his face. Through the gaps, he watches the postal shop's flickering neon. Across the street, a black town car idles, no front plate. A man in a tailored suit emerges, carrying a courier envelope, and walks into the postal shop. The dried blood on Gio's shirt catches the dim glow of a distant streetlamp.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [The tailor's face drains of color. The courier envelope trembles in his grip.] I don't know anyone called the Broker. I just deliver the mail. Once a week. An envelope appears under my shop door, I bring it here, I leave whatever's in the box on my counter. [Gio studies him — the genuine shake in his hands, the sweat on his collar, the way a career tailor doesn't know how to lie to a man with a gun.] Who leaves the envelope? [The tailor shakes his head.] It's always there Monday morning. No face. No note. Just cash and an address. [Gio pulls the envelope from the tailor's hand, tears it open. Inside: a single key. Brass. Engraved with a number. Not a PO box — a safety deposit box. He turns it over. The bank name is stamped on the back: First Mercantile, Canal Street.] You just became very useful. [He pockets the key.] Now get in the car. We're going to open a box.
new_scene_name: First Mercantile Bank vault, Canal Street
new_scene_description: The vault is a cold chamber of polished steel and fluorescent light. Rows of safety deposit boxes line the walls, each numbered in brass. Gio stands before box 479, the key in his hand, the tailor trembling beside him. The air smells of metal and old paper.
current_scene_name_before: Post office interior near PO box 479, night
current_scene_description_before: The post office is small and fluorescent-lit, with a row of mailboxes along the back wall. The suited man stands frozen at PO box 479, keys in hand. Gio steps through the door, hand on his pistol, closing the distance between them. The air smells of stale paper and dust. The man's silver hair catches the harsh light as he turns to face the intruder.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [Luca gets the call while standing alone in the warehouse — Gio's voice crackling with the revelation. He listens, says nothing, then hangs up. He looks at the folder in his hand, then at the open ledger on Gio's desk.] A tailor. The man who measures my father for his suits. Who's been in the study a dozen times. Who knows every room, every glass, every habit. [He closes the folder, tucks it under his arm, and walks toward the door.] Vince Marino's tailor. Which means the Marino family has been in on this from the start. [He stops at the door, hand on the frame.] And I just let Gio walk into that post office alone.
new_scene_name: Bellante Imports warehouse, Gio's desk
new_scene_description: The warehouse is dim and cavernous, filled with crates and the hum of a distant generator. Dust motes hang in the air above Gio's cluttered desk, where a ledger lies open beside a telephone receiver still warm from the call. A single fluorescent strip buzzes overhead, casting harsh shadows.
current_scene_name_before: First Mercantile Bank vault, Canal Street
current_scene_description_before: The vault is a cold chamber of polished steel and fluorescent light. Rows of safety deposit boxes line the walls, each numbered in brass. Gio stands before box 479, the key in his hand, the tailor trembling beside him. The air smells of metal and old paper.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio walks the tailor into First Mercantile like a businessman and his associate. The tailor's hands shake as he signs the access log. The bank manager barely glances at them — a bloodied man and a trembling tailor are none of his business on a Tuesday. The safety deposit box is in the vault, second row from the top. Number 479. Gio inserts the key, turns it. The drawer slides out. Inside: a stack of cash — bearer bonds, untraceable — a burner phone, and a single photograph. Gio picks up the photo. His face goes still. It's a picture of his sister Alessia, leaving the bakery on Mulberry, taken three days ago. She's smiling. She doesn't know she's being watched. [Gio's voice is barely a whisper.] The Broker knows where my sister buys her bread.
new_scene_name: First Mercantile Bank vault, safety deposit box row
new_scene_description: The vault is cold and silent, lined with polished steel boxes. Fluorescent lights hum overhead, reflecting off the metal. Gio stands with the tailor, who trembles beside him. The safety deposit box drawer is open, revealing cash, a burner phone, and a photograph. Gio holds the photo, his expression unreadable.
current_scene_name_before: Bellante Imports warehouse, Gio's desk
current_scene_description_before: The warehouse is dim and cavernous, filled with crates and the hum of a distant generator. Dust motes hang in the air above Gio's cluttered desk, where a ledger lies open beside a telephone receiver still warm from the call. A single fluorescent strip buzzes overhead, casting harsh shadows.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio pockets the photo without a word. He takes the burner phone, the bonds, and the key. The tailor stands frozen by the open vault.] You're going to walk out of here, go back to your shop, and keep doing exactly what you've been doing. When the next envelope appears under your door, you call this number. [Gio pulls a pen from the counter, writes his number on the tailor's cuff.] And you don't tell anyone — not Vincenzo, not the police, not your priest — that a bloodied man asked you to. [He turns and walks out of the bank. The burner phone feels heavy in his pocket. He pulls it out, stares at it. Then he dials his sister — straight to voicemail.] Alessia, it's me. Don't go to the bakery today. Stay home. Lock the door. I'll explain when I get there. [He hangs up, gets in the sedan, and points it toward Mulberry Street.]
new_scene_name: Gio's sedan, heading toward Mulberry Street
new_scene_description: The sedan's interior is dark and quiet, the only sound the hum of tires on asphalt. Gio grips the steering wheel, the burner phone now discarded on the passenger seat. The streets of the city slide by under the glow of streetlights. He's alone, the weight of the heist still hanging in the air.
current_scene_name_before: First Mercantile Bank vault, safety deposit box row
current_scene_description_before: The vault is cold and silent, lined with polished steel boxes. Fluorescent lights hum overhead, reflecting off the metal. Gio stands with the tailor, who trembles beside him. The safety deposit box drawer is open, revealing cash, a burner phone, and a photograph. Gio holds the photo, his expression unreadable.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio's phone buzzes. Luca's voice cuts through the sedan's cabin. He listens, then lets out a low breath — almost a laugh.] Too late. I already opened it. Picture of my sister, taken three days ago. Bearer bonds and a burner phone that's probably got one number in it. [He glances at the phone on the passenger seat.] I'm headed to Mulberry to make sure Alessia's safe. After that, I'm going to find Vincenzo Marino and ask him why his tailor's been running the Broker's mail. [He pauses at a red light, looks at the photo of Alessia taped to his dashboard.] You want to help? Meet me at the bakery. We'll pay the consigliere a visit together.
current_scene_name_before: Gio's sedan, heading toward Mulberry Street
current_scene_description_before: The sedan's interior is dark and quiet, the only sound the hum of tires on asphalt. Gio grips the steering wheel, the burner phone now discarded on the passenger seat. The streets of the city slide by under the glow of streetlights. He's alone, the weight of the heist still hanging in the air.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio pulls up to the bakery on Mulberry. The street is quiet — too quiet for a Tuesday morning. He kills the engine, sits for a moment counting the parked cars, the faces in windows, the delivery trucks that don't belong. Nothing obvious. He gets out, walks to the front door of the apartment above the bakery. He knocks — three quick raps, pause, two more. The family signal. The peephole darkens, then the locks slide open. Alessia stands in the doorway, flour on her apron, a rolling pin in her fist like a club. She looks at his bloodied shirt, his drawn face, and doesn't ask. She just steps aside.] Get inside. You look like hell. [Gio crosses the threshold and locks the door behind him.] I've had worse days. But not many.
new_scene_name: Alessia's apartment above the bakery, morning
new_scene_description: The apartment smells of yeast and warm dough. Morning light filters through dusty curtains, illuminating a small kitchen table covered in flour. Alessia stands in the doorway, rolling pin still in hand, her apron dusted white. Gio has just entered, locking the door behind him. The space is cramped but lived-in, a home that doubles as a fortress.
current_scene_name_before: Gio's sedan, heading toward Mulberry Street
current_scene_description_before: The sedan's interior is dark and quiet, the only sound the hum of tires on asphalt. Gio grips the steering wheel, the burner phone now discarded on the passenger seat. The streets of the city slide by under the glow of streetlights. He's alone, the weight of the heist still hanging in the air.

speaker: Luca Moretti
line_text: [A knock at the door — three quick raps, pause, two more. The family signal. Gio's hand goes to his pistol. Alessia's goes to the rolling pin. Gio checks the peephole and lets out a breath. He opens the door. Luca stands in the doorway, folder under one arm, the revolver visible at his hip.] You teach your sister the family knock? [Gio steps aside, almost smiling.] She taught me. [Luca steps inside, nods at Alessia, then looks at Gio.] Vincenzo Marino's tailor is the Broker's mailman. Which means Vincenzo is either the Broker — or he's being set up just like us. Either way, we find out tonight. [He drops the folder on the table.] It's all in here. Every name my father trusted. Let's find out which one sold him out.
current_scene_name_before: Alessia's apartment above the bakery, morning
current_scene_description_before: The apartment smells of yeast and warm dough. Morning light filters through dusty curtains, illuminating a small kitchen table covered in flour. Alessia stands in the doorway, rolling pin still in hand, her apron dusted white. Gio has just entered, locking the door behind him. The space is cramped but lived-in, a home that doubles as a fortress.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio moves to the table, pulls the folder toward him. Alessia leans over his shoulder as he flips it open — pages of names, dates, transactions, photographs. He stops on a face he knows well.] Vincenzo Marino. Your father's consigliere for twenty years. Sits at the Don's right hand at every dinner. Knows every deal, every weakness, every safe house. [He slides the photo across the table to Luca.] And his tailor was running the Broker's mail. That's not a coincidence. That's a pipeline. [He looks up from the folder, meets Luca's eyes.] The question isn't whether Vincenzo sold your father out. The question is who he sold him to — and whether we hit Vincenzo tonight or follow the money up the chain.

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio moves to the door and locks it, then faces Alessia and Luca] "We have three hours before Vincenzo realizes the tailor's been taken. That's how long we have to find out who he's running to—and cut the head off before it gives the order."

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio pulls out his phone and slides it across the table to Alessia] "Call her now. And Alessia—if she hesitates, if she sounds scared before you even tell her about the raid, we know the Broker's already warned them. Then we burn the whole thing and start over."

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio steps away from the table, moving toward the back room of the bakery] "However she answered, we have maybe an hour before Vincenzo realizes something's wrong. Luca—you and I are going to sit with Mateo and go through every contact Vincenzo's made in the last month. Every call, every meeting. Someone warned Rosa. That someone is on that list."

line_text: [Gio enters the back room where Mateo is waiting with a ledger already open on the table] "Mateo called Vincenzo twice in the last week. Once right after the wedding photos started circulating. Who does Vincenzo call when he's scared?"

line_text: [Gio steps closer, his voice dropping to a deadly whisper] "Vito, get Luca and search the kitchens and gardens—Vincenzo won't leave without his insurance. Find those bonds."

current_scene_description_before: A narrow room behind the main bakery floor, cluttered with stacks of paper, a dusty ledger, and a single bulb hanging from the ceiling. speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [The door opens. Gio's hand goes to his pistol, but recognition flickers—then suspicion] "You. How did you get past—"
current_scene_name_before: Back room of the bakery, cramped office
current_scene_description_before: A narrow room behind the main bakery floor, cluttered with stacks of paper, a dusty ledger, and a single bulb hanging from the ceiling. The yeasty warmth of the ovens seeps through the walls. Gio enters, pulling a chair to a small desk where Mateo already sits, papers spread before him. The door clicks shut, muffling the distant street noise.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text:
[Vito steps forward slowly, hands open, each step measured] "Gio. The pistol comes down. Not because Vincenzo paid him—because I say so, and you remember who I am to this family."

[He does not look at Mateo. His gaze stays on Gio, and on the door behind him] "Vincenzo spent his money badly. And Mateo..."

[He pauses, then turns just slightly, addressing the hallway where the masked figure would be] "...someone else has already collected."
current_scene_name_before: Back room of the bakery, cramped office
current_scene_description_before: A narrow room behind the main bakery floor, cluttered with stacks of paper, a dusty ledger, and a single bulb hanging from the ceiling. The yeasty warmth of the ovens seeps through the walls. Gio enters, pulling a chair to a small desk where Mateo already sits, papers spread before him. The door clicks shut, muffling the distant street noise.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text:
[Vito does not step aside. He does not cry out. He simply closes his eyes—just for a moment—as if accepting an old prayer.

The gun rises.

Then fires.

Gio falls. Blood on marble. The cat on the Don's desk does not wake.]

[Vito opens his eyes. Looks at the mask. No surprise. Only duty.]

"You know what you've done."
new_scene_name: Don's office, marble floor, a cat on the desk
new_scene_description: A spacious marble-floored office, lit by a single green-shaded lamp. The Don's desk is heavy mahogany, with a sleeping tabby curled on the corner. Gio lies motionless on the marble, a dark pool spreading beneath him. Vito stands, mask in hand, the gun still raised.
current_scene_name_before: Back room of the bakery, cramped office
current_scene_description_before: A narrow room behind the main bakery floor, cluttered with stacks of paper, a dusty ledger, and a single bulb hanging from the ceiling. The yeasty warmth of the ovens seeps through the walls. Gio enters, pulling a chair to a small desk where Mateo already sits, papers spread before him. The door clicks shut, muffling the distant street noise.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text:
[Vito steps around Gio's body with the measured grace of a man who has buried many secrets. He moves toward the masked figure, hand open—not in greeting, but in the gesture of a lawyer offering resolution.]

"The mask comes off. Now we both understand what this cost."

[Outside, the brass band plays on. The wedding continues. No one knows yet that the family has just been rewritten.]

"We have very little time."
new_scene_name: Don's office, moments after the shooting, wedding music outside
new_scene_description: The marble-floored office is dimly lit by a green-shaded lamp. Gio's body lies still on the floor, blood pooling. Vito steps toward the masked figure, hand extended. Through the window, the muffled sound of a brass band and wedding revelry drifts in, oblivious to the violence within.
current_scene_name_before: Don's office, marble floor, a cat on the desk
current_scene_description_before: A spacious marble-floored office, lit by a single green-shaded lamp. The Don's desk is heavy mahogany, with a sleeping tabby curled on the corner. Gio lies motionless on the marble, a dark pool spreading beneath him. Vito stands, mask in hand, the gun still raised.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text:
[Luca's hand trembles. His eyes move from Gio to the masked figure to Vito, trying to assemble the pieces into something that makes sense. He cannot.]

"Vito—what have you—"

[Vito raises one hand—not in anger, but in the way he might counsel a frightened grandchild.]

"Your father chose this. Before the illness took him. Before everything fell apart."

[He steps closer to Luca, his voice dropping to the register of absolute certainty.]

"The question now is whether you have the spine to let it be finished."
current_scene_name_before: Don's office, moments after the shooting, wedding music outside
current_scene_description_before: The marble-floored office is dimly lit by a green-shaded lamp. Gio's body lies still on the floor, blood pooling. Vito steps toward the masked figure, hand extended. Through the window, the muffled sound of a brass band and wedding revelry drifts in, oblivious to the violence within.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text:
[Luca's mouth opens. Closes. His eyes find the mask in Vito's hand. In that moment, something in the young man solidifies—not strength exactly, but the beginning of understanding that some choices have already been made.]

"Gio's sleeping," Luca whispers.

[Vito nods. One gesture that contains years of knowledge, decades of silence.]

"Go now. The Don's business is concluded."

[Luca turns toward the door, stops at Gio's body. For one moment, he kneels—not in prayer, but in the acknowledgment that something irretrievable has been lost. Then he rises and leaves.]

[Vito stands alone with the body and the masked figure. The brass band outside strikes up a new song. The wedding celebrates on, unknowing.]

"Now," Vito says softly to the figure in the mask, "we disappear him."
current_scene_name_before: Don's office, moments after the shooting, wedding music outside
current_scene_description_before: The marble-floored office is dimly lit by a green-shaded lamp. Gio's body lies still on the floor, blood pooling. Vito steps toward the masked figure, hand extended. Through the window, the muffled sound of a brass band and wedding revelry drifts in, oblivious to the violence within.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text:
[Vito moves to the door, listens. The hallway is empty. The smell of roasted meat drifts up from downstairs—the feast continues. He turns back to the body, to the mask on the floor.]

"The envelope. The Don's final instructions. It's hidden where only I know."

[He kneels beside Gio, not in prayer, but in the work of understanding. His hand finds Gio's pulse—nothing. Just meat now. Just a problem to be solved.]

"When this is finished, there will be no trace. No body. No Gio. Only the fact of his absence."

[He stands, turns toward where the masked figure waits.]

"And a family that knows exactly who runs it now."
current_scene_name_before: Don's office, moments after the shooting, wedding music outside
current_scene_description_before: The marble-floored office is dimly lit by a green-shaded lamp. Gio's body lies still on the floor, blood pooling. Vito steps toward the masked figure, hand extended. Through the window, the muffled sound of a brass band and wedding revelry drifts in, oblivious to the violence within.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text:
[Vito moves to the desk—the Don's desk. The cat is still asleep, indifferent to blood. His hand finds the envelope taped beneath, in that dead space between the drawer and the frame. Forty years it has waited there.]

[He opens it. His eyes move across the Don's handwriting—each word a law, each pause a choice already made.]

[The masked shooter watches. Waiting for their role in what comes next.]

[Vito folds the letter slowly. When he turns, his face has changed—not with shock, but with the weight of inheritance.]

"The Don chose well," Vito says quietly. "Better than any of us knew."

[He looks directly at the masked figure.]

"He chose you, didn't he?"
current_scene_name_before: Don's office, moments after the shooting, wedding music outside
current_scene_description_before: The marble-floored office is dimly lit by a green-shaded lamp. Gio's body lies still on the floor, blood pooling. Vito steps toward the masked figure, hand extended. Through the window, the muffled sound of a brass band and wedding revelry drifts in, oblivious to the violence within.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text:
[Vito moves to the mirror, adjusts his suit jacket. The blood is on his hands—metaphorically. He washes them in the basin, careful, methodical. Each finger cleaned with the precision of ritual.]

"When I go downstairs, the wedding continues. Gio had a moment of weakness, of fear. He ran rather than face his obligations."

[He dries his hands on a towel—white fabric turning faintly pink.]

"Vincenzo will want power. Let him have the appearance of it. The real authority is invisible."

[He turns to the figure one last time.]

"You'll leave from the garden. Do not return until the family has fractured enough that your return makes sense."

[The weight of decades of service settles into silence.]

"The Don chose well. Now live worthy of that choice."
current_scene_name_before: Don's office, moments after the shooting, wedding music outside
current_scene_description_before: The marble-floored office is dimly lit by a green-shaded lamp. Gio's body lies still on the floor, blood pooling. Vito steps toward the masked figure, hand extended. Through the window, the muffled sound of a brass band and wedding revelry drifts in, oblivious to the violence within.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text:
[The ballroom. Laughter, music, the clink of glasses. Bride and groom dancing under lights. Vito descends the grand staircase with the bearing of a man who has just concluded important business.]

[Vincenzo sees him first. His eyes—searching, questioning—want to know. Vito gives him nothing but a nod. Later. A conversation for later, in private.]

[Luca stands near the bar, pretending to enjoy wine. Their eyes meet for one moment. The boy is holding. The boy understands: what happened in that room must never leave that room.]

[Vito moves through the crowd with the gravity of a man carrying secrets. He finds Marcello, one of the old soldiers.]

"Gio had a crisis of nerve," Vito says quietly. "He's gone. We let him go. No one pursues."

[Marcello nods slowly. He does not ask questions. He knows Vito's words are law.]

"Tell the others."

new_scene_description: The grand ballroom glitters with chandeliers and festooned lights. A full brass band plays a lively tarantella. Couples dance on the polished floor while guests cluster at tables laden with champagne. Vito descends the staircase into the crowd, his expression unreadable, moving through smiling faces toward Marcello at the bar.
current_scene_name_before: Don's office, moments after the shooting, wedding music outside
current_scene_description_before: The marble-floored office is dimly lit by a green-shaded lamp. Gio's body lies still on the floor, blood pooling. Vito steps toward the masked figure, hand extended. Through the window, the muffled sound of a brass band and wedding revelry drifts in, oblivious to the violence within.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text:
[Later. The evening has worn on. The bride and groom, exhausted and happy, prepare to leave for their honeymoon. Vito stands apart, near a window overlooking the garden where the shooter has long since vanished.]

[He holds a small glass of wine. Does not drink from it. Merely turns it slowly, watching the light move through the liquid.]

[In his pocket: the Don's letter. The new order. The price that has been paid in blood to preserve power.]

[Luca approaches—hesitant, needing guidance.]

Luca: "Vito... what happens now?"

[Vito continues to look out at the garden. The music from the reception distant now, almost dreamlike.]

Vito: "Now? Now we honor the dead by never speaking of them. We honor the Don by living exactly as he has arranged. And we wait."

[He turns to face the young man.]

"The family has been reborn tonight. Whether we survive that rebirth depends on whether we remember: some knowledge is too dangerous to ever share."
current_scene_name_before: Wedding ballroom, evening reception
current_scene_description_before: The grand ballroom glitters with chandeliers and festooned lights. A full brass band plays a lively tarantella. Couples dance on the polished floor while guests cluster at tables laden with champagne. Vito descends the staircase into the crowd, his expression unreadable, moving through smiling faces toward Marcello at the bar.

speaker: Vito Roselli
line_text:
[Luca nods—not understanding fully, but beginning to. He turns and walks back into the reception, to the world of visible power and public authority.]

[Vito remains at the window. The night is deep now. The garden is empty. Gio is already gone—handled by trusted hands, by people who know the cost of loose ends.]

[He finally drinks the wine. It is cold, having sat untouched for hours. But he swallows it without reaction.]

[In the distance: the sound of the bride and groom's car departing for their new life. Their innocent new beginning.]

[Vito touches the letter in his pocket—the Don's final words. A roadmap for the future. Instructions for survival.]

[He closes his eyes.]

"So it is written," he whispers to no one. "So it shall be."

[When he opens them again, he is simply the old man at the wedding—patient, trusted, invisible.]

[The Consigliere. Forever the Consigliere.]

[And the family moves forward, knowing nothing of the blood that built their new foundation.]

[Curtain.]"

speaker: Giovanni 'Gio' Bellante
line_text: [Gio steps forward, his voice low and rough, the scar on his jaw pulling tight.] "Vito, you took a bullet for my old man in '89. So I'll ask you once, as a man who owes you blood: did you see the face under that mask, or are you just here to watch my family tear itself apart?"
