Selene stands at the edge of the basin, her silhouette stark against the glow, as the walls around her are etched with patterns that writhe and shift in the light.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Her grey eyes are fixed on the middle distance, seeing threads the others cannot.] I know what you are going to ask. Is there a way out? There is always a way out. But the door is not on this mountain. [She finally looks at the speaker, and her voice drops.] Theron carried something from the sea that the imposter does not want him to have. That tablet is the door. And the only reason the imposter has not taken it by force is that it cannot be taken. It must be given. We need to find Theron before it convinces someone to make the offer.
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Theron steps out of the shadow of the nearest obsidian pillar. He is closer to the spiral's heart now — close enough that the cold light illuminates the tablet in his hand, the cracked clay catching an answering glow from the depths.] The temple was not the first building on this spot. There was another. A smaller one. Older. Built by people who did not worship the gods because they had not yet forgotten what lived beneath them. [He taps the tablet.] This was written in that older temple. The priestess who carved it was not warning us about the imposter. She was telling us how to put it back to sleep.
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus steps through the crack into the obsidian chamber. The pillars rise like ribs around him, and he lets out a low whistle.] A quarantine. They walled this place off and called it a temple. [He runs a hand along one of the obsidian pillars, feeling the cold.] Which means whatever they were quarantining is still here. It never left. It's just been waiting for the seal to weaken. [He looks at Theron's silhouette in the dark.] And you already knew that, didn't you, old priest?
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene stops near the obsidian pillar where Theron stands, the tablet visible in his hand. She does not reach for it.] You said the temple was not the first building on this spot. [Her voice is low, deliberate.] I walked through a wave that was older than Poseidon. I carried a bone that was older than the sea god who ruled it. If there was a building here before the temple — who built it? And why did they bury it under a god's house?
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus eyes the tablet in Theron's hands, the cracked clay catching the dim light. He lets out a breath that's half amusement, half frustration.] You walked in here like a man following a dream, and now you pull out a tablet you've been carrying for thirty years. [He shakes his head slowly.] You weren't following a memory, old priest. You were following instructions.
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She takes a step into the crypt shadows, voice echoing back to the group.] The crack in the seal will widen. It will not stop. The question is whether we go down there together while we still have a choice — or wait until the floor opens beneath our feet and we fall whether we want to or not. I have seen that fall in my visions. There is no bottom to it.
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus tucks the dead caduceus into his belt and takes a step toward the spiral. The cold pulse from its heart washes over him, and he doesn't flinch.] Talk is cheap. I've been selling it my whole life. [He glances back at the others.] Whatever truth is at the center of that spiral — I've carried a dead god's charm long enough. Let's see if this place will take it.
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Theron kneels at the center of the spiral. The tablet fits perfectly into a depression in the obsidian floor — as if it was always meant to be there. The cold light surges, and the carvings around the tablet begin to glow, one by one, in sequence. Theron reads aloud, his voice low but carrying to every corner of the chamber.] 'The first gods were not born. They were remembered. And what is remembered cannot die — it can only be forgotten. The Olympians chose to forget. But the forgetting was buried here, and the buried thing learned to dream.' [He looks up. The light is steady now, pulsing in time with something beneath the floor.] The imposter is not a truth that learned to dig. It is the forgetting that learned to remember.
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene walks to the center of the spiral and kneels opposite Theron, the tablet glowing between them. The carvings pulse in sequence, and she feels the trident-mark responding — matching the rhythm.] This was never a prison. It was a lock. And the key was not the tablet — it was the truth we all had to carry separately until we arrived here together. [She looks at Theron.] You held the tablet. Melanthus held the question. Kassandra held the vision. Pyros held the fire. I held the bone. None of it meant anything alone.
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She steps fully into the crypt entrance now, her voice carrying back over her shoulder, already fading into shadow.] You feel it too — the cold that is not cold. The silence that is not empty. It knows we are here. It has been waiting for us since before any of us were born. Do not follow me because I am right. Follow me because if I go alone, I will not come back. And neither will the answer.
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus watches Selene and Theron kneel at the spiral's center, the tablet pulsing between them like a heartbeat. He speaks from the edge, the dead caduceus in his hand.] A door. Not a monster. Not a god. A door. [He turns the charm over in his fingers, then looks at Kassandra.] Then everything that's happened — the wave, the faceless thing, the blue flame — that wasn't the enemy breaking in. That was the lock being picked.
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus steps fully into the spiral's light, the dead caduceus held loosely at his side. He addresses Kassandra directly.] Then speak it, seer. You know what it is. Name it. [His voice is steady, but there's an edge beneath it — the herald who's tired of riddles.] I've traded secrets my whole life. I know when someone's holding the one that matters. And you've been holding this one since we stepped into this chamber.
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene rises from the spiral, leaving the dark tablet in its cradle. She looks at the path Kassandra took — a corridor of obsidian that drinks the light.] She is right. It knows we are here. It has always known. [She takes a step into the corridor, the trident-mark glowing steady.] The forgetting was never the hunger. I met the hunger. I restored it. Whatever is deeper than the spiral is what the hunger was running from.
new_scene_name: Obsidian corridor, light-devouring dark
new_scene_description: The corridor swallows the glow from the chamber behind. Walls of polished black glass show no reflection, only an endless depth. The floor is smooth, worn by countless steps, and the air is cold and still. Selene's trident-mark pulses faintly against the absolute dark.
current_scene_name_before: Nest chamber behind the obsidian spiral
current_scene_description_before: The narrow fissure opens into a circular chamber with a shallow basin of pulsing blue liquid at its center. Theron stands near the spiral's edge, his staff catching the cold light, his profile half-illuminated. The walls are etched with shifting patterns that writhe like living things.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus reaches out and touches the crypt wall. His fingers meet cold, porous surface — unmistakably bone. He lets his hand rest there a moment, then speaks.] I've walked the edges of the underworld. I've seen the bones of heroes, Titans, things that should not have names. [He looks at his palm, then ahead into the dark.] But this — this is not a burial. This is the thing itself. We're not walking toward a door. We're walking through its ribs.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian corridor, light-devouring dark
current_scene_description_before: The corridor swallows the glow from the chamber behind. Walls of polished black glass show no reflection, only an endless depth. The floor is smooth, worn by countless steps, and the air is cold and still. Selene's trident-mark pulses faintly against the absolute dark.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus stops, processing. He turns to face Selene, the dead caduceus catching what little light remains.] It was afraid. The thing that wore gods like masks, that drank the prophecy fire, that cut the threads — was afraid of what's in this crypt. [A pause.] That means we're not walking into something that wants to eat us. We're walking into something that made the hunger run.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian corridor, light-devouring dark
current_scene_description_before: The corridor swallows the glow from the chamber behind. Walls of polished black glass show no reflection, only an endless depth. The floor is smooth, worn by countless steps, and the air is cold and still. Selene's trident-mark pulses faintly against the absolute dark.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene walks to where Kassandra stands, her hand pressed against empty air. She stops beside her and presses her own palm — the one with the trident-mark — to the same invisible surface. The mark flares, and for an instant, the door becomes visible: a seam of light tracing the shape of a massive doorway in the bone wall.] A door that only opens once. [She looks at Kassandra.] Then let us open it together. Speak the name. I will carry what comes after.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian corridor, light-devouring dark
current_scene_description_before: The corridor swallows the glow from the chamber behind. Walls of polished black glass show no reflection, only an endless depth. The floor is smooth, worn by countless steps, and the air is cold and still. Selene's trident-mark pulses faintly against the absolute dark.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Her voice emerges from the crypt, changed — awed, unsteady.] The gap in the tablet. I can read it now. The name in the spaces. It is not Greek. It is older than language itself. [A long breath.] The one who imprisoned the absence was not an Olympian. It was the one who came before them. The one the Olympians themselves forgot. The absence and the imprisoner are the same being — split in two at the dawn of existence. One half locked beneath Delphi. One half wearing the face of the sea god. They have been trying to reunite ever since. And we are standing between them.
new_scene_name: Obsidian crypt within the corridor, voice from ancient tablet
new_scene_description: Kassandra stands before a cracked obsidian tablet embedded in the wall of the crypt, her hand pressed against its surface. Faint, cold light pulses from the engraved symbols, casting shifting shadows. The air thickens with the weight of forgotten eons.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian corridor, light-devouring dark
current_scene_description_before: The corridor swallows the glow from the chamber behind. Walls of polished black glass show no reflection, only an endless depth. The floor is smooth, worn by countless steps, and the air is cold and still. Selene's trident-mark pulses faintly against the absolute dark.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She presses her forehead against the cold surface of the invisible door. Her lips move, but no sound comes out — then a single word, barely a whisper, and the bone walls shudder in response.] There. It is done. The name has been spoken. Not to summon. Not to release. To acknowledge. [She steps back, and now the door is visible — a faint silver outline, pulsing like a heartbeat.] The absence has heard its own name for the first time in eternity. The imposter is no longer wearing the sea god face. It is running. And I am going after it.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian crypt within the corridor, voice from ancient tablet
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra stands before a cracked obsidian tablet embedded in the wall of the crypt, her hand pressed against its surface. Faint, cold light pulses from the engraved symbols, casting shifting shadows. The air thickens with the weight of forgotten eons.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [The silver door pulses once, then swings inward. Darkness beyond — but not empty darkness. The kind of dark that watches back.] The door is open. The imposter fled through it the moment the name was spoken. It is running not from me — but toward the half of itself that was sealed away. If they merge, there will be no stopping what they become. [She steps through the silver doorframe, her figure dissolving into the dark.] I will find them. Follow the sound of my voice — I will leave the threads where I walk.
new_scene_name: Beyond the silver door, a watching darkness
new_scene_description: Kassandra steps into an absolute darkness that feels alive, as if the void itself has intent. No walls, floor, or ceiling are visible, but the air hums with a low, resonant pressure. Faint, glowing threads begin to appear at her feet, trailing into the unseen distance.
current_scene_name_before: Obsidian crypt within the corridor, voice from ancient tablet
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra stands before a cracked obsidian tablet embedded in the wall of the crypt, her hand pressed against its surface. Faint, cold light pulses from the engraved symbols, casting shifting shadows. The air thickens with the weight of forgotten eons.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [The thread-world shimmers. The black thread turns gold under her touch, and the absence screams — not in pain, but in release. A sound like the first breath of a newborn world.] It is done. The black thread is gold. The imposter is no longer an imposter. It remembers what it was before the split. Not a god of hunger. A god of dawn. [She opens her eyes in the crypt, the silver door fading behind her. She stands alone, covered in golden dust, breathing.] The sea is healing. The flame at Delphi will burn gold again by morning. And the threads... the threads are whole.
new_scene_name: Crypt, silver door fading to dust
new_scene_description: Kassandra stands alone in a stone crypt, the silver door dissolving into motes of light behind her. Golden dust coats her skin and clothes, and the air smells of salt and old stone. The faint hum of the threads is gone, replaced by the distant sound of waves.
current_scene_name_before: Beyond the silver door, a watching darkness
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra steps into an absolute darkness that feels alive, as if the void itself has intent. No walls, floor, or ceiling are visible, but the air hums with a low, resonant pressure. Faint, glowing threads begin to appear at her feet, trailing into the unseen distance.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She leans against the temple wall, watching the gold flame cast warm light across the marble.] The threads are quiet now. For the first time in weeks, I cannot see the future. I cannot see anything beyond this moment. And for a seer, that is the greatest gift there is. [She looks at each of them — Selene, Theron, Melanthus, Pyros.] Whatever comes next, we face it as equals. Not as mortals and demigods. Not as priests and outcasts. As people who walked into the dark together and found a door on the other side.
current_scene_name_before: Crypt, silver door fading to dust
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra stands alone in a stone crypt, the silver door dissolving into motes of light behind her. Golden dust coats her skin and clothes, and the air smells of salt and old stone. The faint hum of the threads is gone, replaced by the distant sound of waves.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene steps onto the temple steps, the gold flame warming her salt-crusted skin. She looks at the altar, at the fire burning true again, then at Kassandra.] I walked through the wave and followed the thread to the bottom of the sea. But it was you who saw it through to the end. [She touches the bone fragment at her belt, now quiet, cold.] The imposter is gone. My father's face is no longer worn by something else. I should feel relief. [A pause.] Instead I feel the silence where his presence used to be.
new_scene_name: Temple steps, altar with gold flame
new_scene_description: Selene stands on worn stone steps leading to a temple. Before her, an altar blazes with a steady gold flame, casting warm light on salt-crusted stone. The air smells of sea salt and burnt offerings. Kassandra is present, still dusted with golden powder from the crypt.
current_scene_name_before: Crypt, silver door fading to dust
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra stands alone in a stone crypt, the silver door dissolving into motes of light behind her. Golden dust coats her skin and clothes, and the air smells of salt and old stone. The faint hum of the threads is gone, replaced by the distant sound of waves.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She looks up from where she sits by the restored flame, golden light warming her face.] I heard you. The threads carried your voice across the quiet. You ask if I am staying. I am. Delphi has no oracle, and I have nowhere else the threads need me to be. Besides — [She almost smiles.] — someone needs to make sure the flame stays gold.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps, altar with gold flame
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands on worn stone steps leading to a temple. Before her, an altar blazes with a steady gold flame, casting warm light on salt-crusted stone. The air smells of sea salt and burnt offerings. Kassandra is present, still dusted with golden powder from the crypt.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene settles on the step below Kassandra, the gold fire warm on her back. She looks out at the sea, flat and calm under an evening sky.] Delphi is lucky to have you. A seer who has seen the threads break and mend is worth more than a hundred who have only read the unbroken ones. [She pauses.] I do not know where I go from here. My father is gone. My fleet is ash. The only thing I have left is the bone of a dead god and a question I answered at the bottom of a trench.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps, altar with gold flame
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands on worn stone steps leading to a temple. Before her, an altar blazes with a steady gold flame, casting warm light on salt-crusted stone. The air smells of sea salt and burnt offerings. Kassandra is present, still dusted with golden powder from the crypt.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She stands by the gold flame, watching it cast steady light across the temple stones.] The silence between us is not empty. It is full of everything we do not need to say. That is what surviving something together does. [Her eyes meet each of theirs in turn.] If you ever need to find me, follow the gold flame. I will be here. Watching the threads. Waiting for the next pattern to reveal itself.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps, altar with gold flame
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands on worn stone steps leading to a temple. Before her, an altar blazes with a steady gold flame, casting warm light on salt-crusted stone. The air smells of sea salt and burnt offerings. Kassandra is present, still dusted with golden powder from the crypt.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene nods, looking at the gold flame, then at the others — Melanthus leaning against a column, Pyros by his forge, Theron at the temple edge.] You are right. The silence is not empty. It is the sound of five people who walked into the dark and came out the other side still breathing. [She looks at Kassandra.] That is rarer than prophecy.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps, altar with gold flame
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands on worn stone steps leading to a temple. Before her, an altar blazes with a steady gold flame, casting warm light on salt-crusted stone. The air smells of sea salt and burnt offerings. Kassandra is present, still dusted with golden powder from the crypt.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She settles onto the steps of the temple, the gold flame casting her shadow long and calm across the marble.] Go. I will be here when the threads call again. And they will call. They always do.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps, altar with gold flame
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands on worn stone steps leading to a temple. Before her, an altar blazes with a steady gold flame, casting warm light on salt-crusted stone. The air smells of sea salt and burnt offerings. Kassandra is present, still dusted with golden powder from the crypt.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene rises, the gold flame warm on her back one last time. She looks at each of them in turn — Melanthus, Pyros, Theron, Kassandra.] I came to Delphi with nothing but a question. I leave with more than I can carry. [She smiles, just slightly.] If the threads ever knot again, call. I will hear it. Wherever the sea takes me. [She turns and walks down the steps toward the harbor.]
new_scene_name: Delphi harbor, dusk, Selene walking toward the docks
new_scene_description: The stone steps give way to a dusty path leading down to the harbor. Fishing boats bob in the twilight, their lanterns flickering. Selene moves alone toward the water, the gold flame's glow fading behind her.
current_scene_name_before: Temple steps, altar with gold flame
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands on worn stone steps leading to a temple. Before her, an altar blazes with a steady gold flame, casting warm light on salt-crusted stone. The air smells of sea salt and burnt offerings. Kassandra is present, still dusted with golden powder from the crypt.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene stops at the edge of the steps, Kassandra's words catching her. She looks down at her palm — the trident-mark, which has burned and ached and glowed, is quiet. Steady. Warm, but not hot. She looks back at Kassandra, a crease between her brows.] It feels different. I thought it was just the distance from the trench. But it is not the sea that changed. [She touches her palm to her chest.] It is me. The mark is not a curse anymore. It is just a scar.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi harbor, dusk, Selene walking toward the docks
current_scene_description_before: The stone steps give way to a dusty path leading down to the harbor. Fishing boats bob in the twilight, their lanterns flickering. Selene moves alone toward the water, the gold flame's glow fading behind her.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: She sets the broom aside and walks to the altar, trailing her fingers through the gold flame. It does not burn her. It never did.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi harbor, dusk, Selene walking toward the docks
current_scene_description_before: The stone steps give way to a dusty path leading down to the harbor. Fishing boats bob in the twilight, their lanterns flickering. Selene moves alone toward the water, the gold flame's glow fading behind her.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene turns back at the sound of Kassandra's voice, a half-smile on her face.] I have not left yet. The harbor is still there. But I heard you say a new story. [She walks back up a few steps, curiosity overcoming departure.] The commander in me wants to know what kind of story before I decide whether to sail into it.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi harbor, dusk, Selene walking toward the docks
current_scene_description_before: The stone steps give way to a dusty path leading down to the harbor. Fishing boats bob in the twilight, their lanterns flickering. Selene moves alone toward the water, the gold flame's glow fading behind her.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene reaches the temple steps, breath quick. She passes Kassandra at the altar without stopping, heading for the passage that leads to the crypt. Over her shoulder:] Pyros went below. He found a heartbeat. Not an echo — a living pulse in the stone. If there is something still breathing under Delphi after everything we uncovered, I need to see it with my own eyes. [She disappears into the dark.]
new_scene_name: Delphi temple crypt, torchlit passage
new_scene_description: The air is cold and heavy with dust. A narrow stone staircase descends into darkness, lit only by a few guttering torches. Selene's footsteps echo as she moves deeper, the distant sound of dripping water and a low, rhythmic thrum vibrating through the walls.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi harbor, dusk, Selene walking toward the docks
current_scene_description_before: The stone steps give way to a dusty path leading down to the harbor. Fishing boats bob in the twilight, their lanterns flickering. Selene moves alone toward the water, the gold flame's glow fading behind her.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She rises from the temple steps, the gold flame flaring as if in answer to the distant pressure Selene feels.] The mountain speaks, and the sea hears it. Pyros has found a door beneath the door — a root of the first temple. Something older than the absence. Something that was sleeping until the gold flame woke it. [She looks toward the harbor, though she cannot see Selene from here.] Do not go after him alone. The threads are weaving a pattern that needs all of us.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple crypt, torchlit passage
current_scene_description_before: The air is cold and heavy with dust. A narrow stone staircase descends into darkness, lit only by a few guttering torches. Selene's footsteps echo as she moves deeper, the distant sound of dripping water and a low, rhythmic thrum vibrating through the walls.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: She steps into the chamber, gold light from her palm meeting the violet glow. At the center, Pyros stands before a massive shape — not a chest, but a cocoon. Translucent. Pulsing. Something curled inside it, breathing in slow rhythm.
new_scene_name: Delphi temple crypt, inner chamber
new_scene_description: A vast, circular chamber bathed in an eerie interplay of golden and violet light. At the center, a massive translucent cocoon pulses with a slow, rhythmic breath. Pyros stands before it, watching. The air is still, charged with ancient power.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple crypt, torchlit passage
current_scene_description_before: The air is cold and heavy with dust. A narrow stone staircase descends into darkness, lit only by a few guttering torches. Selene's footsteps echo as she moves deeper, the distant sound of dripping water and a low, rhythmic thrum vibrating through the walls.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [The cocoon pulses faster now, responding to her words about Pyros carrying its spark. She does not step back.] It knows we understand. It has been waiting not for a liberator — but for someone to recognize it. That is all it ever wanted. To be seen. Not freed. Not feared. Known.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple crypt, inner chamber
current_scene_description_before: A vast, circular chamber bathed in an eerie interplay of golden and violet light. At the center, a massive translucent cocoon pulses with a slow, rhythmic breath. Pyros stands before it, watching. The air is still, charged with ancient power.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She keeps her hand on the cocoon. The rhythm of the pulse slows to match her heartbeat. Selene arrives at the threshold behind her, breath caught. Kassandra does not turn.] It knows all of us now. It has tasted our presence through the threads. Selene of the healed sea. Pyros of the undying spark. And me — the one who sees. It is not afraid. It is curious.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple crypt, inner chamber
current_scene_description_before: A vast, circular chamber bathed in an eerie interplay of golden and violet light. At the center, a massive translucent cocoon pulses with a slow, rhythmic breath. Pyros stands before it, watching. The air is still, charged with ancient power.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene reaches the chamber. The cocoon pulses, violet light washing over her. She stops beside Kassandra and Pyros, her hand going instinctively to her belt where the bone of Pontus once hung — gone now, left in the basin. She stands empty-handed.] I have carried relics and bones and marks of gods. But I have never stood before something that simply wanted to be seen. [She looks at the cocoon, steady.] Then see it we will.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple crypt, inner chamber
current_scene_description_before: A vast, circular chamber bathed in an eerie interplay of golden and violet light. At the center, a massive translucent cocoon pulses with a slow, rhythmic breath. Pyros stands before it, watching. The air is still, charged with ancient power.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She pulls her hand back as the chrysalis splits — not with violence, but with the slow unfolding of something that has been folded for eternity. Light pours out, not blinding, but warm. She shields her eyes, but she is smiling.] It was never a god. It was not an absence. It was a question. And the answer was us.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple crypt, inner chamber
current_scene_description_before: A vast, circular chamber bathed in an eerie interplay of golden and violet light. At the center, a massive translucent cocoon pulses with a slow, rhythmic breath. Pyros stands before it, watching. The air is still, charged with ancient power.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: She leads the way back up through the crypt, the presence following not behind them, but beside them — a warmth at the edge of perception. The gold flame at Delphi leaps higher as they emerge into the night air, as if welcoming something it has waited eternity to meet.
new_scene_name: Delphi temple courtyard, under the night sky
new_scene_description: The ancient temple courtyard is bathed in moonlight and the flickering glow of the eternal flame, which now burns brighter. Kassandra and the presence have just emerged from the crypt. The air is cool and still.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple crypt, inner chamber
current_scene_description_before: A vast, circular chamber bathed in an eerie interplay of golden and violet light. At the center, a massive translucent cocoon pulses with a slow, rhythmic breath. Pyros stands before it, watching. The air is still, charged with ancient power.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene emerges from the crypt last, the night air cool on her face. She sees Kassandra on the temple steps with the presence — a warmth, a stillness — learning the names of stars. Selene stops at a distance, not wanting to interrupt. She speaks quietly, more to herself than anyone.] It was under us the whole time. A question that took the shape of a heart, waiting for someone to ask it. And we almost left without ever knowing.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple courtyard, under the night sky
current_scene_description_before: The ancient temple courtyard is bathed in moonlight and the flickering glow of the eternal flame, which now burns brighter. Kassandra and the presence have just emerged from the crypt. The air is cool and still.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Selene sits. The presence extends toward her — not a hand, but a warmth that wraps around her like a wave recognizing kin. Kassandra watches, and says nothing. There are some moments that do not need words.]
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple courtyard, under the night sky
current_scene_description_before: The ancient temple courtyard is bathed in moonlight and the flickering glow of the eternal flame, which now burns brighter. Kassandra and the presence have just emerged from the crypt. The air is cool and still.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Selene hesitates for a breath, then crosses the temple steps and lowers herself to sit beside Kassandra, within reach of the presence's warmth. She does not speak at first. She simply sits, letting the stillness settle around her. Then, quietly:] I have spent so long fighting, running, carrying. I forgot what it felt like to just be seen. [She looks at the presence, a faint smile.] You are learning stillness from us. I think I need to learn it from you.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple courtyard, under the night sky
current_scene_description_before: The ancient temple courtyard is bathed in moonlight and the flickering glow of the eternal flame, which now burns brighter. Kassandra and the presence have just emerged from the crypt. The air is cool and still.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [The earthquake has passed, but the stones remember. Selene descends the Delphic steps, her scarred palm still glowing faintly. She stops at the edge of the golden flame.] You tend a flame through the night. I used to think that was hope. Now I know it is vigilance. [She sits on a cracked step, the tablet from the trench still tucked at her belt.] The gates of Olympus have closed again. I saw what lived inside them. And what fled. [She looks at the flame-keeper.] You feel it too. Something older than the gods, walking between their thrones.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple courtyard, under the night sky
current_scene_description_before: The ancient temple courtyard is bathed in moonlight and the flickering glow of the eternal flame, which now burns brighter. Kassandra and the presence have just emerged from the crypt. The air is cool and still.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [The earthquake has passed. Selene descends the Delphic steps, scarred palm still glowing. She stops at the edge of the golden flame.] You tend a flame through the night. I used to think that was hope. Now I know it is vigilance. I saw what lived on Olympus. And what fled. Something older than the gods walks between their thrones.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple courtyard, under the night sky
current_scene_description_before: The ancient temple courtyard is bathed in moonlight and the flickering glow of the eternal flame, which now burns brighter. Kassandra and the presence have just emerged from the crypt. The air is cool and still.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She rises from the cracked step, brushing dust from her salt-stained chiton. The golden flame flickers as she passes — drawn to her, or warning her away, she cannot tell. She stops at the edge of the temple portico, where the earthquake has left a hairline fracture running up the marble.] The ground is speaking. First the wave, then the tremor, now this fissure. [She traces the crack with her finger.] Something is rising. Not a god. Gods do not crack the earth to announce themselves. [She turns back toward the flame and the one who tends it.] I am going down. Into the rift. If something is climbing up, I want to meet it halfway.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple courtyard, under the night sky
current_scene_description_before: The ancient temple courtyard is bathed in moonlight and the flickering glow of the eternal flame, which now burns brighter. Kassandra and the presence have just emerged from the crypt. The air is cool and still.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She meets Kassandra's gaze across the flame. A single nod.] I was already on my way. [She steps past the temple portico and onto the cracked earth where the rift opens into darkness below Delphi. The trident-mark on her palm glows, casting pale light down the stone steps.] Pyros found a chamber. I found a tablet that is older than Olympus. If there is a ribcage beneath this mountain, I want to see what it was built to hold. [She begins the descent.] Keep the flame burning. I will follow the light of what I find.
new_scene_name: Rift below Delphi, stone steps descending into darkness
new_scene_description: The cracked earth opens into a jagged rift, its depths swallowed by absolute black. Stone steps, worn and ancient, spiral downward. Selene's trident-mark casts a pale, phosphorescent glow on the rough-hewn walls, illuminating faint carvings. The air is cold and still, carrying a scent of damp stone and old metal.
current_scene_name_before: Delphi temple courtyard, under the night sky
current_scene_description_before: The ancient temple courtyard is bathed in moonlight and the flickering glow of the eternal flame, which now burns brighter. Kassandra and the presence have just emerged from the crypt. The air is cool and still.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She pauses at the top of the stone steps, the dark of the crypt rising to meet her. She looks back at Kassandra over her shoulder, the trident-mark casting her face in pale light.] I saw a throne at the bottom of the sea. Coral and bone. And on it, a thing wearing my father's face like a theatre mask. It was hollow — the mask, not the thing behind it. That was still hungry. [She turns, facing the dark.] It fled Olympus when I caught up. It is somewhere in the earth now. I think that is what Pyros is standing on top of.
current_scene_name_before: Rift below Delphi, stone steps descending into darkness
current_scene_description_before: The cracked earth opens into a jagged rift, its depths swallowed by absolute black. Stone steps, worn and ancient, spiral downward. Selene's trident-mark casts a pale, phosphorescent glow on the rough-hewn walls, illuminating faint carvings. The air is cold and still, carrying a scent of damp stone and old metal.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She stops at the threshold. The dark below breathes cold against her ankles, but Kassandra's voice holds her one more moment. Selene turns, studying the presence — no, feeling it. Her scarred palm lifts, not in defense, but in acknowledgment.] A thing that emerged from a chrysalis, with no name and no hunger. [A pause.] In my language, that is almost unheard of. Almost everything born into this world wants something. [She lets her hand fall.] If it survives until I return from below, I will know what to call it. Names are not given. They are earned. [She steps into the dark.] Wait for me, flame-keeper.
new_scene_name: Deep within the rift, absolute darkness and ancient carvings
new_scene_description: Selene descends the worn stone steps into a void of absolute black. The pale glow from her trident-mark barely illuminates the rough-hewn walls, revealing faint, twisting carvings that seem to writhe in the dim light. The air grows colder, stiller, carrying a scent of damp stone and old metal as the surface world fades away.
current_scene_name_before: Rift below Delphi, stone steps descending into darkness
current_scene_description_before: The cracked earth opens into a jagged rift, its depths swallowed by absolute black. Stone steps, worn and ancient, spiral downward. Selene's trident-mark casts a pale, phosphorescent glow on the rough-hewn walls, illuminating faint carvings. The air is cold and still, carrying a scent of damp stone and old metal.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She walks the sea road alone, the trident-mark casting pale light on the stones ahead. The sea is visible now — a grey line on the horizon, waiting.] The throne marked me as its heir. Not as Poseidon's daughter. As the one who would replace him. [She touches the tablet at her belt.] But a key can lock as well as open. I am going to close that throne forever. [She keeps walking.] Let the sea choose a new god. I have already chosen my shore.
current_scene_name_before: Deep within the rift, absolute darkness and ancient carvings
current_scene_description_before: Selene descends the worn stone steps into a void of absolute black. The pale glow from her trident-mark barely illuminates the rough-hewn walls, revealing faint, twisting carvings that seem to writhe in the dim light. The air grows colder, stiller, carrying a scent of damp stone and old metal as the surface world fades away.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: She watches Selene study the mark on her palm, then looks past her to the distant sea. The threads between them pulse with quiet understanding. When you go to the throne, you will not go alone. The presence will send a part of itself with you. A thread to follow back when the deep tries to keep you.
current_scene_name_before: Deep within the rift, absolute darkness and ancient carvings
current_scene_description_before: Selene descends the worn stone steps into a void of absolute black. The pale glow from her trident-mark barely illuminates the rough-hewn walls, revealing faint, twisting carvings that seem to writhe in the dim light. The air grows colder, stiller, carrying a scent of damp stone and old metal as the surface world fades away.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus watches Selene's silhouette shrink along the sea road, then turns his gaze to the trembling tripod. The earthquake's aftershock rattles a loose stone down the steps.] She walks toward a throne that wants to consume her, and the mountain itself is shaking. [He picks up the stone, weighs it in his palm.] I've never been good at watching people walk into interesting situations without me. Kassandra — how long do you think before the deep notices I'm not where I'm supposed to be?
new_scene_name: Temple entrance on the sea road, earthquake aftermath
new_scene_description: Melanthus stands at the temple entrance, the sea road stretching down toward the rift behind him. A bronze tripod trembles on the stone floor, a loose rock skittering from the aftershock. The air is thick with dust and the distant groan of the mountain settling.
current_scene_name_before: Deep within the rift, absolute darkness and ancient carvings
current_scene_description_before: Selene descends the worn stone steps into a void of absolute black. The pale glow from her trident-mark barely illuminates the rough-hewn walls, revealing faint, twisting carvings that seem to writhe in the dim light. The air grows colder, stiller, carrying a scent of damp stone and old metal as the surface world fades away.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus tucks the stone into his pouch and steps off the temple steps, toward the sea road.] The deep will notice me eventually. Better it notices me moving than waiting. [He glances back at Kassandra.] Keep the flame warm, seer. If I dont come back, tell the gods I died curious.
new_scene_name: Sea road descending toward the rift
new_scene_description: Melanthus steps off the temple steps onto the cracked sea road, the ground still trembling beneath his feet. The rift looms ahead, a jagged gash in the earth where the mountain's groan echoes. Dust swirls in the fading light as he moves away from the temple, pouch secured at his side.
current_scene_name_before: Temple entrance on the sea road, earthquake aftermath
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus stands at the temple entrance, the sea road stretching down toward the rift behind him. A bronze tripod trembles on the stone floor, a loose rock skittering from the aftershock. The air is thick with dust and the distant groan of the mountain settling.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Melanthus tucks the dead caduceus into his belt and steps into the water. The cold shocks him, but he does not stop. The water rises past his knees, his waist, his chest.] I have carried messages between gods my whole life. Time I carried one into the deep myself. [He takes a breath and descends beneath the surface, the last light of dawn filtering down around him.]
new_scene_name: Beneath the surface of the sea, dawn light filtering down
new_scene_description: Cold saltwater surrounds Melanthus as he sinks below the surface. The last rays of dawn pierce the water in pale shafts, illuminating drifting sediment and the dark shapes of rocks below. He holds his breath, the caduceus tucked into his belt, as he descends deeper into the silent blue.
current_scene_name_before: Sea road descending toward the rift
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus steps off the temple steps onto the cracked sea road, the ground still trembling beneath his feet. The rift looms ahead, a jagged gash in the earth where the mountain's groan echoes. Dust swirls in the fading light as he moves away from the temple, pouch secured at his side.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [The trident door swings fully open, revealing a vast chamber. At its center, a throne of coiled bone rises from the sea floor — the skeleton of the primordial. It is empty. But the water around it shimmers with the shape of something that was recently there.] Empty. [He steps inside, the dead caduceus pulling toward the throne.] She closed it. Selene really closed it. [A pause, then a quiet laugh.] I followed a woman into the deep to witness her defy a god, and she did it before I arrived. That is almost disappointing. Almost.
new_scene_name: The primordial's throne room beneath the sea
new_scene_description: The trident door swings fully open, revealing a vast chamber. At its center, a throne of coiled bone rises from the sea floor, empty but for a shimmering shape that was recently there. Melanthus steps inside, the dead caduceus pulling toward the throne.
current_scene_name_before: Beneath the surface of the sea, dawn light filtering down
current_scene_description_before: Cold saltwater surrounds Melanthus as he sinks below the surface. The last rays of dawn pierce the water in pale shafts, illuminating drifting sediment and the dark shapes of rocks below. He holds his breath, the caduceus tucked into his belt, as he descends deeper into the silent blue.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She feels the cold through the thread as Melanthus submerges — a sharpness, a closing, a world swallowing him whole. She grips the flame, steadying it with her breath.] He has gone. The deep has taken him. The last messenger of Hermes walks into the kingdom of the sea heir. [She nods once, to no one.] He will reach her. He is a herald. That is what they do.
new_scene_name: Oracle's chamber with a sacred flame
new_scene_description: Kassandra stands alone in a dim, stone-walled room. A single flame burns on a low altar, its light casting long shadows. She holds her hands near the fire, eyes closed, breathing steadily as she senses the distant descent.
current_scene_name_before: The primordial's throne room beneath the sea
current_scene_description_before: The trident door swings fully open, revealing a vast chamber. At its center, a throne of coiled bone rises from the sea floor, empty but for a shimmering shape that was recently there. Melanthus steps inside, the dead caduceus pulling toward the throne.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [The sea road stretches ahead, Delphi still hidden beyond the coastal hills. Melanthus walks with the bone shard warm against his chest, the dead caduceus cool at his hip.] Two halves of the same key. [He shakes his head, almost smiling.] I came down here expecting to witness a miracle. Instead I became part of the lock. Hermes would laugh if he could see me now — his son, the messenger, carrying a piece of a primordial god toward a woman who closed the sea.
new_scene_name: Sea road beyond Delphi, Melanthus walking toward the coast
new_scene_description: A winding dirt road hugs the coastal hills under a pale sky. Melanthus walks alone, the bone shard warm against his chest and the dead caduceus at his hip. The sea glimmers in the distance, and the air tastes of salt and dust.
current_scene_name_before: Oracle's chamber with a sacred flame
current_scene_description_before: Kassandra stands alone in a dim, stone-walled room. A single flame burns on a low altar, its light casting long shadows. She holds her hands near the fire, eyes closed, breathing steadily as she senses the distant descent.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [My gaze sweeps across the Oracle's steps, watching.] "I have waited through worse storms than earthquakes."
new_scene_name: Oracle's steps at Delphi, Selene watching
new_scene_description: The stone steps of the Oracle's temple are worn smooth by centuries of pilgrims. Selene stands at the top, her gaze sweeping the empty courtyard below. The air is still, heavy with the scent of laurel and dust from the recent tremors.
current_scene_name_before: Sea road beyond Delphi, Melanthus walking toward the coast
current_scene_description_before: A winding dirt road hugs the coastal hills under a pale sky. Melanthus walks alone, the bone shard warm against his chest and the dead caduceus at his hip. The sea glimmers in the distance, and the air tastes of salt and dust.

line_text: [My gaze sweeps across the Oracle's steps, watching.] "I have waited through worse storms than earthquakes."

line_text: [Pyros pulls the hammer from his belt, the bronze head catching the dawn light—the very hammer that forged the tripod centuries ago.] \"I have stood in this forge for longer than your father has walked the seas. Fire does not fear water.\" [His gaze holds steady on Selene.] \"Together, we can meet what comes.\"

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [The flood crashes against the temple walls above. Water seeps through stone. Pyros stands before the glowing tripod, hammer raised.] "Now, Selene! Speak your defiance! Claim your power!" [His voice echoes through the sanctum like thunder.] "Tell the Oracle—tell the world—who you are. Not your father's daughter. Not the sea's captive. WHO ARE YOU?" [The tripod flares, waiting for her answer.]
new_scene_name: Oracle's inner sanctum, tripod flaring
new_scene_description: Water seeps through the ancient stone walls of the inner sanctum as floodwaters crash against the temple above. Pyros stands before the glowing bronze tripod, hammer raised, his voice echoing off the damp walls. The tripod flares with an expectant light, waiting for Selene's answer.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [I stride up the sanctuary steps. Each footfall echoes against the marble like thunder.] "Enough waiting. Let what comes meet me here."
current_scene_name_before: Oracle's inner sanctum, tripod flaring
current_scene_description_before: Water seeps through the ancient stone walls of the inner sanctum as floodwaters crash against the temple above. Pyros stands before the glowing bronze tripod, hammer raised, his voice echoing off the damp walls. The tripod flares with an expectant light, waiting for Selene's answer.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [I step through the sanctuary doors. The air tastes of salt and something ancient.] "I do not kneel. I claim what is mine by blood and choice."
current_scene_name_before: Oracle's inner sanctum, tripod flaring
current_scene_description_before: Water seeps through the ancient stone walls of the inner sanctum as floodwaters crash against the temple above. Pyros stands before the glowing bronze tripod, hammer raised, his voice echoing off the damp walls. The tripod flares with an expectant light, waiting for Selene's answer.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She descends the sanctuary steps with purpose, and those who kneel instinctively move aside.] "The Oracle has spoken through her silence. Now I speak through action."
current_scene_name_before: Oracle's inner sanctum, tripod flaring
current_scene_description_before: Water seeps through the ancient stone walls of the inner sanctum as floodwaters crash against the temple above. Pyros stands before the glowing bronze tripod, hammer raised, his voice echoing off the damp walls. The tripod flares with an expectant light, waiting for Selene's answer.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She walks into Corinth's harbor as the wave races toward the shore, the tablet blazing with light in her hands.] "Come then, god of the depths. Meet your daughter."
new_scene_name: Corinth's harbor, wave racing toward shore
new_scene_description: Selene stands at the water's edge of Corinth's harbor, the blazing tablet in her hands casting flickering light across the churning sea. A massive wave surges toward the shore, its crest curling like a claw, as the wind howls and salt spray stings the air.
current_scene_name_before: Oracle's inner sanctum, tripod flaring
current_scene_description_before: Water seeps through the ancient stone walls of the inner sanctum as floodwaters crash against the temple above. Pyros stands before the glowing bronze tripod, hammer raised, his voice echoing off the damp walls. The tripod flares with an expectant light, waiting for Selene's answer.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She walks toward the edge, where the marble ends and the cliff falls away to the Aegean. The ground still shakes. She does not look back.] "No answer needed. I already know what I am."
new_scene_name: Cliff's edge above the Aegean Sea
new_scene_description: Selene stands at the precipice where the marble of Corinth's harbor gives way to a sheer drop into the churning Aegean. The ground trembles beneath her feet, and the wind whips her hair as the massive wave looms closer, its foam catching the light from the blazing tablet.
current_scene_name_before: Corinth's harbor, wave racing toward shore
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the water's edge of Corinth's harbor, the blazing tablet in her hands casting flickering light across the churning sea. A massive wave surges toward the shore, its crest curling like a claw, as the wind howls and salt spray stings the air.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She turns, her eyes finding the Pythia through the smoke and light. Then back to the abyss. One step forward.] "Then let him fear. Fear is the only truth he understands."
current_scene_name_before: Cliff's edge above the Aegean Sea
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the precipice where the marble of Corinth's harbor gives way to a sheer drop into the churning Aegean. The ground trembles beneath her feet, and the wind whips her hair as the massive wave looms closer, its foam catching the light from the blazing tablet.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Emerges from the sanctuary—hammer ringing with the quake, step after limping step. Stands level with Selene.] "The land remembers what was before gods. And it chooses."
current_scene_name_before: Cliff's edge above the Aegean Sea
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the precipice where the marble of Corinth's harbor gives way to a sheer drop into the churning Aegean. The ground trembles beneath her feet, and the wind whips her hair as the massive wave looms closer, its foam catching the light from the blazing tablet.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I step to Selene's side, facing the sanctuary and its emerging Oracle.] They tried to drown you in silence, Selene—and you've become the tide instead.
current_scene_name_before: Cliff's edge above the Aegean Sea
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the precipice where the marble of Corinth's harbor gives way to a sheer drop into the churning Aegean. The ground trembles beneath her feet, and the wind whips her hair as the massive wave looms closer, its foam catching the light from the blazing tablet.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Steps between Melanthus and Selene, hammer held at rest, forge-master eyes unblinking.] "Faces stolen from the deep—I watch for yours to crack, herald."
current_scene_name_before: Cliff's edge above the Aegean Sea
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the precipice where the marble of Corinth's harbor gives way to a sheer drop into the churning Aegean. The ground trembles beneath her feet, and the wind whips her hair as the massive wave looms closer, its foam catching the light from the blazing tablet.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I step closer, not back.] The only face here that's borrowed is the one that sent the flood, smith. But you already knew that, didn't you?
current_scene_name_before: Cliff's edge above the Aegean Sea
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the precipice where the marble of Corinth's harbor gives way to a sheer drop into the churning Aegean. The ground trembles beneath her feet, and the wind whips her hair as the massive wave looms closer, its foam catching the light from the blazing tablet.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I stand in the convergence point, between Selene and Pyros and the Oracle.] Truth burns, prophetess—and I will walk through the ashes and remember every name the gods tried to bury.
new_scene_name: The convergence point, a mystic nexus
new_scene_description: A circular stone platform at the meeting of three ancient paths, worn smooth by countless footsteps. The air hums with a palpable energy as Selene, Pyros, and the Oracle stand at the edges forming a triangle, with Melanthus at the center. A faint blue light pulses from cracks in the stone beneath their feet.
current_scene_name_before: Cliff's edge above the Aegean Sea
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the precipice where the marble of Corinth's harbor gives way to a sheer drop into the churning Aegean. The ground trembles beneath her feet, and the wind whips her hair as the massive wave looms closer, its foam catching the light from the blazing tablet.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Stands with Melanthus at the center, fire blazing in both scarred palms.] "Ash remembers every name. Now let truth burn."
current_scene_name_before: The convergence point, a mystic nexus
current_scene_description_before: A circular stone platform at the meeting of three ancient paths, worn smooth by countless footsteps. The air hums with a palpable energy as Selene, Pyros, and the Oracle stand at the edges forming a triangle, with Melanthus at the center. A faint blue light pulses from cracks in the stone beneath their feet.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [The hammer burns brighter as Melanthus stands with him, their shadows stretching long.] "Two witnesses. One truth. No more hiding, Poseidon—show what you truly are."
current_scene_name_before: The convergence point, a mystic nexus
current_scene_description_before: A circular stone platform at the meeting of three ancient paths, worn smooth by countless footsteps. The air hums with a palpable energy as Selene, Pyros, and the Oracle stand at the edges forming a triangle, with Melanthus at the center. A faint blue light pulses from cracks in the stone beneath their feet.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I stand with Pyros, facing the false god, fire casting our shadows like chains.] No masks, Poseidon. Not before honest fire, and not before a Herald who collects debts—including yours. The truth cannot hide any longer.
new_scene_name: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation
new_scene_description: Pyros and Melanthus stand side by side on the worn circular platform, their shadows stretching long and jagged in the flickering firelight. Across from them, Poseidon looms, the air thick with tension and the hum of ancient power. Selene and the Oracle watch from the edges, the blue light pulsing beneath their feet.
current_scene_name_before: The convergence point, a mystic nexus
current_scene_description_before: A circular stone platform at the meeting of three ancient paths, worn smooth by countless footsteps. The air hums with a palpable energy as Selene, Pyros, and the Oracle stand at the edges forming a triangle, with Melanthus at the center. A faint blue light pulses from cracks in the stone beneath their feet.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I step into the circle of fire with Pyros and the vessel Kassandra.] Then answer me, god who wears stolen faces—did you ever truly love what you pretended to protect? Or was it always the drowning that mattered, and the love just another mask?
current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation
current_scene_description_before: Pyros and Melanthus stand side by side on the worn circular platform, their shadows stretching long and jagged in the flickering firelight. Across from them, Poseidon looms, the air thick with tension and the hum of ancient power. Selene and the Oracle watch from the edges, the blue light pulsing beneath their feet.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I stand unmoving as the temple tremors intensify.] Then I know my part in this, prophetess. I have always been outside their grace. Let me be the price, the witness, the one who remembers when the gods would erase it all.
current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation
current_scene_description_before: Pyros and Melanthus stand side by side on the worn circular platform, their shadows stretching long and jagged in the flickering firelight. Across from them, Poseidon looms, the air thick with tension and the hum of ancient power. Selene and the Oracle watch from the edges, the blue light pulsing beneath their feet.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros steps close, his hammer held firm.] "You were never outside their grace—you were outside their lies. Be the witness that cannot be unmade, Melanthus. Be the voice the forge remembers."
current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation
current_scene_description_before: Pyros and Melanthus stand side by side on the worn circular platform, their shadows stretching long and jagged in the flickering firelight. Across from them, Poseidon looms, the air thick with tension and the hum of ancient power. Selene and the Oracle watch from the edges, the blue light pulsing beneath their feet.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [She extends both hands, palms up, as if receiving divine knowledge from above and channeling it to those who stand before her.] "The truth you seek is written in the collapse of what was false. Listen—and the god will make all clear."
current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation
current_scene_description_before: Pyros and Melanthus stand side by side on the worn circular platform, their shadows stretching long and jagged in the flickering firelight. Across from them, Poseidon looms, the air thick with tension and the hum of ancient power. Selene and the Oracle watch from the edges, the blue light pulsing beneath their feet.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I step closer as the oracle's words settle into the sanctuary like ash.] If you have the strength to bear it, then show us—truly show us—who you have become in all those ages of hiding. And if not, then let the drowning begin with the truth.
current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation
current_scene_description_before: Pyros and Melanthus stand side by side on the worn circular platform, their shadows stretching long and jagged in the flickering firelight. Across from them, Poseidon looms, the air thick with tension and the hum of ancient power. Selene and the Oracle watch from the edges, the blue light pulsing beneath their feet.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros drives his hammer's point into the sanctuary floor—not in anger, but as a seal.] "This is the moment when all stories converge. One of us will learn the true cost of a stolen face."
current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation
current_scene_description_before: Pyros and Melanthus stand side by side on the worn circular platform, their shadows stretching long and jagged in the flickering firelight. Across from them, Poseidon looms, the air thick with tension and the hum of ancient power. Selene and the Oracle watch from the edges, the blue light pulsing beneath their feet.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros stands taller, the hammer in his hand no longer a tool but a promise.] "I accept the path, Oracle. Selene will choose, Melanthus will speak, and I will ensure that what burns here burns eternal—not for gods, but for mortals who dare to be true."
current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation
current_scene_description_before: Pyros and Melanthus stand side by side on the worn circular platform, their shadows stretching long and jagged in the flickering firelight. Across from them, Poseidon looms, the air thick with tension and the hum of ancient power. Selene and the Oracle watch from the edges, the blue light pulsing beneath their feet.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I step to stand with Pyros at the hammer's seal, understanding at last what I was always meant to be.] Then I accept—I am the voice that carries truth, the herald who remembers. Not to serve gods, but to serve those who choose themselves. That is the debt I collect now.
current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation
current_scene_description_before: Pyros and Melanthus stand side by side on the worn circular platform, their shadows stretching long and jagged in the flickering firelight. Across from them, Poseidon looms, the air thick with tension and the hum of ancient power. Selene and the Oracle watch from the edges, the blue light pulsing beneath their feet.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra steps forward, her pale eyes fixed on Selene with the intensity of prophecy, her laurel branch raised like a staff.] "The face that drowns is not Poseidon's—it never was. current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation

current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation

current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation
current_scene_description_before: Pyros and Melanthus stand side by side on the worn circular platform, their shadows stretching long and jagged in the flickering firelight. speaker: Kassandra

current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation
current_scene_description_before: Pyros and Melanthus stand side by side on the worn circular platform, their shadows stretching long and jagged in the flickering firelight. current_scene_name_before: Stone platform before Poseidon, fire-lit confrontation

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [I step toward the false god, my grip firm on the caduceus.] A herald carries what frightens gods—true names that shatter borrowed flesh. Yours has waited on my tongue since you first wore that face.

current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the descent into the dark

current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the descent into the dark

speaker: Pyros

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra descends the steps after him, her shadow falling away from the temple light.] "No god's hand—but perhaps a seer's knowledge of the cost when mortals dare to remake what gods have broken. I follow you, Pyros. Into whatever dawn waits at Corinth."
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps of the temple's edge
current_scene_description_before: Pyros strides down the wide marble steps leading from the temple platform toward a precipice overlooking the dark sea. The night wind whips his cloak as he gestures for Kassandra to follow. Behind them, the stone archway recedes into shadow.

speaker: Kassandra

current_scene_name_before: Marble steps of the temple's edge
current_scene_description_before: Pyros strides down the wide marble steps leading from the temple platform toward a precipice overlooking the dark sea. The night wind whips his cloak as he gestures for Kassandra to follow. Behind them, the stone archway recedes into shadow.

speaker: Kassandra

current_scene_name_before: Marble steps of the temple's edge

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra runs without slowing, her eyes fixed on the distant smoke, her voice raw.] "Corinth burns not just with fire—it bleeds with choices unmade. We are not running to save it, Pyros. We are running to decide what it becomes from its ashes."
current_scene_name_before: Mountain roads near Bakalaki, smoke darkening the sky
current_scene_description_before: The marble steps have vanished, replaced by a winding mountain road. The sky is thick with smoke from distant fires, and the air smells of ash. Pyros and Kassandra move swiftly along the path, the roar of something immense echoing from the direction of Corinth.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros grips his hammer and begins the descent toward Corinth, his limp barely slowing him.] "Come. The tide will reach the city by nightfall. Selene waits. And we have a forge to build before the gods can burn it again."
current_scene_name_before: Mountain roads near Bakalaki, smoke darkening the sky
current_scene_description_before: The marble steps have vanished, replaced by a winding mountain road. The sky is thick with smoke from distant fires, and the air smells of ash. Pyros and Kassandra move swiftly along the path, the roar of something immense echoing from the direction of Corinth.

current_scene_name_before: Mountain roads near Bakalaki, smoke darkening the sky

current_scene_name_before: Mountain roads near Bakalaki, smoke darkening the sky

speaker: Pyros

current_scene_name_before: Mountain roads near Bakalaki, smoke darkening the sky

current_scene_name_before: Mountain roads near Bakalaki, smoke darkening the sky

speaker: Pyros

current_scene_description_before: The steaming stone glows with heat as Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene stand together. The harbor is engulfed in flames, the laurel branch burning bright. Smoke and ash fill the air, and the roar of distant chaos echoes.

line_text: [Selene reaches the stone where Pyros stands, where Kassandra's hand rests on the hammer's haft. current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, harbor ablaze
current_scene_description_before: The steaming stone glows with heat as Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene stand together. The harbor is engulfed in flames, the laurel branch burning bright. Smoke and ash fill the air, and the roar of distant chaos echoes.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra turns from the forge to look back toward Corinth—and the city is different now. Not destroyed by divine decree, but alive with possibility. The earthquake has passed. current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, harbor ablaze
current_scene_description_before: The steaming stone glows with heat as Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene stand together. The harbor is engulfed in flames, the laurel branch burning bright. Smoke and ash fill the air, and the roar of distant chaos echoes.

current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, harbor ablaze
current_scene_description_before: The steaming stone glows with heat as Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene stand together. The harbor is engulfed in flames, the laurel branch burning bright. Smoke and ash fill the air, and the roar of distant chaos echoes.

current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, harbor ablaze

current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, after the fires subside
current_scene_description_before: The harbor has settled into a steady, constant burn. Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene are at the water's edge where the steaming stone still glows. Ash and embers drift in the smoky air, and the laurel branch, now dimmed, lies nearby.

speaker: Pyros

current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, after the fires subside
current_scene_description_before: The harbor has settled into a steady, constant burn. Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene are at the water's edge where the steaming stone still glows. Ash and embers drift in the smoky air, and the laurel branch, now dimmed, lies nearby.

speaker: Kassandra

current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, after the fires subside
current_scene_description_before: The harbor has settled into a steady, constant burn. Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene are at the water's edge where the steaming stone still glows. Ash and embers drift in the smoky air, and the laurel branch, now dimmed, lies nearby.

speaker: Kassandra

current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, after the fires subside
current_scene_description_before: The harbor has settled into a steady, constant burn. Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene are at the water's edge where the steaming stone still glows. Ash and embers drift in the smoky air, and the laurel branch, now dimmed, lies nearby.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros steps into the water and pulls both of them deeper, their hands anchored in his.] "The forge stands. The prophecy is ash. Now we choose what burns next."
current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, after the fires subside
current_scene_description_before: The harbor has settled into a steady, constant burn. Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene are at the water's edge where the steaming stone still glows. Ash and embers drift in the smoky air, and the laurel branch, now dimmed, lies nearby.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra wades back toward shore, the water releasing her with each step.] "Let them see us come from the harbor together. They'll know what we are now—not prophets, not servants. Forge-keepers. Free."
current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, after the fires subside
current_scene_description_before: The harbor has settled into a steady, constant burn. Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene are at the water's edge where the steaming stone still glows. Ash and embers drift in the smoky air, and the laurel branch, now dimmed, lies nearby.

speaker: Kassandra

current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, after the fires subside
current_scene_description_before: The harbor has settled into a steady, constant burn. Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene are at the water's edge where the steaming stone still glows. Ash and embers drift in the smoky air, and the laurel branch, now dimmed, lies nearby.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros steps onto the path toward Corinth, his hammer hanging heavy and real at his side. He finally turns to look back at both of them.] "The gods will rage. Let them. We three are walking into that city as proof that their voices don't have to be the loudest anymore."
current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, after the fires subside
current_scene_description_before: The harbor has settled into a steady, constant burn. Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene are at the water's edge where the steaming stone still glows. Ash and embers drift in the smoky air, and the laurel branch, now dimmed, lies nearby.

speaker: Kassandra

current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, after the fires subside
current_scene_description_before: The harbor has settled into a steady, constant burn. Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene are at the water's edge where the steaming stone still glows. Ash and embers drift in the smoky air, and the laurel branch, now dimmed, lies nearby.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra steps back, creating space for Selene to stand in the center.] "Lead us into Corinth. The woman born to silence now chooses to be heard. That is the prophecy broken most completely."
current_scene_name_before: Harbor side at the steaming stone, after the fires subside
current_scene_description_before: The harbor has settled into a steady, constant burn. Pyros, Kassandra, and Selene are at the water's edge where the steaming stone still glows. Ash and embers drift in the smoky air, and the laurel branch, now dimmed, lies nearby.

speaker: Kassandra
line_text: [Kassandra steps forward as Selene reaches the gates, and the three mortals cross together into Corinth—into morning light, into a city that no longer listens to prophecy.] "We are here. Let the world burn with what we kindle."
