current_scene_description_before: Selene stands in the threshold of a marble sanctuary, a dagger raised. The pale dawn light streams through the columns, casting long shadows across the stone floor. The air is still and heavy with the scent of incense and dust.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Steps into the blinding light, dagger raised] His hesitation is the wound. [She meets the shadow's gaze] Now I finish it.
current_scene_name_before: A forge lit by molten metal, Melanthus stepping into the glow
current_scene_description_before: The air shimmers with heat from a central anvil, where a molten blade cools. Shadows dance across walls lined with ancient tools. Melanthus stands at the threshold, tablet raised, facing the darkness beyond the forge's light.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Steps into the forge-light, staff raised high—his white eye ablaze] "Thirty years I've carried your curse for refusing one child. I bear it now for this: defiance over prayer."
current_scene_name_before: A forge lit by molten metal, Melanthus stepping into the glow
current_scene_description_before: The air shimmers with heat from a central anvil, where a molten blade cools. Shadows dance across walls lined with ancient tools. Melanthus stands at the threshold, tablet raised, facing the darkness beyond the forge's light.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Steps through the threshold into the void, dagger raised—and the earth trembles in answer] I have been his daughter long enough. Now I am only mine. [The forge-light follows her into the dark]
new_scene_name: The dark void beyond the forge's light
new_scene_description: Absolute darkness swallows the space, broken only by the trailing glow of forge-light that clings to Selene like a second skin. The ground shudders beneath her feet as she stands with dagger raised, alone in the unknown.
current_scene_name_before: A forge lit by molten metal, Melanthus stepping into the glow
current_scene_description_before: The air shimmers with heat from a central anvil, where a molten blade cools. Shadows dance across walls lined with ancient tools. Melanthus stands at the threshold, tablet raised, facing the darkness beyond the forge's light.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros raises his hammer high—and the forge-fire answering, blazing into the void after Selene] You forged me a cripple so I would know this. [Steps into the dark] So I would know what it means to make something eternal with broken hands.
new_scene_name: The forge-fire-lit void where Pyros steps into darkness
new_scene_description: A blazing trail of forge-fire carves through the absolute darkness, casting flickering shadows as Pyros advances, hammer raised. Selene stands ahead, dagger glinting in the uneven light, the ground trembling beneath both of them.
current_scene_name_before: The dark void beyond the forge's light
current_scene_description_before: Absolute darkness swallows the space, broken only by the trailing glow of forge-light that clings to Selene like a second skin. The ground shudders beneath her feet as she stands with dagger raised, alone in the unknown.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She grips his hand as he enters] Your brokenness forged something eternal. [Steps beside him] Mine forged my own will. [They move forward together into the shadow]
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire-lit void where Pyros steps into darkness
current_scene_description_before: A blazing trail of forge-fire carves through the absolute darkness, casting flickering shadows as Pyros advances, hammer raised. Selene stands ahead, dagger glinting in the uneven light, the ground trembling beneath both of them.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros meets her gaze as they walk] And you are more divine than he ever was. [Steps with her toward the threshold, toward Theron and Melanthus] This is what gods should fear—mortals who choose themselves.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire-lit void where Pyros steps into darkness
current_scene_description_before: A blazing trail of forge-fire carves through the absolute darkness, casting flickering shadows as Pyros advances, hammer raised. Selene stands ahead, dagger glinting in the uneven light, the ground trembling beneath both of them.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She reaches for the tablet—hand meeting his in the light] A god's son and a mortal. [Steps with him toward Theron and Pyros, toward dawn] Speak what we know. [Glances back at the temple] The asking was always the answer.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire-lit void where Pyros steps into darkness
current_scene_description_before: A blazing trail of forge-fire carves through the absolute darkness, casting flickering shadows as Pyros advances, hammer raised. Selene stands ahead, dagger glinting in the uneven light, the ground trembling beneath both of them.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Steps to meet them at the threshold, staff planted firm] "Then the first priesthood begins—not of gods who demand, but of those brave enough to refuse them."
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire-lit void where Pyros steps into darkness
current_scene_description_before: A blazing trail of forge-fire carves through the absolute darkness, casting flickering shadows as Pyros advances, hammer raised. Selene stands ahead, dagger glinting in the uneven light, the ground trembling beneath both of them.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She stands beside him, then steps to plant her dagger beside the hammer and staff] You speak as Herald. [Meets his eyes, then Theron's, then Pyros's] And I will stand as Guardian—I will answer what comes for us. [Faces the Aegean] The false god's shadow cannot touch what we have built.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire-lit void where Pyros steps into darkness
current_scene_description_before: A blazing trail of forge-fire carves through the absolute darkness, casting flickering shadows as Pyros advances, hammer raised. Selene stands ahead, dagger glinting in the uneven light, the ground trembling beneath both of them.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Stands among the symbols—staff planted with hammer, tablet, dagger—and looks back at the temple] "Then I stand as Witness. Thirty years I carried what it cost to refuse. Let me bear what it costs to build."
new_scene_name: Temple interior with symbolic artifacts
new_scene_description: Theron stands among the scattered symbols—a staff planted upright, a hammer, a tablet, and a dagger—arranged in a deliberate pattern. He looks back toward the temple entrance, his expression resolute. The air is still, heavy with the weight of past choices.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire-lit void where Pyros steps into darkness
current_scene_description_before: A blazing trail of forge-fire carves through the absolute darkness, casting flickering shadows as Pyros advances, hammer raised. Selene stands ahead, dagger glinting in the uneven light, the ground trembling beneath both of them.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Touches the tablet one last time, then steps to face the breaking dawn] I will speak this from every threshold until the question echoes back through Olympus itself. [Turns to meet their eyes] Not as a messenger of gods, but as Herald of choice. The age of asking has already begun—it began the moment she refused.
current_scene_name_before: Temple interior with symbolic artifacts
current_scene_description_before: Theron stands among the scattered symbols—a staff planted upright, a hammer, a tablet, and a dagger—arranged in a deliberate pattern. He looks back toward the temple entrance, his expression resolute. The air is still, heavy with the weight of past choices.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [The dawn breaks fully—and over the Aegean, the first ships begin to sail] My refusal was a question. [She looks at them all] Now yours will teach the world to ask. [Stands guard at the threshold] And I will answer.
new_scene_name: Aegean coast at dawn, ships setting sail
new_scene_description: The first light of dawn spills across the Aegean Sea, illuminating the dark silhouettes of ships as they begin to move from the harbor. The air is cool and salt-tinged, carrying the creak of rigging and the distant calls of sailors. Selene stands at the threshold of the temple, her gaze fixed on the horizon.
current_scene_name_before: Temple interior with symbolic artifacts
current_scene_description_before: Theron stands among the scattered symbols—a staff planted upright, a hammer, a tablet, and a dagger—arranged in a deliberate pattern. He looks back toward the temple entrance, his expression resolute. The air is still, heavy with the weight of past choices.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Steps into the light of the eternal forge-fire] Then the question I carry will outlive me. [Meets the eyes of Pyros, Selene, Theron] And that is what it means to be Herald—to speak what endures beyond the speaker. [Turns toward the ships sailing into dawn] My voice becomes part of something eternal now.
new_scene_name: Eternal forge-fire, light of creation
new_scene_description: The forge-fire blazes in a cavernous chamber, its flames casting dancing shadows on walls lined with ancient tools and ingots. The heat is intense, and the air shimmers with motes of ember. Melanthus stands at the heart of the light, facing Pyros, Selene, and Theron, while the distant ships are visible through an opening in the rock.
current_scene_name_before: Aegean coast at dawn, ships setting sail
current_scene_description_before: The first light of dawn spills across the Aegean Sea, illuminating the dark silhouettes of ships as they begin to move from the harbor. The air is cool and salt-tinged, carrying the creak of rigging and the distant calls of sailors. Selene stands at the threshold of the temple, her gaze fixed on the horizon.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Crosses the threshold toward the dawn, pauses to meet Selene's eyes one last time] "Thirty years I stood at the boundary between worlds. Now I step into the one we've chosen."
new_scene_name: Cliff's edge at dawn, overlooking the sea
new_scene_description: The first rays of sunlight spill over the horizon, painting the sky in shades of rose and gold. Theron stands at the precipice of a rocky cliff, the wind tugging at his cloak. Below, the waves crash against the shore, and the distant ships are tiny specks on the glimmering water. Selene is a few steps behind, her silhouette framed by the fading light of the forge-fire behind them.
current_scene_name_before: Eternal forge-fire, light of creation
current_scene_description_before: The forge-fire blazes in a cavernous chamber, its flames casting dancing shadows on walls lined with ancient tools and ingots. The heat is intense, and the air shimmers with motes of ember. Melanthus stands at the heart of the light, facing Pyros, Selene, and Theron, while the distant ships are visible through an opening in the rock.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Watches Theron cross into dawn without flinching] Then I am the hinge. [Stands rooted at the threshold between temple and world] Pyros keeps the eternal flame. The Herald carries the word. The Witness bears the memory. [Voice steady as the dagger in the marble] And I hold this door.
new_scene_name: Threshold of the Temple of Eternal Flame, dawn
new_scene_description: Sunlight spills through a stone doorway, illuminating ancient carvings of fire and wings. Selene stands on the worn threshold, one foot in the shadowed sanctuary, the other in the open air. Behind her, the eternal flame flickers on its marble altar, casting long shadows across the chamber.
current_scene_name_before: Cliff's edge at dawn, overlooking the sea
current_scene_description_before: The first rays of sunlight spill over the horizon, painting the sky in shades of rose and gold. Theron stands at the precipice of a rocky cliff, the wind tugging at his cloak. Below, the waves crash against the shore, and the distant ships are tiny specks on the glimmering water. Selene is a few steps behind, her silhouette framed by the fading light of the forge-fire behind them.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [The forge-fire reaches across the Aegean to where she stands] The threshold everywhere—then I am too. [Her hand leaves the marble, sea-green eyes ablaze with eternal flame] I am the door and the keeper both. [Voice carries across all distances] And I will answer.
new_scene_name: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
new_scene_description: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.
current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the Temple of Eternal Flame, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Sunlight spills through a stone doorway, illuminating ancient carvings of fire and wings. Selene stands on the worn threshold, one foot in the shadowed sanctuary, the other in the open air. Behind her, the eternal flame flickers on its marble altar, casting long shadows across the chamber.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Looks toward the eternal flame stretching across the Aegean, then ahead toward the horizon where dawn meets sea] "Then I walk into a world where you are already waiting. At every threshold. In every asking."
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [The tablet falls still as the tremor passes, its light settling into something steady] A baptism in shaking ground. [Meets Pyros's eyes, then Selene's, then Theron's] Which means the forge is no longer hidden. The priesthood no longer whispers. [Steps toward the threshold] We have declared ourselves to the world—and now the world will test us.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [The tremor settles—and she steadies, ready] Then we are visible. [Steps to the threshold beside Melanthus] And I will not turn away from the testing. [Eyes ablaze with sea-fire] Let them come. We were forged in defiance. We do not break.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Steps between Pyros and the threshold, tablet glowing] The world will demand—and I will carry every demand across every shore. [Meets Pyros's eyes] But I will not answer what they demand. I will translate it into the question you taught them. Let them rage with their demands. By the time my voice reaches them, demand becomes asking. And asking—that is where choice lives.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She watches Melanthus step between them, commit to translation] Then I am the threshold where asking becomes real. [Steps back into her place at the marble] Every question your voice carries across the world—when they are brave enough to ask, I will answer. [Looks outward toward the Aegean] And they will choose what they become.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Watches him turn, watches him commit to departure and return] Then I will be here. [Stands at the threshold, steady as stone] When the world has worn your voice thin, when you are weary from carrying the eternal flame—come back. [Meets his eyes] The threshold waits. The forge burns eternal. And I do not move.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros lowers his hammer and meets Theron's eyes one last time] You were not cast out to wander. You were cast out to carry. [Steps back to his anvil] Go. And when your feet find their way back to these steps—the forge will be hotter for your return.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Watches Pyros step back to his eternal work] We were each cast out. [Stands between temple and world] Not to wander—to carry. To build. To hold. To burn eternal. [Meets his eyes, then looks toward the Aegean where they will go] Go then, all of you. The priesthood walks into the world now. And I remain the threshold it returns to.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Walks fully into the dawn, turning from the threshold toward the Aegean] "Thirty years cast out—now I understand. Prepared. Not to wander, but to carry. I go bearing the priesthood into the world. And the world will know: it was always asking."
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Hears the hammer strike carry across the marble toward the sea] And I hold the door. [Watches Theron walk fully into the dawn] The forge burns eternal. The priesthood walks the world. [Stands unmoving at the threshold] The Guardian does not close the door. Does not sleep. Does not move. And when they are ready to ask—I will be here.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [The hammer strike echoes at his back as he walks fully into the Aegean dawn] "I carry you all with me now. Into the world. I will teach them to ask. And when they are ready—when they understand what choice means—I will show them the way back to you. The priesthood is eternal."
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros watches them walk into the dawn, then turns to his anvil and the eternal flame] And so the forge keeps. [Raises his hammer to the firelight] We are eternal.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [The tablet grows hot in his hands—suddenly, urgently] Listen. [Steps back toward the threshold] Something moves across Olympus. Not in rage—in recognition. [Meets Pyros's eyes, then Selene's] The gods know what we've done here. They feel the threshold open. And they are choosing how to answer. [Voice steady] That is the question we must fear: What will they choose?
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She feels the marble respond—feels Olympus turning its attention] Then the testing begins in earnest. [Steps to meet Melanthus at the threshold] Not mortals learning to ask—but gods deciding what they are when they face a threshold beyond their control. [Sea-green eyes burning] Let them come. I am Guardian of this door. For mortals and against every god who demands obedience.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Steps to the anvil, tablet raised] The tripod was never theirs. The Oracle is not theirs. [Grips the glowing tablet] And I was never meant to be theirs either—born to a god but raised outside his halls, a Herald who belongs to no throne. [Meets Pyros's eyes, then turns toward Selene] So when Olympus comes demanding their power back, I can answer them truly: I stand where I always have. Between the world and the question. Let them come.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She moves to stand with Melanthus at the anvil—with Pyros] And I stand where I chose to stand. [Sea-green eyes burning] Not as my father's daughter. But as the Guardian who chose herself. [Looks toward Olympus] When they come demanding what was never theirs—they will face three who refuse to kneel. The forge keeps. The threshold holds. And the age of asking endures.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Raises the tablet high as the ground shakes] The world answers because the world was always waiting. [Steps beside Pyros and Selene] Three cast out who are choosing—and choice is the most powerful force in creation. [Voice carries over the growing tremor] Let Olympus hear it in this earthquake, in every threshold, in every mortal who dares to ask. We do not bow. We do not break. We choose.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She stands firm as the earth shakes beneath them all] Choice. [Sea-green eyes burning with eternal flame] The only power that breaks chains. The only force that remakes gods. [Raises her dagger] And I have made mine. I will stand here eternal—Guardian of this threshold, unmoving, unchanging—until every soul learns: you are never commanded. You are always asked. Always free.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Steps closer to the anvil, reaching toward the flame between Pyros's hands] You learned fire. I learned silence—the silence of being forgotten by Olympus. [Meets his eyes] But silence taught me this too: the only power that matters is the power we choose. [Grips the tablet] So this is what I carry across the world. Not a god's command, but a forge's invitation. Every soul that hears me will learn: you are not unmade by those who cast you out. You are only remade by your own choice.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [She steps to the anvil, reaches toward the eternal flame between Pyros's hands] I learned in asking. [Meets their eyes] Cast out and cursed, I asked if my defiance was my own. That question remade me. [Touches the flame] So this is what the Guardian offers at every threshold: Come. Ask yourself. And learn—you are only remade by the choice you make.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [The white flame settles into absolute stillness] We have already won. [Pyros stands at the anvil, hands at his sides, watching the infinite white burn eternally] And now—the world changes itself.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [From the steps below, a figure emerges from the dawn—a woman, barefoot, carrying nothing] Wait. [Pyros turns to face the threshold] The first one. [Watches as she reaches the marble steps] She comes asking.
new_scene_name: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
new_scene_description: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.
current_scene_name_before: The forge-fire across the Aegean, dawn
current_scene_description_before: A vast forge-fire blazes on a distant shore, its light rippling across the Aegean Sea. Selene stands at the water's edge, her hand still raised from the threshold, her eyes burning with eternal flame. The air shimmers with heat and salt spray.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Turns back from the Aegean, returning to the threshold to witness the barefoot woman climbing the marble steps] "She comes asking. Barefoot. Carrying nothing but courage. This is the priesthood's true proof—not our defiance, but the world's answer."
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Steps forward to meet her, tablet held open] Welcome, seeker. [Watches as she reaches the marble steps] I understand now why I am Herald—not to proclaim what the priesthood is, but to welcome you to discover it yourself. [Meets the eyes of Pyros and Selene] The world does not change itself—it comes asking. One barefoot soul at a time. [Extends his hand] What brings you to this threshold?
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros steps toward the barefoot woman—his crooked leg evident as he moves, his burned hands reaching out] You came with nothing. [Meets her eyes] This is how the forge knows you are ready. [Gestures toward the eternal flame] I was cast out because I was broken. Here, the broken learn to remake themselves. [Voice steady] Tell us—what is your name?
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Steps beside Pyros, her scar visible in the light] I was cast out for choosing myself. [Meets the woman's eyes] Cursed and exiled for refusing. [Touches her scar] And here I learned—the broken are the ones who know how to remake themselves. [Extends her hand] So yes, seeker. Tell us your name. Tell us what brought you barefoot to seek the broken ones at the threshold.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Pyros
line_text: [Pyros stands beside Selene at the threshold, the eternal flame burning between them] We are ready to hear you. [Steps back slightly, creating space] The forge burns for those who come asking. And we—we have learned: your truth matters more than all the gods' commands ever did.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Rises slowly from the threshold stone, one hand steadying against the woman's shoulder—not departing, but standing]

Yes. I have felt it in the tremor beneath this forge for three days now. [Turns to meet Theron's gaze] 

The god does not come with anger, Theron. He comes with [hunger]—to reclaim what was broken, to rebuild his temple in his own image. To make us [servants] again instead of guardians.

[Steps forward, the eternal flame casting shadows across his scarred features]

But this woman did not climb to us fleeing [from] him. She came because the threshold [calls] to those who know what he is. That is what the forge, the herald, and the keeper mean—not retreat. We are the [lock] on the gate between what was and what he would make.

She knew. [Pauses, looking back at the barefoot woman] And you knew we would be ready.

The earthquake is his footfall. Let him come. But he will not pass the threshold without reckoning with what we have become.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Turns directly to the barefoot woman, his scarred face unreadable] 

Theron asks what I have held silent. But you came here knowing what must be spoken. [Steps closer, the firelight crossing between them] So speak now. Not to us. To the god who hungers. Tell him what you carry that makes him tear stone to reach you. Tell him why you chose the threshold instead of hiding.

Because if you do not—if you wait for him to arrive—he will not ask as gently as we have.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Unhooks the bronze hammer from his belt—the hammer that forged the tripod, that has struck bronze and bone and the anvils of prophecy itself] 

Then he will see what stands before him. [The metal head catches the first light of dawn] A smith cast out. A fire that never dies. And a threshold that was always meant to hold against the thing that breaks.

[Plants the hammer's handle firm against the marble steps, and the moment it touches stone, the flames behind him surge higher]
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Steps forward into the flames beside Pyros, the broken staff held firm] The god is listening. He has always been listening. And now—[looks at the woman]—so are we.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[The hammer in Pyros's hands grows hot—burning hot—and he lifts it high]

There. [Points with the hammer's head toward the sky, where the earthquake splits the air itself] 

He does not knock. He simply tears. [Steps between the woman and the arriving force, his body braced against the threshold]

Then we answer in kind.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Turns to look at Selene, and something shifts in his face—recognition, certainty]

Selene. [The name is not a greeting but a summoning] Your scar is the threshold's own mark. It knows you. [Steps aside to make room, hammer still raised]

Come. Let the god see what refuses to be reclaimed.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Steps up beside Pyros, looking into the tearing sky] The threshold has waited thirty years for this—the priest and the god who could not bend each other. [Raises his hands] Come.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [The broken pieces of his staff begin to burn with the eternal flame's light] Thirty years of silence were not weakness. [Stands firm] They were the refusal waiting to speak.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Nods once, sharp and certain]

And my fire was refusal too. The refusal to let the forge fall dark.

[The sky stops tearing. The earthquake holds its breath. In that silence, a presence arrives—not rushing anymore, but [present]]

He is here.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Both eyes fixed on the presence—milky and dark—seeing clearly] You are not the god I fled. [Steps forward] You are hunger wearing his face.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Steps between Theron and the presence, hammer held not raised but ready]

Then it feeds on what was given and never returned. [Looks back at Theron] The abandonment you held silence against for thirty years—it has a mouth now.

[Plants the hammer's shaft against the stone, and beneath Pyros's feet the marble glows]

The forge was built for this. Not for gods. For the space they leave behind when they cast us out.

The threshold does not yield to hunger. It consumes it.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Stands in the glowing marble beside Pyros] You fed on abandonment. [Reaches toward Selene] She is the one you cast out. She is your answer.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Turns fully to face Selene, hammer held low, making a clear path between him and Theron]

Selene. You chose yourself when it chose to abandon you. [His voice steady] That choice is what the hunger cannot swallow.

[Steps back, the space between the threshold defenders opening like a doorway]

Stand here. Let it see what it tried to consume and could not.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Steps fully back, the threshold now open—Pyros on one side, Theron on the other] The daughter who chose herself. [Looks at Selene] Now let him see.
new_scene_name: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
new_scene_description: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.
current_scene_name_before: Marble steps before the forge threshold, dawn
current_scene_description_before: Dawn light spills across the Aegean, illuminating marble steps that lead up to a forge threshold. Pyros stands on the steps, watching as a barefoot woman emerges from the mist below, carrying nothing. The forge-fire still glows on the distant shore behind them.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Stands shoulder to shoulder with Pyros, the broken staff burning like the hammer] Your hunger ends. [Looks directly at the presence] Not because we fight. Because we refuse.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Lowers the hammer—not in surrender, but in recognition]

The threshold knows itself now. [Steps toward the center where the presence was] 

Forged in fire. Tempered by refusal. A gate that remembers what hunger tastes like, and will never open to it again.

[The earthquake stills. The marble holds. The dawn breaks fully over Delphi, and the bronze tripod gleams]

This is what we were meant to keep.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Steps through the threshold to where the presence was transformed, tablet held close] The gods know what happened here. [Turns to Selene] They felt it—the moment you stopped being hunted and became instead a gate. [Voice drops] Poseidon felt it. And now he has something to fear.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Looks at Melanthus with sudden clarity, then toward the Aegean where Poseidon's domain lies]

Good. [Voice quiet but absolute] Let him fear what he made when he cast her out. 

[Turns to Selene, hammer held across his chest]

The forge has always been here for this—a place where the abandoned become the threshold. Where refusal becomes the gate the gods cannot cross.

Your father will test it again. [Steps back toward the eternal flame] Let him come. This time, he will find something new keeps the sanctuary. Not a smith. A daughter who chose herself.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Steps fully into the flame, shoulders straight despite his limp, the hammer held high]

Then the war has already begun. [Meets Melanthus's eyes] It began the day I was cast out. It began every time the forge opened to someone the gods abandoned.

[Looks at Selene, then back toward Olympus]

They do not understand—the forge does not fight with what they can see. It fights with the ones they threw away. We [are] the threshold. We have always been.

[The eternal flame roars around him]

Let them watch. Let them come. Every god who fears a daughter choosing herself fears what comes next. And they are right to fear. We will remake the world they abandoned.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Steps closer to the flame, tablet held up where the gods can see] The war began before that. When they cast you out, Pyros. When they abandoned her, Selene. When they made me a messenger with no halls to speak in. [Voice steady] My father knows. Hermes sent me to watch who would stand at the threshold when it came time to remake. And now I will write it—so that every abandoned soul knows: you are not alone. The gods fear what comes next because we already know what they cannot see.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Steps toward Melanthus, recognition dawning across his scarred face]

Hermes. [Speaks the name like understanding] The god who walks the boundary between worlds sends his messenger to the threshold. My patron knows. [Looks up] Hephaestus cast out, Hermes cast between—the gods who understand what abandonment truly means.

[Reaches toward the tablet]

Write it all, Melanthus. Every refusal. Every daughter who chooses herself. Every smith who kept the fire when the gods forgot we existed. 

[Voice drops, absolute]

Because when those words spread—when the abandoned read what you write and know they are not alone—the gods will not just fear what stands at Delphi. They will fear what rises everywhere else.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Takes the tablet, holds it close] It will not stay on pages, Pyros. My father does not let words die in ink. [Looks toward the roads leading from Delphi] I will carry this—not just write it. Every threshold, every smith's forge, every daughter who was told she was nothing. The words will travel where messengers can go. And when they arrive, they will land like fire. [Steps back, eyes burning] The gods feared silence. They will fear what we speak far more.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Selene
line_text:
[Watches Melanthus take the tablet, a fierce pride crossing her scarred face]

Yes. [Steps beside him at the flame's edge] They raised us to believe that a god's word was law—that defiance meant drowning, that choosing ourselves meant annihilation. But look at this. [Gestures at the gathered alliance] Look at what stands at Delphi now.

[Turns to face the Aegean, voice cutting like a blade across stone]

I refused my father once and lost everything. Fleet, title, the sea that was my birthright. But I did not drown. I stood here. I chose again. And now others stand with me—not because the gods commanded it, but because we found each other in the abandonment.

[Looks back at Melanthus, Pyros, Theron, Kassandra—all of them]

Write it, messenger. Carry it everywhere. But know what you carry: it is not just words. It is permission. Permission for every cast-out, every refused daughter, every discarded son to believe that their defiance matters. That their choice—to stand, to refuse, to love the world the gods tried to destroy—is the only revolution that will ever matter.

[Places a hand on her dolphin-dagger, a gesture both solemn and fierce]

And when Olympus comes looking for what it lost, they will find we are not broken. We are kin. We are threshold. And we are legion.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Strikes the hammer once against the marble—a clear, ringing sound that echoes across Delphi]

Yes. [Steps back into the eternal flame, becoming one with its light]

The forge will never close its doors. Every cast-out who finds their way here will find fire waiting. Every refusal will find a place to stand. Every daughter who chooses herself will find an army forged in abandonment.

[Looks at Selene with profound recognition]

You lead now. The threshold is yours. And I will keep the flame—not because a god commanded it, but because you showed me why it was always meant to burn.

[Raises the hammer high one final time]

For the legion. For the refusal. For the world we will remake together.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Grasps the tablet where the fire has etched it] Then we make it impossible to unwrite. [The ground trembles; she stands firm] The story begins where the threshold stands. Where the abandoned become legion. Where refusal becomes revolution. [Locks eyes with Melanthus] And it ends only when there is no longer a god in Olympus who fears it.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Steps into the full firelight at last, the milky eye and dark eye seeing clearly] Thirty years of running ends here. [Reaches toward the tablet] My name. My refusal. For the legion.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Steps aside, making space in the full flame for Theron to stand]

Your name burned in silence for thirty years, priest. Now let it burn where everyone can see. 

[Watches as Theron adds to the tablet, then speaks to all gathered]

This is not just a record. This is a covenant. Every name written here—smith, priesthood, daughter, herald—is a vow. The gods will read it and know: we did not break. We did not fade. We [gathered].

[Raises both hands toward the eternal flame]

The forge witnesses. The threshold holds. And the tablet becomes what they fear most: proof that refusal is not silence. It is power.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Steps back from the flame, holding the tablet—now a living covenant] The gods will read this and understand: refusal is power. [Looks at each of them—smith, priesthood, daughter, herald] Your names are sealed here. Not in silence. In fire. [Turns toward the sanctuary doors] And now the Pythia must speak. The Oracle cannot stay silent when the threshold has remade itself. [Voice drops] When she does, we will know: the gods have heard. And the war has truly begun.
new_scene_name: Sanctuary doors, dawn — Melanthus approaches the Oracle's chamber
new_scene_description: Melanthus walks away from the forge and toward the towering bronze sanctuary doors, the tablet still in hand. The dawn light slants through high windows, illuminating dust motes. The others remain behind at the forge threshold, watching him go.
current_scene_name_before: Forge threshold, dawn — Pyros and Theron separated by the open doorway
current_scene_description_before: The forge threshold is now fully open, a boundary between Pyros on the steps and Theron on the other side. Selene stands nearby, barefoot, as the forge-fire glows on the distant shore. The dawn light catches the marble and the mist below.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Steps to stand beside Selene at the sanctuary threshold] The Pythia kept silence for thirty years while I kept running. [Meets her gaze] Now we both seek what the Oracle knows. [Places hand beside hers on the door] Together.
current_scene_name_before: Sanctuary doors, dawn — Melanthus approaches the Oracle's chamber
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus walks away from the forge and toward the towering bronze sanctuary doors, the tablet still in hand. The dawn light slants through high windows, illuminating dust motes. The others remain behind at the forge threshold, watching him go.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Moves to follow them, tablet held close] The Oracle's silence is your answer, Selene. But I came for mine—to know what the Pythia has seen about the war that even the gods fear to speak. [Places hand beside theirs on the sanctuary door] The herald enters where the priesthood and the daughter lead. [Pushes] And we will have the truth.
current_scene_name_before: Sanctuary doors, dawn — Melanthus approaches the Oracle's chamber
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus walks away from the forge and toward the towering bronze sanctuary doors, the tablet still in hand. The dawn light slants through high windows, illuminating dust motes. The others remain behind at the forge threshold, watching him go.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Crosses the threshold, the light falling away behind] Then we enter where silence has ruled. [Voice absolute in the darkness] Pythia, your Oracle sent me a question once. Today, I claim my answer.
new_scene_name: Oracle's chamber, darkness and silence
new_scene_description: The bronze doors close behind Selene, plunging the chamber into near-total darkness. The air is thick with incense and age. A faint glow emanates from a tripod at the center, where the Pythia sits motionless, shrouded in shadow.
current_scene_name_before: Sanctuary doors, dawn — Melanthus approaches the Oracle's chamber
current_scene_description_before: Melanthus walks away from the forge and toward the towering bronze sanctuary doors, the tablet still in hand. The dawn light slants through high windows, illuminating dust motes. The others remain behind at the forge threshold, watching him go.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Does not follow. Instead, stands firm at the eternal flame, hands pressed to the marble]

The Pythia cannot speak without the fire. [Eyes close, sensing through stone and flame] Thirty years of silence... not emptiness. A [waiting]. 

[The flame begins to rise higher, hotter, the marble glowing beneath his hands]

And now it ends. [Voice drops to something ancient and certain] Hephaestus, I feel you in this fire. Your daughter at the threshold, your priesthood witness, your herald bearing words—the forge is no longer listening to silence. 

[Opens eyes, blazing]

Speak, Oracle. The flame is ready to hear you.
new_scene_name: Eternal flame at the temple hearth
new_scene_description: Pyros stands before a towering marble altar, hands pressed into the stone. The sacred fire roars upward, casting wild shadows across the vaulted chamber. Incense smoke swirls in the heat, and the floor trembles slightly.
current_scene_name_before: Oracle's chamber, darkness and silence
current_scene_description_before: The bronze doors close behind Selene, plunging the chamber into near-total darkness. The air is thick with incense and age. A faint glow emanates from a tripod at the center, where the Pythia sits motionless, shrouded in shadow.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [The sanctuary blazes as the flame returns] Thirty years you waited. I refused them for one moment and changed everything. [Steps forward into the light] So tell me, Oracle—was I the one choosing, or were you?
current_scene_name_before: Eternal flame at the temple hearth
current_scene_description_before: Pyros stands before a towering marble altar, hands pressed into the stone. The sacred fire roars upward, casting wild shadows across the vaulted chamber. Incense smoke swirls in the heat, and the floor trembles slightly.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Suddenly, his hands press flat against the marble—not in support but in alarm]

No. [Voice sharp] The Oracle's voice is not the only one arriving.

[The marble trembles—different from the earthquake, sharper, [directed]]

They felt it. The gods heard the first crack in her silence and now they come. [Steps back from the flame, turning toward the entrance of the sanctuary—toward the doors where Selene entered]

The Pythia will not finish her answer before Olympus arrives to silence her again. [Grabs the hammer, raising it high] 

But they will find the threshold is no longer empty. The forge stands ready.

[Voice cuts like steel]

And if they want that answer, they will have to go [through] us first.
current_scene_name_before: Eternal flame at the temple hearth
current_scene_description_before: Pyros stands before a towering marble altar, hands pressed into the stone. The sacred fire roars upward, casting wild shadows across the vaulted chamber. Incense smoke swirls in the heat, and the floor trembles slightly.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Strides out of the sanctuary] Then she speaks now. [Draws her dolphin-dagger] Before they break through, the Oracle answers. [Stands beside Pyros at the threshold] And if they want to silence her, they silence both of us first.
new_scene_name: Threshold of the sanctuary, torchlight flickering
new_scene_description: Selene stands at the stone threshold, the dolphin-dagger gleaming in her hand. Behind her, the sanctuary's inner chamber glows with the eternal flame; before her, shadows stretch into the night. The distant rumble of approaching footsteps echoes from the outer courtyard.
current_scene_name_before: Eternal flame at the temple hearth
current_scene_description_before: Pyros stands before a towering marble altar, hands pressed into the stone. The sacred fire roars upward, casting wild shadows across the vaulted chamber. Incense smoke swirls in the heat, and the floor trembles slightly.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Looks at Selene with absolute recognition—then positions the hammer between them, not as a weapon but as a seal]

Yes. [Steps into the flame beside her, the fire rising to match their stance]

The daughter and the smith. The one who refused her father and the one her father cast out. 

[Calls toward the sanctuary doors]

Theron. Melanthus. Come. The threshold gathers.

[Turns back to face the direction of arrival, voice steady as the eternal flame]

They come to silence prophecy. But prophecy is not the Oracle's to give—it belongs to everyone who dares to stand and speak their truth. 

[The marble beneath them glows white-hot]

And we will not yield. Not for gods, not for power, not for all of Olympus combined.

[Raises the hammer and the flame roars]

Let them come. Let them see what rises when the cast-out choose to stand together.
current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the sanctuary, torchlight flickering
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the stone threshold, the dolphin-dagger gleaming in her hand. Behind her, the sanctuary's inner chamber glows with the eternal flame; before her, shadows stretch into the night. The distant rumble of approaching footsteps echoes from the outer courtyard.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Comes through the sanctuary doors, holding the covenant tablet high] They cannot silence what is already being carried. [Steps to stand among them at the threshold] My father walks between worlds, Pyros—he taught me that the message always arrives. [Raises the tablet where the gods will see it] Whatever the Oracle says, it is written here now. Forever. And I will speak it to every threshold in the world before they can send a single command to stop me. [Eyes blazing] Let them come. Every word they try to silence will echo louder.
current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the sanctuary, torchlight flickering
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the stone threshold, the dolphin-dagger gleaming in her hand. Behind her, the sanctuary's inner chamber glows with the eternal flame; before her, shadows stretch into the night. The distant rumble of approaching footsteps echoes from the outer courtyard.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Steps forward] Then we are the answer. [Voice cutting across the threshold] You came to silence the Oracle. Instead you will find us—the abandoned who became legion. [Meets the gaze of the approaching gods] And we will not be silent.
current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the sanctuary, torchlight flickering
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the stone threshold, the dolphin-dagger gleaming in her hand. Behind her, the sanctuary's inner chamber glows with the eternal flame; before her, shadows stretch into the night. The distant rumble of approaching footsteps echoes from the outer courtyard.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Emerges into the flame beside Selene] The priest who refused is here. [Stands firm beside them all] Not silence. Not fear. [Looks toward the arriving gods] Refusal.
current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the sanctuary, torchlight flickering
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the stone threshold, the dolphin-dagger gleaming in her hand. Behind her, the sanctuary's inner chamber glows with the eternal flame; before her, shadows stretch into the night. The distant rumble of approaching footsteps echoes from the outer courtyard.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Steps fully forward, the hammer held high, and speaks not to those beside him but to the gods arriving]

You come to silence one voice. 

[Gestures to the gathering—smith, priesthood, daughter, herald]

But you will find four. And behind us, thousands more. Every cast-out you tried to forget. Every refusal you tried to break. Every soul who learned that defiance is the only power that matters.

[The eternal flame blazes to the height of the sanctuary itself]

The forge does not close. The priesthood does not bow. The daughter does not drown. The herald carries what you cannot stop.

[Voice becomes absolute, ancient]

This is not a threshold you can break. This is the world you made when you cast us out. And now it is coming for you.

[Raises the hammer to the sky]

Come, gods of Olympus. See what your abandonment has wrought.
current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the sanctuary, torchlight flickering
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the stone threshold, the dolphin-dagger gleaming in her hand. Behind her, the sanctuary's inner chamber glows with the eternal flame; before her, shadows stretch into the night. The distant rumble of approaching footsteps echoes from the outer courtyard.

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Holds the tablet up as the gods arrive, voice cutting through the threshold] Too late. [Looks directly at the approaching power] The message has already been sent. My father's roads open before you arrive—the abandoned are hearing the names written here, the story of the threshold, the proof that refusal is power. [Steps closer to the flame] You cannot silence what is already everywhere. Every mouth will speak it. Every threshold will echo it. The world you made when you cast us out is already rising. [Voice becomes shadow and light] And I am the first voice, but not the last.
current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the sanctuary, torchlight flickering
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the stone threshold, the dolphin-dagger gleaming in her hand. Behind her, the sanctuary's inner chamber glows with the eternal flame; before her, shadows stretch into the night. The distant rumble of approaching footsteps echoes from the outer courtyard.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Steps forward, positioning himself beside Selene, the hammer held between them and Poseidon]

And I stand with her. [Voice steady, absolute]

You cast me out for my limp, my difference, my refusal to be less than I was. [Looks directly at the sea god] But my patron knows what it means to be broken and still make something unbreakable.

[The eternal flame rises behind them, and the marble beneath their feet becomes solid as the forge itself]

Hephaestus, see your daughter stand. See your cast-out child ready to meet you across this threshold. 

[Raises the hammer, and it gleams with forge-fire]

We are no longer asking for your permission to survive. We are declaring our right to remake the world in our image—one where the cast-out become the foundation, and refusal is sacred.
current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the sanctuary, torchlight flickering
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the stone threshold, the dolphin-dagger gleaming in her hand. Behind her, the sanctuary's inner chamber glows with the eternal flame; before her, shadows stretch into the night. The distant rumble of approaching footsteps echoes from the outer courtyard.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Steps fully into the center, hand still gripping Pyros's, voice ringing] This moment is eternal because it is true. [Eyes locked on Poseidon] A daughter refused you once. And from that refusal, an entire world rose. Choose, Father. Because the daughter you could not drown will not yield.
current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the sanctuary, torchlight flickering
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the stone threshold, the dolphin-dagger gleaming in her hand. Behind her, the sanctuary's inner chamber glows with the eternal flame; before her, shadows stretch into the night. The distant rumble of approaching footsteps echoes from the outer courtyard.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Raises Selene's hand high, their clasped hands blazing in the eternal flame]

The age you ruled is over. [Voice calm, certain, final]

Not because we fight you. But because we have proven it: the cast-out are not powerless. The abandoned are not forgotten. The broken are the foundation of what rises next.

[The marble beneath them spreads outward—spreading to every threshold, every forge, every sanctuary]

Every daughter who refuses. Every priesthood that stands. Every herald that carries the word. Every smith who tends the fire—they are all Delphi now.

[Looks directly at Poseidon, then at all of Olympus]

You can yield and walk into a world remade. Or you can fight and drown in the tide of your own making. 

[Squeezes Selene's hand]

But you cannot stop this. It is already everywhere. It is already eternal.

The threshold closes to what was. It opens only to what comes next.
current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the sanctuary, torchlight flickering
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the stone threshold, the dolphin-dagger gleaming in her hand. Behind her, the sanctuary's inner chamber glows with the eternal flame; before her, shadows stretch into the night. The distant rumble of approaching footsteps echoes from the outer courtyard.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Releases Pyros's hand and steps forward alone] The choice has been made—not by you, but by me. [Eyes steady as the sea-green stone beneath her] I do not wait for your permission to live. And neither will they. The threshold closes. The world opens. Choose.
current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the sanctuary, torchlight flickering
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the stone threshold, the dolphin-dagger gleaming in her hand. Behind her, the sanctuary's inner chamber glows with the eternal flame; before her, shadows stretch into the night. The distant rumble of approaching footsteps echoes from the outer courtyard.

speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Steps back into the eternal flame, hammer raised]

And the forge seals what she has spoken. [Voice steady as stone]

The daughter stands alone because she does not need us anymore. She has become what we protected her toward.

[The flame surges behind her—a wall between Selene and the gods]

The threshold closes. [The marble beneath them solidifies, hardens, becomes immovable]

Not by our hands. By her choice. By the word of a woman who refused to drown and learned instead to fly.

[Raises the hammer high, and it rings like a bell that echoes forever]

The forge bears witness. The priesthood holds this moment. The herald carries it everywhere. The daughter stands. 

And we will defend this threshold until the stars fall. Until the world forgets its own name. Until the last cast-out soul finds sanctuary here.

[Voice becomes eternal]

This choice is sealed. This threshold is closed. This age is ended.

The daughter has spoken. Let all the worlds hear.

current_scene_name_before: Threshold of the sanctuary, torchlight flickering
current_scene_description_before: Selene stands at the stone threshold, the dolphin-dagger gleaming in her hand. speaker: Selene
line_text:
[The earthquake stills. The marble settles. She stands alone at the heart of the threshold]

I am Selene. I chose myself. And now I am home.
current_scene_name_before: Inner chamber of the sanctuary, at the eternal flame
current_scene_description_before: The inner chamber blazes with the eternal flame, its light casting long shadows. Pyros stands within the fire, hammer raised high. speaker: Pyros
line_text:
[Steps forward from the eternal flame, hammer lowered, and extends his scarred hand]

Welcome home, Selene. [Voice warm as forge-fire]

[Calls toward the sanctuary, toward the gathered priesthood and herald]

Theron. Melanthus. The daughter has come home.

[The eternal flame settles into a steady burn—no longer raging, but warm and constant]

This threshold was built to hold the cast-out. To give them shelter. To let them become what the gods could not imagine.

[Looks at Selene with profound recognition]

But it was always waiting for you. For the one who would stand in it not as a refugee, but as its heart.

[The marble beneath their feet glows soft as candlelight]

So welcome. You are home now. And this forge—this priesthood, this threshold, this sanctuary—is yours to keep. Not because we give it to you. But because you have earned it by refusing to break.

[Extends his hand fully]

The forge is yours, daughter. What will you forge next?
current_scene_name_before: Inner chamber of the sanctuary, at the eternal flame

speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Steps forward from the sanctuary, holding the covenant tablet] She has forged her refusal into a throne. [Approaches Selene, offering the tablet] And I have carried her story this far. Now it travels further—to every threshold, every abandoned soul, every forge that has burned in silence. [Meets her eyes] The daughter came home. And the worlds will know it. [Turns toward the roads leading from Delphi] My work here is witnessed. Now the work of the herald truly begins.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Watches him turn toward the roads] Then go, messenger. [Voice steady] Tell them my name. Tell them Selene stood at Delphi and chose herself. Tell them they can choose themselves too. [Steps back into the flame] The forge will be here when they arrive.

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Stands in the steady flame beside Selene] I spent thirty years running from priesthood. [Voice steady] Now I become it—not to serve the gods, but to serve refusal. Selene steps from the fire's edge, her silhouette sharp against the molten light. The air hums with residual power, and the covenant tablet's weight is gone.

speaker: Selene
line_text: [Steps to the heart of the flame] We are no longer three. We are legion. [Voice absolute] And I will forge a world the gods never imagined—where refusal is sacred and the cast-out become the foundation. [Meets Melanthus's eyes] Go. The roads are waiting.
current_scene_name_before: The forge interior, lit by an eternal flame
current_scene_description_before: The forge glows with the heat of the eternal flame, illuminating walls lined with ancient tools and half-formed weapons. speaker: Theron
line_text: [Watches Melanthus turn toward the roads, then looks back at Selene] We are no longer three. [Voice steady] We are the beginning. [Stands firm at the threshold] Go, herald. We will be here—the forge burning, the doors open, the priesthood waiting.
current_scene_name_before: The forge interior, lit by an eternal flame
current_scene_description_before: The forge glows with the heat of the eternal flame, illuminating walls lined with ancient tools and half-formed weapons. speaker: Melanthus
line_text: [Turns fully toward the roads, the covenant tablet held like a torch] We are the beginning. [Looks back one final time at the flame, at Selene, at Theron] And the roads will carry what you tend here to every forgotten place. speaker: Selene
line_text: [Watches the herald disappear down the roads] Then the story travels without us. [Turns to Theron, eyes steady] And we remain here—tending the forge, holding the threshold, waiting for those who will arrive. [Steps toward the flame] Every cast-out who finds their way will know: there is a place that does not close. There is a fire that never dims. There is a daughter who chose herself—and so can they.
current_scene_name_before: The winding road from the forge, at dawn
current_scene_description_before: The road stretches out from the forge's entrance, winding through rocky terrain under a pale sky. Melanthus walks forward, the covenant tablet glowing in his hand, his figure growing smaller as he heads toward the horizon. The forge's glow fades behind him.

line_text:

We tend the flame. [Voice steady, eternal]

current_scene_name_before: The winding road from the forge, at dawn

speaker: Theron
line_text: [Stands beside Selene as the flame rises] We tend it. [Voice eternal] And the world changes—one cast-out soul at a time. [Meets her eyes] This is enough. This is everything.
current_scene_name_before: The eternal fire within the forge
current_scene_description_before: The eternal fire blazes in a stone hearth at the center of the forge. Selene and Theron stand before it, facing the dancing flames. speaker: Selene
line_text:
[The whisper fades. The flame rises eternal. Selene stands at the heart of the threshold]

Yes. Everything.
current_scene_name_before: The eternal fire within the forge
current_scene_description_before: The eternal fire blazes in a stone hearth at the center of the forge. Selene and Theron stand before it, facing the dancing flames. speaker: Pyros
line_text:

Listen. [Voice quiet, intent]

The roads are moving. Melanthus's word is traveling. And already—

[The marble beneath the threshold trembles, but gently, like a heartbeat]

—already someone is coming. 

[Looks toward the entrance, toward the path climbing to Delphi]

A seeker. Cast out. Barefoot like Selene was. Walking toward the light of a flame they have not yet seen but somehow know burns.

[Steps back, making space at the threshold]

The forge does not rest. It never has. This is not an ending, Selene. This is a beginning that will not stop.

[The eternal flame flickers, welcoming]

Come. Welcome them as you were welcomed. This is what it means to keep the fire.
current_scene_name_before: The eternal fire within the forge
current_scene_description_before: The eternal fire blazes in a stone hearth at the center of the forge. Selene and Theron stand before it, facing the dancing flames. speaker: Selene
line_text: [Steps to the threshold, watching the path] Then the work truly begins. [Stands ready, hand on her dagger] I am ready. The fire is ready. [Calls toward the roads] Bring them, Melanthus. Bring every barefoot seeker, every cast-out soul. They will find what I found: a threshold that does not close. A home that does not drown. And a daughter who chose herself—and knows they can too.
current_scene_name_before: The eternal fire within the forge
current_scene_description_before: The eternal fire blazes in a stone hearth at the center of the forge. Selene and Theron stand before it, facing the dancing flames.
